TicketTypeDescription.Description: Online only ticket for travel on Heathrow Express services

Validity.TimeOutward: Anytime

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
• Anytime Web Single tickets can only be purchased online or through the mobile app;
  • Anytime Web Single tickets are available as eTickets or mobile tickets;
  • Anytime Web Single tickets are valid for travel anytime of the day and can be bought on the day of  travel;
  • Anytime Web Single tickets are valid for one person, one way on the date it is booked to travel for;
  • Anytime Web Single tickets are refundable
  • Anytime Web Single tickets cannot be used in conjunction with any other promotion, offer, discount or any other train company special offers;
  • Anytime Web Single tickets can be used in conjunction with Railcards;
  • Anytime Web Single tickets are available only in Express Class;
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Online at Heathrow Express website, or via Heathrow Express app only
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Tickets can be refunded
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Tickets can be refunded

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
The 2PA product will be used to support our promotional activity. For 2025, we are launching an Autumn Sale that will utilise the 2PA Advance fare.

As part of this promotion, the 2PA product will be made available for sale through all retail channels. Being an Advance fare, tickets must be purchased in advance of the travel date—they will not be available for purchase on the day of travel.

Railcard discounts will not be applicable to this promotional fare however child discounts will still be allow.

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.

Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Customers may not start, break and resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Not applicable. Advance are single tickets only.

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: 1 Day in Advance
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Advance tickets are non-refundable.

* * * * * * * * * * * *

Change of Journey/Excess Fare paid

Where an excess fare has been paid up to the price of a walk-up ticket, the excess fare paid varies the terms under which the passenger can travel, but it does not alter the refund terms of the original Advance ticket.

If the passenger subsequently asks for a refund on the combination of Advance ticket, Excess Fare paid and any administration fees paid, the Advance ticket and change of journey fee remain non-refundable and a refund is only payable on the difference in fare paid. This may be subject to a refund fee of no more than £5.

If your journey is cancelled or disrupted: If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed by more than 60 minutes, special arrangements will be made to accommodate them on another train (although a seat cannot be guaranteed). If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed, and as a result you decide not to travel, a refund will be offered on completely unused tickets and you will not be charged an administration fee.

There may be rare occasions where a cheaper Advance fare becomes available closer to the time of travel, for example if a group booking is cancelled and extra seats become available. Please note that in these circumstances Advance tickets that have already been bought cannot be refunded in part or in full.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Advance purchase ticket for families

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the date and time shown on the ticket (per Advance ticket condition).

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: All channels
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be bought the day before travel. Minimum 1 adult, 1 child / Maximum 2 adults, 4 children
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: • Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. Customers will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when they request a change.Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased.The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same.The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable, plus a £10 administration fee per single ticket for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded.Please note that where an Advance ticket has been purchased online and the ticket has either been self-printed at home or downloaded to a mobile device this may alter the rules for changing the ticket. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Family tickets are non-refundable

BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of journey is permitted on outward and return legs.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
The Bittern Ranger offers one day's unlimited travel on Greater Anglia's Bittern line, which runs through the northern part of the Norfolk Broads between Norwich, Hoveton & Wroxham and North Walsham to the coastal towns of Cromer and Sheringham.

The Bittern Ranger is also valid, on payment of a small supplement, on the Coasthopper buses nos. CH4 and CH5 between Mundesley, Cromer, Sheringham and Wells-next-the-Sea and the Coastliner bus no. 36 between Wells and Hunstanton, but not on any other sections of these routes.

Tickets are valid from 08.15 on Mondays to Fridays and at any time on Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

BV1 - One Day Ranger (Adult/Child)

BVF - One Day Ranger (Family - no Railcard required)

BSR - One Day Ranger (Senior - no Railcard required)

Validity.DayOutward: Available any day. Valid on date on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Available any day. Valid on date on the ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid after 08:15 Monday-Friday and all day Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid after 08:15 Monday-Friday and all day Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Station ticket offices, some travel agents, Greater Anglia (and selected other train company) telesales, on board the Norwich to Cromer/Sheringham train if boarding at an unstaffed station.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No reservations are needed.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are available if the ticket is surrendered before the date on the ticket. An administration fee may be deducted.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Unaccompanied children pay half-fare.

Accompanied children are charged £2.00.

RailCard.Note:
All national Railcards (except the Jobcentre Plus Card) give their usual discount.

GA Club 50 Railcards give 10% discount.

Annual Gold Cards give 1/3 discount.

Family and Senior versions are available without a Railcard being required.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: This ticket includes a travel ticket from various locations around the Great Western Railway network to Oxford, for both the outward and return journey by train, a Day Return ticket for the Woodstock Bus, and a ticket for admission to Blenheim Palace.

Validity.DayOutward: Outward rail travel must be made on the date on the ticket, and the ticket for admission to Blenheim Palace is only valid on the date shown on the ticket.

RailCard.Note: Senior Railcard discount applies

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Bus add-on ticket for rail Season Tickets
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No reservations are needed.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A Standard Class return ticket which can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.

Valid for a bus journey, often as part of a through journey from a rail origin to a bus destination.

Validity.DayOutward: The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: The return journey must be on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, or change the date of travel.

Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey.

NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative Anytime product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

A full refund is available if you return the completely unused ticket to the place of purchase within 28 days of expiry of the ticket. The amount of the refund will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive):  50% discount.

RailCard.Note:
Up to 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard.

Up to 50% discount for 16-17 Saver.

See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Business Anytime Plus fares include First class travel on East Midlands Railway services, Breakfast on selected services to London St Pancras and a complimentary snack on your return journey. Also included is a Zones 1 and 2 Underground return, 48-hour Car parking (except London St Pancras) and use of the First Class lounges at London St Pancras , Leicester, Derby and Nottingham.

Validity.DayOutward:
Conditions of Use.  You must travel in the class of accommodation shown on your ticket. Business Anytime Plus tickets provide access to First Class Lounges at stations. Railcard holders travelling on a discounted ticket must carry their Railcard when they travel. If a Railcard holder fails to produce their valid Railcard with their ticket, they will be required to pay a full priced ticket for their journey as if no Railcard and or no ticket were held. The outward part of the Business Anytime Plus ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket. When and where the ticket can be used. The outward portions of Anytime Returns are valid for five days including the date shown on the ticket. Return portions of Anytime Return tickets are valid for travel until 0429 in the early morning following the last day of validity (i.e. one calendar month from the outward journey). All travel must be completed by this time. Travelling via London. If the route shown on the ticket has a + the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool St and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Great Northern/Thameslink services appropriate to the route of the journey being made. 

Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR on the date shown on the ticket (or last day of validity for return portions of Super Off-Peak Returns) and until 0429 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is NOT permitted. Travel restrictions do not apply on public holidays. All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel. You can reserve a seat on many longer distance services. Reservations are recommended on busy services.There are no restrictions on when you can travel, however if you wish to change your date of outward travel or the origin andor destination of your ticket, you will need to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see below).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date. You may be required to pay an administration fee. The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) 50% discount. Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

Railcard discounts: Two Together, 16-25 Railcard: No discount, Disabled Persons Railcard: 1/3 off, Family & Friends Railcard: No discount, HM Forces Railcard: 1/3 off, Network Railcard : No discount and Senior Railcard: 1/3 off.

Other discount cards: Gold Cards : No discount and Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: No discount.

Note: Minimum fares  time restrictions may apply to tickets bought with a Railcard or other discount card. See terms and conditions of the appropriate Railcard or discount card for details.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) 50% discount. Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

Railcard discounts: Two Together, 16-25 Railcard: No discount, Disabled Persons Railcard: 1/3 off, Family & Friends Railcard: No discount, HM Forces Railcard: 1/3 off, Network Railcard: No discount and Senior Railcard: 1/3 off.

Other discount cards: Gold Cards : No discount and Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: No discount.

Note: Minimum fares  time restrictions may apply to tickets bought with a Railcard or other discount card. See terms and conditions of the appropriate Railcard or discount card for details.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Standard Class Return ticket to London with added benefits

Validity.DayOutward:
Conditions of UseYou must travel in the class of accommodation shown on your ticket. Railcard holders travelling on a discounted ticket must carry their Railcard when they travel. If a Railcard holder fails to produce their valid Railcard with their ticket, they will be required to pay a full priced ticket for their journey as if no Railcard andor no ticket were held.The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

When and where the ticket can be used

The outward portion of a Business Plus ticket is valid for travel on any train on the date shown on your ticket until 0429 on the following day, by which time all travel must be completed.

Travelling via London.If the route shown on the ticket has a + the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool St and Paddington)by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Great Northern/Thameslink services appropriate to the route of the journey being made. 

NOTE, Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR on the date shown on the ticket (or last day of validity for return portionsof Super Off-Peak Returns) and until 0429 the following day. - Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is NOT permitted.Travel restrictions do not apply on public holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Outward Tickets are valid ONLY on the date shown on the ticket(s). Where applicable, you must travel in the class and reserved seat(s) shown on the ticket(s).
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel. You can reserve a seat on many longer distance services. Reservations are recommended on busy services. There are no restrictions on when you can travel, however if you wish to change your date of outward travel or the origin andor destination of your ticket, you will need to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see below).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date. You may be required to pay an administration fee. The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

Child.Note: Child discounts, Senior, 16-25 and Disabled Persons Railcards discounts apply.

RailCard.Note: Child discounts, Senior, 16-25 and Disabled Persons Railcards discounts apply.

Group.Note: Child discounts, Senior, 16-25 and Disabled Persons Railcards discounts apply.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Standard Class Rail Rover for 1 day of train travel on the c2c Line, available to travellers aged 65 and over or Senior Railcard holders allowing off-peak travel between all c2c Stations.

Valid Mondays to Fridays only, excluding bank holidays. Travel time restrictions apply. Not valid on the services of other train operators or London Underground.

Validity.DayOutward: One Day Rover valid on date shown on ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: One Day Rover valid on date shown on ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: The c2c Senior Rover is valid for use after 09:30 and is not valid on any trains travelling within the London Travelcard area (Zones 1-6) between 16:00-18:59.
Validity.TimeReturn: The c2c Senior Rover is not valid on any trains travelling within the London Travelcard area (Zones 1-6) between 16:00-18:59.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available Monday to Friday (excluding Bank Holidays).
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: c2c Station ticket offices, c2c train travel app, c2c web
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: If the customer wishes to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of their ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on their existing ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds must be claimed within 28 days of the ticket's expiry date. The expiry date depends on the terms and conditions of the ticket you have purchased.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Caledonian Sleeper Business Tickets - Allows access to station lounges and books into rooms with en-suite showers.
TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Only valid on the train booked.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: These tickets are only available through selected business partners.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Available to purchase while reservations are open.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: If a change of journey is required the ticket should be refunded and a new ticket purchased.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refunds are available up to midday the day before travel. A £10 admin fee applies.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Classic accommodation are twin rooms with beds one above the other and an option to connect room door to the next room. Classic rooms are available for solo or twin occupancy. Breakfast is available at an additional charge. More information on Caledonian Sleeper is available at www.sleeper.scot.

Validity.DayOutward: Booked train only.
Validity.TimeOutward: Booked train only

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Only valid on the service reserved at the time of booking.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Subject to availability.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any selling location
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Must be able to reserve a room to purchase this product.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: There are no changes to these products. If a change is required the tickets should be refunded, within the refund conditions and a new ticket sold.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Refunds are available on a sliding scale depending on when the refund is requested.

  • 7 days or more before travel - a full refund is available with no admin fee
  • Less than 7 days and before midday the day before travel (2 days before for departures between 00:01 and 04:30) a 50% refund is available
  • After midday the day before departure (2 days before for departures between 00:01 and 04:30) refunds are no longer available

RailCard.Note:
16-25 Railcard - Discount on Solo products only

Senior Railcard - Discount on Solo products only

HM Forces/Veterans - Discount on Solo products only

Disables Railcard

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A book of Anytime Return tickets available as either 5 or 10 return journeys.

CAA - Carnet 5 (Book of 5x Anytime Returns)CAG - Carnet 10 (Book of 10x Anytime Returns)

Validity.DayOutward:
Carnet 5 are valid any day within 1 month of the date of the ticket.

Carnet 10 are valid any day within 3 months of the date on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn:
Carnet 5 are valid any day within 1 month of the date of the ticket.

Carnet 10 are valid any day within 3 months of the date on the ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid on any train.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid on any train.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: East Midlands Trains ticket offices or telesales office.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No reservations are needed.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: No changes.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: No refunds.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Gatwick Express Carnet tickets offer 10 single journeys for the price of 8.

Validity.DayOutward:
Tickets are valid for 12 months from the date of issue.

Carnets are issued as ten single tickets valid in either direction.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable. Carnets are issued as ten single tickets valid in either direction.
Validity.TimeOutward: There are no time-of-day restrictions.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Service is non-stop. Only valid between London Victoria and Gatwick Airport.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Valid for 12 months from the date of purchase.

Passengers must validate their Carnet ticket by writing the date of travel using permanent ink before boarding the train. Attempted fraudulent use will result in withdrawal of these tickets.

See also Carnet Train Ticket Gatwick Airport | Carnet to Gatwick | Gatwick Express
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Gatwick Express Carnet tickets are currently only available from the Gatwick Express ticket offices at Victoria Station and Gatwick Airport.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: No changes.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refunds are only available if all 10 tickets have not been used.

Child.Note: No discounts.

RailCard.Note: No discounts.

Group.Note: No discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Ten single tickets for the price of nine.  They are available to purchase between most Greater Anglia stations and London Liverpool Street, in either direction. Please note that Carnet tickets can only be used in the direction shown on the ticket, so you will need to buy a separate book of tickets to travel in the other direction.

They are priced at 10% below half of the Anytime Return fare and are valid for 3 months. Carnet tickets are sold in quantities of 10.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid for travel any day within 3 months of the date on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: n/a

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Tickets may be purchased through your Business Travel Management Company or from Greater Anglia's own Business Travel department by calling 0345 600 7245.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: No changes.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: No refunds.

Child.Note: No further discounts.

RailCard.Note: No further discounts.

Group.Note: No further discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A book of Standard Class Single tickets for 5 journeys

Validity.DayOutward: Carnet are valid any day within 3 months of the date on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: N/A
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid on any train.
Validity.TimeReturn: N/A

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: • Generally the same as 'Anytime tickets', except that tickets are valid for three months, but are not refundableRoute and/or Train Company validity is as shown on the ticket routeing.Can be bought at any time up to departure but cannot be bought on board trains.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Can only be purchased through LNER business channels
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Not compulsory but strongly recommended as trains can be very busy.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: No changes.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: No refunds.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Chiltern Railways offer Carnet tickets which offer passengers 12 open return journeys to London Marylebone for the price of 10 and these are Anytime tickets valid for 6 months from the date of purchase.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid for 6 months from the date of issue for outward and return travel.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid for 6 months from the date of issue for outward and return travel.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid at any time.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid at any time.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: The date of travel on the ticket must be completed on the ticket before boarding the train to make a journey.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Only available through Chiltern Railways Business Travel Department.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No reservations are needed.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Not refundable but can be used any time with 6 months from the date of issue.

Child.Note: No further discounts.

RailCard.Note: No further discounts.

Group.Note: No further discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Rail Rover for unlimited travel (subject to time restrictions shown) throughout central Scotland as defined below.

Available for 3 consecutive days of travel.

The Central Scotland Rover area of validity is:

  • on Glasgow - Alloa / Dunblane services
  • on Edinburgh - Alloa / Dunblane services
  • on the Glasgow - Edinburgh services via either Falkirk High or Shotts
  • West of Partick on the Helensburgh-Edinburgh and Argyle Line routes (i.e. not on stations between Helensburgh or Milngavie - Partick)
  • on the Cumbernauld / Falkirk Grahamston services
  • on the Edinburgh - North Berwick route
  • on the Fife Circle as far as Markinch

In addition, it is also valid on:

  • the SPT subway (Glasgow Underground trains)

Further information can be found on ScotRail's website.

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid for 3 consecutive days of travel.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid for 3 consecutive days of travel.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Valid from 09:15 Mondays to Fridays.

Valid any time Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Valid from 09:15 Mondays to Fridays.

Valid any time Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Rover tickets may not be issued more than 3 days in advance of their validity (5 days for telesales and internet bookings).
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: The Rover has unlimited travel but you cannot change the travel validity period of travel passes.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are only given on Rail Rover tickets returned before the start date shown on them and are subject to a £10 administration fee.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 50% Child discounts.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off for holders of 16-25, Senior and Disabled Persons Railcard.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A day ranger ticket, allowing unlimited travel in the ranger area which covers most of Cheshire and a little further afield.

View the map on Northern Rail's website.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Valid after 08:45 Monday to Friday.

Valid any time at weekends and on Bank Holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Valid after 08:45 Monday to Friday.

Valid any time at weekends and on Bank Holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any staffed railway station. Can also be purchased from the conductor on the train within the validity area when boarding at an unstaffed station or if the ticket office is closed.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refundable only if surrendered before the start date.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 50% discount.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note: Child discounts. Discounts also for holders of Adult Disabled Persons, Family & Friends, Senior and 16-25 Railcards. Discounts also for holders of Staff Travel Cards.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A Chiltern Duo Return offers two customers a 25% discount when travelling together on selected Chiltern Railways routes to London and returning the same day.

Tickets must be purchased by 23:59 the day before travel.

Validity.DayOutward: Chiltern Duo Return tickets are valid for outward travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.DayReturn: Chiltern Duo Return tickets are valid for return travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Not valid on trains timed to arrive at London Marylebone after 04:29 and before 11:30 Mondays to Fridays.

Time restrictions do not apply at weekends and on public holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Not valid on trains timed to depart London Marylebone after 04:29 and before 08:30, or after 16:00 and before 19:00 Mondays to Fridays.

Time restrictions do not apply at weekends and on public holidays.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is allowed with this ticket, as described in the National Rail Conditions of Travel, and subject to the time restrictions applicable to the ticket.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: A break of journey is allowed with this ticket, as described in the National Rail Conditions of Travel, and subject to the time restrictions applicable to the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket. Chiltern Duo Return tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations  by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR on the day of validity of the ticket. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Chiltern Duo tickets must be purchased by 23:59 the day before you wish to travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Chiltern Duo tickets are available to purchase from Ticket Offices, Ticket Vending Machines, online retailers, and mobile apps.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Chiltern Duo tickets must be purchased by 23:59 the day before you wish to travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Chiltern Duo tickets are sold as a package at one price for both passengers. No further discounts are available.

RailCard.Note: Chiltern Duo tickets are sold as a package at one price for both passengers. No further discounts are available.

Group.Note: Chiltern Duo tickets are sold as a package at one price for both passengers. No further discounts are available.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A Chiltern Duo Return offers two customers a 25% discount when travelling together on selected Chiltern Railways routes to London.

Tickets must be purchased by 23:59 the day before travel.

Validity.DayOutward: Chiltern Duo Single tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Not valid on trains timed to arrive at London Marylebone after 04:29 and before 11:30 Mondays to Fridays.

Time restrictions do not apply at weekends and on public holidays.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is allowed with this ticket, as described in the National Rail Conditions of Travel, and subject to the time restrictions applicable to the ticket.

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations  by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR on the day of validity of the ticket. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Chiltern Duo tickets must be purchased by 23:59 the day before you wish to travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Chiltern Duo tickets are available to purchase from Ticket Offices, Ticket Vending Machines, online retailers, and mobile apps.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Chiltern Duo tickets must be purchased by 23:59 the day before you wish to travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

Child.Note: Chiltern Duo tickets are sold as a package at one price for both passengers. No further discounts are available.

RailCard.Note: Chiltern Duo tickets are sold as a package at one price for both passengers. No further discounts are available.

Group.Note: Chiltern Duo tickets are sold as a package at one price for both passengers. No further discounts are available.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Chiltern Friends & Family tickets offer the holder unlimited travel across the Chiltern Railways network on the date shown on the ticket, other than at stations managed by London Underground as they don't have the ability to accept barcode tickets.

Validity.DayOutward: 1 day
Validity.TimeOutward: No time restrictions apply to this product

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: No break of journey restrictions apply

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Whilst issued as a Single for technical reasons, tickets are valid for unlimited travel across the Chiltern network on the date shown on the ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: This product is only available for purchase through the Chiltern Railways website and mobile apps.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets can be purchased on the day of travel or in advance.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: As tickets are only available through online channels, changes to travel plans require the original ticket to be refunded and a new one purchased.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds aren't generally allowed unless the tickets are returned before the date of validity shown on them.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.
TicketTypeDescription.SpecialConditions:
As tickets are only issued as e-tickets, they are not valid for travel to/from stations between Harrow-on-the-Hill and Amersham (inclusive), nor at South Ruislip and West Ruislip, as these stations are managed by London Underground and are unable to accept e-tickets.

For technical reasons, the product is priced to discourage general sales, with eligible staff being provided with a discount code to enter at checkout stage to reduce this to the desired level.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Classic accommodation are twin rooms with beds one above the other and a connecting door to the next room. Classic rooms are available for solo or twin occupancy.

Family products allow families to book 2 or 3 room. The two room product allows a maximum of 2 adults and the 3 room product a maximum of 3 adults.

These tickets can only be purchased directly with Caledonian Sleeper at: www.sleeper.scot.

Validity.DayOutward: Booked train only
Validity.TimeOutward: Booked train only

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Only valid on the train booked at the time of purchase.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Subject to availability
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Caledonian Sleeper website www.sleeper.scot and  telesales only.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Must be booked before 23:59 the day before depature
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: No changes or upgrades are allowed on Family fares.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased before 07 June 2023

Full refunds are available up to midday the day before travel. For after midnight departures this is midday 2 days before travel. There is no admin fee.

After midday the day before departure refunds are not available.

For tickets purchased on or after 07 June 2023

Refunds are available on a sliding scale depending on when the refund is requested.

  • 7 days or more before travel - a full refund is available with no admin fee
  • Less than 7 days and before midday the day before travel (2 days before for departures between 00:01 and 04:30) a 50% refund is available
  • After midday the day before departure (2 days before for departures between 00:01 and 04:30) refunds are no longer available

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A Standard Class return ticket which can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel for passengers aged 55 or over.

55B - Great Western Railway

55D - Hull Trains

55S - Great Western Railway, Hull Trains, TransPennine Express

55W - Transport for Wales

S55 - Scotrail, Avanti West Coast, LNER

Validity.DayOutward: The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: The return journey must take place within one calendar month(s) of the date shown on the ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is allowed on both portions of the ticket, as described in the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: A break of journey is allowed on both portions of the ticket, as described in the National Rail Conditions of Travel.

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Station ticket offices and some online retailers including those of the train companies involved in this promotional fare.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: This ticket can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: You may change your travel plans at any time within the validity of the ticket without having to pay an additional fare.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

A full refund is available if you return the completely unused ticket to the place of purchase within 28 days of expiry of the ticket. The amount of the refund will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: No further discounts apply.

RailCard.Note: No further discounts apply.

Group.Note: No further discounts apply.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Rail Rover for unlimited travel (subject to time restrictions shown) within the Coast & Peaks area of validity.

Available for flexible travel - 4 days within 8.

Further information can be found on Northern website.

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid for 4 days out of 8.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid for 4 days out of 8.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid on trains timed to depart after 08:45 Mondays to Fridays and at any time weekends and bank holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid on trains timed to depart after 08:45 Mondays to Fridays and at any time weekends and bank holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Rover tickets may not be issued more than 3 days in advance of their validity (5 days for telesales and internet bookings).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are only given on Rail Rover tickets returned before the start date shown on them.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 50% Child discounts.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off for holders of Two Together, Disabled Persons Railcard and Senior Railcard.

Group.Note: Family ticket available which is valid for up to four people travelling together. Maximum two adults. Two adults can travel without a child.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Conference Package sold to business customers only via GWR
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: GWR Business Travel only

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Anytime fares are fully flexible walk-up tickets with no restrictions on when you can travel

Validity.DayOutward: Anytime Singles are valid for two days including the date shown on the ticket, until 04:29 in the early morning after the last day of validity. All travel must be completed by that time.
Validity.DayReturn: n/a

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Selected Corporate channels only
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines:
Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel. Reservations are recommended on busy services.

There are no restrictions on when you can travel, however if you wish to change your date of travel or the origin and/or destination of your ticket, you will need to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see below).
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Yes
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: See refunds section

TicketTypeDescription.Description: The Cotswolds Discoverer pass is a one-day pass offering travel on trains and buses in the Cotswolds area.

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid any day.

The three day ticket is only valid for three consecutive days.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Not valid on trains before 08:50, Monday to Friday. Valid any time at weekends and on Bank Holidays.

Valid any time by bus.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Not valid on trains before 08:50, Monday to Friday. Valid any time at weekends and on Bank Holidays.

Valid any time by bus.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Can be purchased any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any staffed railway station or from the driver of any participating bus company in the Cotswold area.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines:
Cannot be purchased from a railway station more than 7 days in advance.

Can only be purchased from the bus driver on the first day of travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Ticket is valid for flexible travel but the date and duration cannot be changed once the first day of validity has commenced. Any changes to the ticket must be made before the first day of validity.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refundable, less an administration fee - only before the first day of validity.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 50% discount.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Advance Promo tickets are single (one-way) tickets.

Tickets must be booked in advance of travel and are subject to availability.

Advance tickets are valid only on the specific booked train but can be changed before departure of the first reserved service.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: N/A
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: N/A

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Customers may not start, break and resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets are valid only on the date and train service(s) shown on the ticket(s). Where applicable, valid only for travel in the Standard class and reserved seat(s) shown on the ticket(s).
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company website and selected rail appointed travel agents.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased in advance of travel, subject to availability. Tickets are available up to 12 weeks before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. You will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when they request a change.

Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased.

The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same.

The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable. An administration fee of no more than £10 may be charged per ticket for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded.

Please note: Where an Advance ticket has been purchased online and the ticket has either been self-printed at home or downloaded to a mobile device this may alter the rules for changing the ticket. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Advance tickets are non-refundable.

* * * * * * * * * * * *

Change of Journey/Excess Fare paid

Where an excess fare has been paid up to the price of a walk-up ticket, the excess fare paid varies the terms under which the passenger can travel, but it does not alter the refund terms of the original Advance ticket. If the passenger subsequently asks for a refund on the combination of Advance ticket, Excess Fare paid and any administration fees paid, the Advance ticket and change of journey fee remain non-refundable and a refund is only payable on the difference in fare paid. This may be subject to a refund fee of no more than £10.

If your journey is cancelled or disrupted

If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed by more than 60 minutes, special arrangements will be made to accommodate them on another train (although a seat cannot be guaranteed). If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed, and as a result you decide not to travel, a refund will be offered on completely unused tickets and you will not be charged an administration fee.

There may be rare occasions where a cheaper Advance fare becomes available closer to the time of travel, for example if a group booking is cancelled and extra seats become available. Please note that in these circumstances Advance tickets that have already been bought cannot be refunded in part or in full.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Rail Rover for unlimited travel within the Cumbria and beyond area of validity.

Valid for one day.

Also offers 10% off a single or return ticket on the Ravenglass & Eskdale Railway.

Further information can be found on Northern website.

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid any day.

Note that Sunday services are limited on some routes and there is no Sunday service between Barrow-in-Furness and Whitehaven.
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid any day.

Note that Sunday services are limited on some routes and there is no Sunday service between Barrow-in-Furness and Whitehaven.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Rover tickets may not be issued more than 3 days in advance of their validity (5 days for telesales and internet bookings).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are only given on Rail Rover tickets returned before the start date shown on them and are subject to an administration charge.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 50% Child discounts.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note: Family ticket available which is valid for up to four people travelling together. Maximum two adults. Two adults can travel without a child.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Multi-journey off-peak ticket, valid from 09:30 for all modes of travel in all zones of the 'nNetwork' (Network West Midlands PTE area) in Birmingham. Standard Class only.

Allows unlimited travel by train in the 'nNetwork' area and Midland Metro. In addition, it allows unlimited bus travel in the same area and further afield to and from Baginton, Bilbrook, Codsall, Himley, Hollywood, Gannow, Keresley, Kinver, Little Aston, Pattingham, Perton, Rubery, Swindon, Wombourne and Wythall.

Additional 'rail add on' tickets to stations outside of the 'nNetwork' area) are also available:

Daytripper 'add-on'

Available as an add-on to the nNetwork Daytripper ticket and allows travel on all trains from the 'nNetwork' area to Landywood, Cannock, Hednesford, Rugeley Town and Rugeley Trent Valley stations.

Daytripper Plus

Available as an add-on to the nNetwork Daytripper ticket and allows travel on all trains from the 'nNetwork' area to the following stations, Stratford-upon-Avon, Kidderminster, Leamington Spa, Lichfield and Warwick. Does not cover travel on the Coventry to Leamington Spa train line.

Full details of the full range of tickets, periods of validity, zones and maps can be found on the Network West Midlands website.

C1D - Daytripper Adult

DTA - Daytripper Adult (add-on)

CFD - Daytripper Family

DTF - Daytripper Family (add-on)

DAA - Daytripper Plus

DAF - Daytripper Plus (Family)

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Off-peak multi-journey day ticket valid for travel on most trains and buses in Derbyshire.

Derbyshire Wayfarer tickets can be used between any of the stations on the following rail lines at any time on Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holiday Mondays and after 09:00 on Mondays to Fridays:

  • Buxton - Chapel-en-le-Frith - New Mills (Newtown)
  • Sheffield - Hathersage - Hope - Edale - Chinley - New Mills (Central)
  • Sheffield - Chesterfield - Alfreton - Langley Mill
  • Sheffield - Chesterfield - Derby
  • Derby - Belper - Ambergate - Cromford - Matlock
  • Derby - Long Eaton
  • Derby - Tutbury and Hatton - Uttoxeter
  • Derby - Willington - Burton-on-Trent
  • Shirebrook - Whitwell.

These tickets can be used on the above lines on trains operated by CrossCountry, East Midlands Railway, Northern and TransPennine Express The tickets are not valid to/from East Midlands Parkway.

Nottingham and Beeston stations sell special Derbyshire Wayfarer tickets which also include rail travel from those stations to Long Eaton or Langley Mill. Group tickets are not available from these stations.

In addition, Derbyshire Wayfarer tickets can be used for travel within Derbyshire on all the local bus services operated by the companies listed, at any time on any day of the week.

They can also be used for journeys to or from Derbyshire on these companies' services operating between Derbyshire and Sheffield (city centre), Macclesfield (town centre), Leek (town centre), Burton-on-Trent (town centre) or Uttoxeter (town centre).

  • Aimée's
  • Arriva Midlands
  • D W Coaches
  • First
  • Glovers
  • G & J Holmes
  • High Peak (including Transpeak) 
  • Huddersfield Bus
  • Hulleys
  • Kinchbus
  • Little's Trave
  • McEwen's
  • Midland Classic
  • Notts & Derby
  • Stagecoach
  • TM Travel
  • Trent Barton (including, Spondon Flyer, The Mickleover, The Allestree, Amberline, Royal Derby, Harlequin, Villager, Ilkeston Flyer, Black Cat, Pronto, Swift, Two and Rainbow services)
  • Wardle Transport
  • Warringtons
  • Yourbus.

Derbyshire Wayfarer tickets cannot be used on local buses within Sheffield, Macclesfield, Leek, Burton-on-Trent or Uttoxeter, although travel between these town centres and Derbyshire is allowed.

In addition, Derbyshire Wayfarer tickets are valid:

  • within Staffordshire on Services 441 and 442
  • within Nottinghamshire on Service 15 (Ilkeston - Sawley)
  • between Derbyshire and East Midlands Airport on Services SKY ("Skylink" and "Skylink Nottingham")
  • between New Mills and Lyme Park on Service 199 (on Sundays only)
  • between Glossop and Holmfirth on Service 951.

WAY - Adult WAX - Adult (from Nottingham or Beeston) WAZ - Group

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day as shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day as shown on the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
You can buy Derbyshire Wayfarer tickets in advance, or (subject to opening hours) on the day you wish to travel, from any of these outlets:

  • tourist information centres in Ashbourne, Bakewell, Buxton, Castleton, Chesterfield, Derby, Leek, Sheffield and Swadlincote
  • the bus travel office at Derby Bus Station
  • public libraries in Alfreton, Belper, Heanor, Ilkeston, Long Eaton, Melbourne and Swadlincote
  • Peak Rail at Matlock Rail Station
  • New Mills Heritage Centre
  • Newbury's News near Dronfield Station
  • East Staffordshire Council customer service centre in Burton-on-Trent
  • Uttoxeter Town Hall.

You can also buy Derbyshire Wayfarer tickets on the day of travel from the drivers of these local buses:

  • Aimee's
  • Arriva Midlands
  • Ashbourne DRT
  • Bakewell CT (service222)
  • DW Coaches
  • First
  • G&J Holmes

  • Midland Classic
  • Moorlands Connect
  • Notts & Derby
  • Stagecoach
  • Stotts Tours
  • Tates
  • TM Travel
  • Trent Barton
  • Yourbus

Tickets can only be purchased where they are valid.

These railway station ticket offices sell Derbyshire Wayfarer tickets on the day of travel:

  • Alfreton
  • Burton-on-Trent
  • Buxton
  • Chesterfield
  • Derby, Long Eaton
  • New Mills Newtown
  • Sheffield.

Nottingham and Beeston stations sell special Derbyshire Wayfarer tickets which also include rail travel from those stations to Long Eaton or Langley Mill. Group tickets are not available from these stations.

Derbyshire Wayfarer tickets cannot be purchased on board trains, at unstaffed stations, or from station ticket machines.

Derbyshire Wayfarer tickets can also be obtained in advance by post from Derbushire County Council.

Multi-journey rail ticket for travel, subject to time restrictions shown below, in Devon.

Ticket covers the cities of Plymouth and Exeter, the seaside resorts of Torbay, South and East Devon and the lines to Barnstaple, Exmouth and Axminster.

Available as a Day Ranger

Further information can be found on Great Western's website.

TSA - Devon Day Ranger

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid for one day on the date shown on the ticket.

Except on Sleeper services, this ticket is valid for travel until 04:29 of the morning immediately following the expiry date shown on the ticket.

All travel must be completed by this time.

Sleeper Services towards London

Only valid on the date shown on the ticket (and only within the area covered by the ticket)

Sleeper Services towards Penzance (Saturday Morning Arrivals only)

For Sleeper services arriving on a Saturday morning only, this ticket must be valid on the date of arrival at your destination but need not be valid on the date of departure of the Sleeper service. The date of arrival is the first date of validity of the ticket. (Only valid within the area covered by the ticket.)

  • 07:25, 08:24 Exeter St Davids - Axminster

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A Standard Class return ticket for two people travelling together which can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.

Validity.DayOutward:
The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket.

There are time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.
Validity.DayReturn:
The return journey must be on the date shown on the ticket.

There are time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is allowed on the outward portion of the ticket.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of journey is allowed on the return portion of the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: This ticket can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: You may change your travel plans at any time within the validity of the ticket without having to pay an additional fare. There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

A full refund is available if you return the completely unused ticket to the place of purchase within 28 days of expiry of the ticket. The amount of the refund will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Validity.DayOutward: On date shown
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid at any time

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: The ticket can only be used by two people travelling together.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available between Stansted Airport and London Liverpool Street, Tottenham Hale or Stratford only, in both directions
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Available from ticket offices, travel agents, ticket vending machines and online.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Unused tickets can be refunded subject to an administration fee.

Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Multi-journey train ticket for travel in the East Midlands area.

The area of validity is bounded by Crewe, Alsager, Stoke-on-Trent, Uttoxeter, Derby, Matlock, Alfreton, Worksop, Nottingham, Lincoln, Grantham, Peterborough, Melton Mowbray, Leicester, Tamworth and Nuneaton.

More information can be found at www.nationalrail.co.uk/tickets-railcards-offers/promotions/east-midlands-day-ranger/

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day, on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day, on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid after 09:00 Mondays to Fridays and all day Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid after 09:00 Mondays to Fridays and all day Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Valid only on CrossCountry, East Midlands Railway, London Northwestern Railway, West Midlands Railway, and Northern services.

This Ranger ticket may be used in combination with another ticket for a journey commencing outside the validity area. However, the Ranger must be valid at the time the train being used makes its first stop inside the area of validity.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No reservations are needed. Tickets may be bought in advance or on the day of travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are only given on Rail Rover tickets returned before the start date shown on them.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 50% discount.

RailCard.Note:
1/3 off with Senior, 16-25 and Disabled Persons Railcards.

Flat fares for accompanied children on Family & Friends Railcards do not apply to Day Ranger tickets. The normal child rate is payable.

Group.Note: No discounts
