TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A Standard Class return ticket which can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.

This ticket can only be sold to passengers who already hold onward Eurostar or Rail Sea international tickets.

Validity.DayOutward: Tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.DayReturn: Return travel from London must commence on the day or the next day that the return travel from the Continent has taken place day, as shown by the passenger's ticket between the Continent and London, and all travel must be completed by 2 months from the date of outward travel.
Validity.TimeOutward: Passengers may travel at any time

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
Customers who change the date of their international travel leg from London, must request the ticket to be re-issued from the original retailer with a new date of travel (no earlier than the day before onward travel).

You may need to pay a difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the date and time you need to travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive):  50% discount. Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive):  50% discount. Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger. Railcard discounts: Disabled Persons Railcard: 1/3 off, Family & Friends Railcard: 1/3 off, HM Forces Railcard: 1/3 off, Network Railcard: No discount, Senior Railcard: 1/3 off and Two Together, 16-25 Railcard: 1/3 off. Other discount cards: Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: No discount and Gold Cards: No discount.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A Standard Class single ticket which can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.

This ticket can only be sold to passengers who already hold onward Eurostar or Rail Sea international tickets.

Validity.DayOutward:
Tickets are valid for travel any day between the date shown on the ticket and for two calendar months. Travel must be completed no earlier than the date before cross-border travel, and no later than 04:29 the date following the expiry date shown.

e.g. Euro Anytime Single valid from 01/04/2025 to 31/05/2025 may be used on any intermediate date, providing cross-border travel is on the same day or day following, but travel must be completed no later than 04:29 on 01/06/2025
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Passengers may travel at any time.

Refer to 'Outward Validity Day' section for further information
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: You must hold a ticket or confirmation of cross-border travel
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
Customers who change the date of their international travel leg from London, must request the ticket to be re-issued from the original retailer with a new date of travel (no earlier than the day before onward travel).

You may need to pay a difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the date and time you need to travel.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): 50% discount. Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): 50% discount. Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger. Railcard discounts: Disabled Persons Railcard: 1/3 off, Family & Friends Railcard: 1/3 off, HM Forces Railcard: 1/3 off, Network Railcard: 1/3 off, Senior Railcard: 1/3 off and Two Together, 16-25 Railcard: 1/3 off. Other discount cards: Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: No discount and Gold Cards: No discount.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A Standard class single ticket which can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.

This ticket can only be sold to passengers who already hold onward Eurostar or Rail Sea international tickets.

Validity.DayOutward:
These tickets must be used within 2 calendar months of the date of the ticket, however as they are normally associated with pre-booked services customers who wish to change their travel plans must arrange for the ticket to be re-issued at the point of purchase with a new date of travel.

Tickets are valid until 04:29 in the early morning after the last day of validity, by which time all travel must be completed.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Passengers may travel at any time.

Refer to 'Outward Validity Day' section for further information.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is permitted

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: You must hold a ticket or confirmation of cross-border travel
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, or change the date of travel.

Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey.

NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative Anytime product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): 50% discount. Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): 50% discount. Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger. Railcard discounts: Disabled Persons Railcard: 1/3 off, Family & Friends Railcard: 1/3 off, HM Forces Railcard: 1/3 off, Network Railcard: 1/3 off, Senior Railcard: 1/3 off and Two Together, 16-25 Railcard: 1/3 off. Other discount cards: Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: No discount and Gold Cards: No discount.

Validity.DayOutward:
The outward journey must take place within two calendar month(s) of the date shown on the ticket.

There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.
Validity.DayReturn:
The return journey must take place within two calendar month(s) of the date shown on the ticket.

There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Off-Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may be required to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route.

Validity.DayOutward:
Conditions of Use. You must travel in the class of accommodation shown on your ticket. Where accommodation is provided, you may upgrade to First Class.

The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket. When and where the ticket can be used. Off-Peak tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on.

Evening Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and from 18:00 until 0429 on the following day, by which time all travel must be completed.

Time restrictions are indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
Validity.DayReturn:
Conditions of Use. You must travel in the class of accommodation shown on your ticket. Where accommodation is provided, you may upgrade to First Class.

The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket. When and where the ticket can be used. Off-Peak tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on.

Evening Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and from 18:00 until 0429 on the following day, by which time all travel must be completed.

Time restrictions are indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.  If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable. If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see below)

  • 3 Child

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Evening fare for up to 4 people - at least 1 adult in the group - available after 18:30 between any two network [Network West Midlands] rail stations in standard class accommodation.

Validity.DayOutward: Any day including weekends and public holidays.
Validity.DayReturn: Any day including weekends and public holidays.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Journeys must be made on the date on the ticket after 18:30 and completed before trains stop running for the night. This applies to all days including weekends and public holidays.

Valid for travel on trains in the network area only.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Journeys must be made on the date on the ticket after 18:30 and completed before trains stop running for the night. This applies to all days including weekends and public holidays.

Valid for travel on trains in the network area only.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Not valid on Midland Metro or bus services.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability:
Available any day from stations in the

network area.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Staffed station ticket offices in the network area only.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No pre-booking required but tickets may be bought in advance. No reservations.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Wholly unused tickets are refunded. An administration charge is deducted.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Children can be added to the ticket at the special rate shown above but there is no further discount on the already discounted child rate.

RailCard.Note: No discounts.

Group.Note: This is a group discount ticket subject to the minimum and maximum numbers permitted in the description above.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Evening Out Day Travelcard is available on shorter distance journeys to London, Monday - Saturday.

It allows customers to travel to London and enjoy unlimited travel throughout London on National Rail, London Underground, Docklands Light Railway, Tramlink and London Bus services within Fare Zones 1-6

Validity.DayOutward: Evening Out Day Travelcard tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.DayReturn: Evening Out Day Travelcard tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Evening Out Day Travelcard ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Evening Out Day Travelcard ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
A break of journey is permitted on the outward portion off an Evening Out Day Travelcard unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Within the London Fares Zones, the ticket is valid for unlimited travel.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote:
A break of journey is permitted on the return portion off an Evening Out Day Travelcard unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Within the London Fares Zones, the ticket is valid for unlimited travel.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Evening Out Day Travelcard tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Evening Out Day Travelcard ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None

If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Evening Out is a cheaper ticket for shorter distance journeys, Monday - Saturday.

Validity.DayOutward: Evening Out Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.DayReturn: Evening Out Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

Evening Out Return tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Evening Out ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Evening Out Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.

If you board a train at a time when your Evening Out ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Advance purchase ticket for families

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the date and time shown on the ticket (per Advance ticket condition)

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: All channels
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Minimum 1 Adult, 1 Child. Maximum, 2 Adults, 3 Children
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: • Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. Customers will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when they request a change.Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased.The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same.The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable, plus a £10 administration fee per single ticket for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded.Please note that where an Advance ticket has been purchased online and the ticket has either been self-printed at home or downloaded to a mobile device this may alter the rules for changing the ticket. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Family tickets are non-refundable

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Advance Standard Premium Family Single

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on date and time shown on ticket

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: All channels
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be bought the day before travel. Minimum 1 adult, 1 child. Maximum 2 adults, 3 children

If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased.The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same.The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable, plus a £10 administration fee per single ticket for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded.Please note that where an Advance ticket has been purchased online and the ticket has either been self-printed at home or downloaded to a mobile device this may alter the rules for changing the ticket. TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Family tickets are non-refundable

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Return ticket valid for one month on Reading-Heathrow Railair Coach for a group of adults and children

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid for outward travel on the date on the ticket.

There are no time-of-day restrictions.
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid to return within one month.

There are no time-of-day restrictions.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Service is non-stop.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Walk-up fare. Pre-booking not required. No reservations available. A walk-up ticket is one you can buy in advance or at the station on the day you travel.

Child.Note: No further discounts.

RailCard.Note: No further discounts.

Group.Note: No further discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Transport for Wales Family Tickets allow up to 2 adults and 4 children to travel together, the adult will be charged the equivalent cost of the available return fare with up to 4 children then added at no extra cost.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid within the Transport for Wales network where an Anytime Day Return is available, tickets are valid for one return journey. The outward portion is valid on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid within the Transport for Wales network where an Anytime Day Return is available, tickets are valid for one return journey. The return portion is valid on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid to travel at any time of the day.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid to travel at any time of the day.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

All members of the family group must travel together at all times to validate the product.

If there are 10 or more traveling together, you need contact the Group Travel Department on 02920 320 002 (Opening hours: Mon-Fri 08:30 - 16:00)
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: This ticket can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company website and selected rail appointed travel agents.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: You may change your travel plans at any time within the validity of the ticket without having to pay an additional fare. Restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket may apply to both the outward and return journeys - please check before travelling.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Family tickets are refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket (s) to make your intended journey or the number of booked travellers changes, then you can get a full refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date. You will be required to purchase a new ticket for the correct number of travellers.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Group.Note: This ticket is a Group product.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Transport for Wales Family Tickets allow up to 2 adults and 4 children to travel together, the adult will be charged the equivalent cost of the available return fare with up to 4 children then added at no extra cost.

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid within the Transport for Wales network where an Off-Peak Return is available, tickets are valid for outward travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.

If you cannot complete your journey on the same day you will be allowed to finish it the following day - see 'Break of journey' below.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid within the Transport for Wales network where an Off-Peak Return is available, tickets are valid for return travel within one calendar month from the start date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 in the morning after last day of validity.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/AW [where 'AW' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Overnight break of journey - if the journey cannot be completed on the same day, the ticket may be used to continue the journey on the following day.

Unless otherwise indicated in the relevant restriction code, time restrictions apply as from the initial origin station on both days.

The appropriate restrictions for the actual day on which travel is being undertaken apply (for example, it may be that if day 1 is on Sunday, no restrictions apply, but on day 2, the Monday-Friday restrictions apply). All travel must be completed by 04:29 in the morning after this second day.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of journey is permitted on the return portion off an Off-Peak Return unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

All members of the family group must travel together at all times to validate the product.

If there are 10 or more traveling together, you need contact the Group Travel Department on 02920 320 002 (Opening hours: Mon-Fri 08:30 - 16:00)

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company website and selected rail appointed travel agents.

Restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket may apply to both the outward and return journeys - please check before travelling.

You will be required to purchase a new ticket for the correct number of travellers.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Group.Note: This ticket is a Group product.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
An Off-Peak Family Travelcard allows a minimum of 1 Adult and 1 Child (5-15 years old inclusive) return travel to London and enjoy unlimited travel throughout London on National Rail, London Underground, DLR, London Trams, and London Bus services within Zones 1-6.

All group members must travel together at all times.

Validity.DayOutward: Off-Peak Family Travelcard tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.DayReturn: Off-Peak Family Travelcard tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is permitted with an Off-Peak Family Travelcard unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code. Within the London Fares Zones, the ticket is valid for unlimited travel.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: A break of journey is permitted with an Off-Peak Family Travelcard unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code. Within the London Fares Zones, the ticket is valid for unlimited travel.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Eligibility

Off-Peak Family Travelcard is valid only when at least 1 Adult (16+) and 1 Child (5-15 years) is travelling together. Maximum group size is 2 Adults and 4 Children

Time Restrictions

Off-Peak Day tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

More Information

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Chiltern Railways Off-Peak Family Travelcards must be purchased by 23:59 the day before you wish to travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Chiltern Railways Off-Peak Family Travelcards must be purchased by 23:59 the day before you wish to travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket.

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Chiltern Railways Family Travelcards are sold as a package at one price for the entire group. No further discounts are available.

RailCard.Note: Chiltern Railways Family Travelcards are sold as a package at one price for the entire group. No further discounts are available.

Group.Note: Chiltern Railways Family Travelcards are sold as a package at one price for the entire group. No further discounts are available.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Fixed Date tickets are single (one-way) tickets for selected dates available in Express Class. Tickets are only valid on the Heathrow Express Service in the direction for which they have been purchased. Tickets must be purchased in advance of travel. These tickets are sold in limited numbers and are subject to availability. Fixed date tickets are valid only on the specific day of travel and are non-refundable.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid Only on Date of Travel
Validity.DayReturn: N/A
Validity.TimeOutward: No Time Restrictions on Travel
Validity.TimeReturn: N/A

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
• Fixed Date Single Advance tickets can only be purchased online or through the mobile app
  • Fixed Date Single Advance tickets are only available as e-tickets or mobile tickets
  • Fixed Date Single Advance tickets are valid for travel anytime of the day
  • Fixed Date Single Advance tickets are valid for one person, one way on the date it is booked to travel for
  • Fixed Date Single Advance tickets are non-refundable and non-exchangeable. This includes even during COVID-19 lockdown or quarantine scenarios
  • Fixed Date Single Advance tickets cannot be used in conjunction with any other promotion, offer, discount or any other train company special offers
  • Fixed Date Single Advance can be used in conjunction with Railcards
  • Fixed Date Single Advance tickets are available only in Express Class
  • Fixed Date Single Advance tickets are released in limited quantities and are subject to availability, book as early as possible to avoid disappointment
  • Fixed Date Single Advance tickets are released 52 weeks in advance
  • There may be rare occasions where cheaper Fixed Date Single Advance tickets becomes available closer to the time of travel. Please note that in these circumstances Fixed Date Single Advance tickets that have already been bought cannot be refunded in part or in full.
  • Heathrow Rewards points are earned on Fixed Date Tickets at the standard rate. Visit https://rewards.heathrow.com for further information.
  • Children aged 15 or under travel free with an accompanying adult (aged 18 or over) travelling with a valid ticket, including Fixed Date Tickets. When booking adult tickets online, the number of children's tickets much be selected.
  • Fixed Date Tickets purchased cannot be re-sold.
  • Fixed Date Tickets are non-exchangeable and non-refundable during COVID-19 lockdown or quarantine scenarios.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: 90 days or longer before travel
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Online
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Tickets are non-exchangeable
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Tickets are non-refundable

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Fixed Family Advance tickets are single (one-way) tickets for many longer and some shorter distance journeys, are available in First Class and Standard Class and can offer the best available price for each journey. These tickets are for groups of up to six consisting of a minimum of one adult and one child and up to two adults and up to four children.

Tickets must be booked in advance of travel and are subject to availability.

Advance tickets are valid only on the specific booked train, but can be changed before departure of the first reserved service.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Customers may not start, break and resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Not applicable. Advance are single tickets only.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Change of Journey/Excess Fare paid

Where an excess fare has been paid up to the price of a walk-up ticket, the excess fare paid varies the terms under which the passenger can travel, but it does not alter the refund terms of the original Advance ticket.

If the passenger subsequently asks for a refund on the combination of Advance ticket, Excess Fare paid and any administration fees paid, the Advance ticket and change of journey fee remain non-refundable and a refund is only payable on the difference in fare paid. This may be subject to a refund fee of no more than £10 per group ticket.

If your journey is cancelled or disrupted

If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed by more than 60 minutes, special arrangements will be made to accommodate them on another train (although a seat cannot be guaranteed).

If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed, and as a result you decide not to travel, a refund will be offered on completely unused tickets and you will not be charged an administration fee.

There may be rare occasions where a cheaper Advance fare becomes available closer to the time of travel, for example if a group booking is cancelled and extra seats become available. Please note that in these circumstances Advance tickets that have already been bought cannot be refunded in part or in full.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: The 'Family ticket' fares are available for purchase all year round (inclusive). Tickets are valid only on the date and train service(s) shown on the ticket(s). Where applicable, valid only for travel in the class and reserved seat(s) shown on the ticket(s).
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company website and selected rail appointed travel agents.

Some Advance fares (particularly the very cheapest) or additional availability on low quota fares are only available to purchase direct from the train company that the ticket is valid to travel with - usually only via own website GWR.com.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased in advance of travel, subject to availability. Generally, the earlier the ticket is booked the better the deal. Tickets are available up to 12 weeks before travel. Tickets must be purchased in advance and is not available for purchase once the service has departed. This ticket is not guaranteed on any train and availability may be limited.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. Customers will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when they request a change.

Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased.

The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same.

The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable.

An administration fee of no more than £10 may be charged per ticket (per group) for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded.

Please note: Where an Advance ticket has been purchased online and the ticket has either been self-printed at home or downloaded to a mobile device this may alter the rules for changing the ticket. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought. These terms and conditions (“terms”) apply to the discounted fares offered by GWR for Families travelling with this ticket.

These terms apply in addition to GWR's full ticket terms and conditions, and in the event of any conflict between any of these terms and the full ticket terms and conditions these terms will prevail.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Advance tickets are non-refundable.

Group.Note: Yes (Up to 2 Adults and up to four Children).

Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Flexi Season products that can be fulfilled to a smartcard

Validity.TimeOutward:
Flexi season 10 includes 10 return day tickets with a month validityFlexi season 20 includes 20 return day tickets with a 3 month validity

The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A Flexi Season ticket provides customers with 8 days of travel in 28 days - any time between two named stations.

The 8 days of travel doesn't need to be specified in advance, giving part-time commuters more flexibility to travel when they want and need.

The Flexi Season ticket will offer a minimum of 20% discount on an equivalent Monthly Season ticket, offering savings for commuters travelling during peak times, 2-3 days a week.

Flexi Season tickets will be available as either a Smartcard or Barcode ticket, check with your train operator to see what they offer. If you don't have a Smartcard then please allow plenty of time to get one before you travel - they can take up to 5 days to arrive by post.

Validity.DayOutward: Any day and up to 04:29 after expiry date on ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Any day and up to 04:29 after expiry date on ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Any time
Validity.TimeReturn: Any time

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Season Ticket Terms & Conditions can be found at https://www.nationalrail.co.uk/tickets-railcards-and-offers/ticket-types/season-tickets/season-tickets-terms-and-conditions/
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Online, Ticket Office and TVM
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: It is not possible to change your journey. Any outstanding days' worth of travel should be refunded and a new Flexi Season Ticket purchased.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Flexi Season Ticket is refundable within it's period of validity. A deduction from the amount paid will be made to cover each days' worth of travel already used, and an administration charge of no more than £10.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Rail Rover for unlimited travel (subject to time restrictions shown) within Devon and Cornwall.

Available for flexible periods, either 3 days within 7 [D07] or 8 days within 15 [D37].

Further information can be found on Great Western Railway's website.

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid for 3 days out of 7 or 8 days out of 15 according to choice.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.

Except on Sleeper services, this ticket is valid for travel until 04:29 of the morning immediately following the expiry date shown on the ticket.

All travel must be completed by this time.

Sleeper Services towards London

Only valid on the date shown on the ticket (within the area covered by the ticket)

Sleeper Services towards Penzance (Saturday Morning Arrivals only)

For Sleeper services arriving on a Saturday morning only, this ticket must be valid on the date of arrival at your destination but need not be valid on the date of departure of the Sleeper service. The date of arrival is the first date of validity of the ticket. (Only valid within the area covered by the ticket.)
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid for 3 days out of 7 or 8 days out of 15 according to choice.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.

Except on Sleeper services, this ticket is valid for travel until 04:29 of the morning immediately following the expiry date shown on the ticket.

All travel must be completed by this time.

Sleeper Services towards London

Only valid on the date shown on the ticket (within the area covered by the ticket)

Sleeper Services towards Penzance (Saturday Morning Arrivals only)

For Sleeper services arriving on a Saturday morning only, this ticket must be valid on the date of arrival at your destination but need not be valid on the date of departure of the Sleeper service. The date of arrival is the first date of validity of the ticket. (Only valid within the area covered by the ticket.)
Validity.TimeOutward:
Valid from 08:45 Monday to Friday, with easements on earlier services as follows:

All day on the St Ives Branch

All day on trains from Exeter to Barnstaple or Okehampton

08:35 from Barnstaple and 08:43 from Umberleigh, on the service towards Exeter

08:30 from Newton Abbot and 08:42 from Totnes, on the service towards Plymouth

08:38 service from Newton Abbot to Paignton

08:09 service from Plymouth to Penzance

08:28 service from Plymouth to Gunnislake

08:16 service from Liskeard to Looe

07:25, 08:24 Exeter St Davids - Axminster

Valid at any time on Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn:
From 7th September 2025 the following validity will apply:

All day on the St Ives Branch

All day on trains from Exeter to Barnstaple or Okehampton

08:35 from Barnstaple and 08:43 from Umberleigh, on the service towards Exeter

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Rover tickets may not be issued more than 3 days in advance of their validity (5 days for telesales and internet bookings).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are only available on unused tickets surrendered before the day of travel and may be subject to an administration fee.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 50% discount.

RailCard.Note: Discounted rate for holders of 16-25 Railcard, Family & Friends, Two Together, Disabled Persons Railcard, HM Forces Railcard and Senior Railcard.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Friends and Family Ticket (Network West Midlands)
TicketTypeDescription.Description:
nNetwork Friends and Family fare is available off-peak for one adult for £1 and up to two children aged 5-15 for 50p each when accompanying a holder of a valid adult nTrain or nNetwork Season Ticket, for a return journey between two stations in the Network West Midlands area, as long as the stations are in the same rail zones covered by the nTrain or nNetwork Season Ticket.

All persons using this fare, including the Season Ticket holder, must travel together at all times.

Friends and Family fares are available between any two stations in the Network West Midlands area (plus Rugeley Trent Valley, Rugeley Town, Hednesford, Cannock and Landywood - only if a train Season ticket is held) on journeys covered by the Season Ticket, e.g. if Season Ticket is valid for zones 1-3 then the Friends and Family fare is also valid for journeys in zone 1-3.

Season ticket holders must possess a 1 week, 4 week, monthly Direct Debit, 13 week or 52 week nTrain or nNetwork Season Ticket.

Full details of the full range of tickets, periods of validity, zones and maps can be found on the nNetwork [Network West Midlands] website.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid for travel only on the date on the ticket and on the routes permitted by the accompanying passenger's Season Ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid for travel only on the date on the ticket and on the routes permitted by the accompanying passenger's Season Ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Friends and Family fares are available for off-peak travel after 09:30 on Monday to Fridays and anytime on Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holiday Mondays.
Validity.TimeReturn: Friends and Family fares are available for off-peak travel after 09:30 on Monday to Fridays and anytime on Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holiday Mondays.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Fare applies to adult Season Tickets only, i.e. not valid with

network Child Term Extra season ticket.

nNetwork 2 week Off-Peak, nNetwork Student, Concessionary Passes and pre-09:30 tickets cannot be used in conjunction with this fare.

Not available with any other rail offer.

No further discounts for Railcard holders.

Not valid to or from stations outside the nNetwork area.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available any day from stations in the nNetwork area.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
Staffed station ticket offices in the nNetwork area.

Not available to purchase on board the train unless travelling from an unstaffed station.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No pre-booking required.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Wholly unused tickets are refunded. An administration charge is deducted.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Children can be added to the ticket at the special rate shown above but there is no further discount on the already discounted child rate.

RailCard.Note: No discounts.

Group.Note: No discounts.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Minimum fares / time restrictions may apply to tickets bought with a Railcard or other discount card. See terms and conditions of the appropriate Railcard or discount card for details.

Railcard holders travelling on a discounted ticket must carry their Railcard when they travel. If a Railcard holder fails to produce their valid Railcard with their ticket, they will be required to pay a full priced ticket for their journey as if no Railcard and/or no ticket were held.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A Standard Class return ticket for 2, 3 or 4 adults travelling together to Gatwick Airport at a special flat rate price. Additionally, up to 4 accompanied children can travel for the flat fare of £1 each.

  • G2F - Gatwick Flyaway Two
  • G3F - Gatwick Flyaway Three
  • G4F - Gatwick Flyaway Four
  • GF1 - Gatwick Flyaway Child (this can only be sold alongside one of the adult ticket types G2F, G3F, G4F. GF1 can only be sold separately if the customer already holds an adult Gatwick Flyaway ticket for the same journey, which they show at the time of booking.)

Validity.DayOutward: The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: The return journey must take place within one calendar month of the date shown on the ticket
Validity.TimeOutward: There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: No break of journey is allowed with this ticket
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: No break of journey is allowed with this ticket

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available from ticket offices at all railway stations.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: This ticket can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: You may change your travel plans at any time within the validity of the ticket without having to pay an additional fare. There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are available on completely unused tickets, within 28 days of the expiry date, less a £10 admin fee, per booking. 

If some travel has been completed, the refund should be calculated as follows: 

Calculate the price that would have been paid if the customer had purchased Anytime tickets for all members of the group, for all travel completed using the ticket.  Add one £10 admin fee to this total. Subtract this amount from the total price paid for the tickets.  The remaining amount (if any) is the refund due.  (In some circumstances no refund will be due.) 

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Children travelling with adults need the £1 flat fare child ticket. No other child discounts are available.

TicketTypeDescription.SpecialConditions:
Tickets are only available from the origin to Gatwick Airport (not available with Gatwick as the origin). They are only valid via the North Downs route (Reading – Guildford – Redhill – Gatwick Airport) and are not valid for travel via London.

Customers using a Gatwick Flyaway ticket must travel together at all times during the outward and return journey.

Proof of airline travel may be requested when purchasing tickets.

One ticket covers the whole adult group. Children are issued with separate individual tickets.

Tickets are currently only available from ticket offices, so will currently only be issued on CCST / paper tickets.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
• One-way Standard Class ticket offering the best value price for each journey
  • Non-refundable
  • Change of journeys may incur a fee
  • Ticket must be booked in advance of travel and are subject to availability
  • Valid only on the specific booked train.

Luggage

Know your Luggage limits - You may travel with no more than 3 items of luggage - a maximum of one suitcase (not exceeding 30x70x90cm), a small carry-on bag, and a handbag. Please don’t exceed these limits, as extra items will only be carried if there is available space on board, and excess charges may apply. Visit our Grand Central Luggage page for more information: https://www.grandcentralrail.com/travelling-with-gc/on-board/cyclists-luggage

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: N/A
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket
Validity.TimeReturn: N/A

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Customers may not start, break and resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Not applicable. GC Advance are single tickets only.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Where an excess fare has been paid up to the price of a walk-up ticket, the excess fare paid varies the terms under which the passenger can travel, but it does not alter the refund terms of the original GC Advance ticket. If the passenger subsequently asks for a refund on the combination of GC Advance ticket, Excess fare paid and any administration fees paid, the GC Advance ticket and change of journey fee remain non-refundable and a refund is only payable on the difference in fare paid. This may be subject to a refund fee of no more than £10 if your journey is cancelled or disrupted.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets are valid only on the date and train service(s) shown on the ticket(s). Where applicable, valid only for travel in the class and reserved seat)s) shown on the ticket(s).
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company websites and selected rail appointed travel agents.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased in advance of travel, subject to availability. Generally, the earlier the ticket is booked the better the deal. Tickets are available upto 26 weeks before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new ticket must be purchased. You will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when a change is requested.

Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased.

  • The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same.
  • The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable.
  • An administration fee of no more than £10 may be charged per ticket for each change to a journey.
  • If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded.

Please note

Where a GC Advance ticket has been purchased online and the ticket has either been self-printed at home or downloaded to a mobile device this may alter the rules for changing the ticket. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: GC Advance tickets are non-refundable.

Child.Note: Children aged 5 to 15 inclusive: 50% discount. Up to two children under 5 may travel free of charge with each fare-paying adult.

  • Senior,
  • HM Forces,
  • Disabled Persons,
  • Two Together.

Customers can bring a maximum of 3 items onboard:

1 x suitcase (no bigger than 30x70x90cm)

1 x small carry-on bag

1 x handbag

All luggage is carried in accordance with the National Rail Conditions of Travel.

Please don't exceed these limits, as extra items will only be carried if there is available space onboard, and from May 2024 excess charges will apply.

Supplement available onboard GC services only.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Travel for a day anywhere in Greater Manchester and in parts of Cheshire, Derbyshire, Lancashire, Staffordshire and the Peak District.

The Wayfarer ticket lets you travel by Metrolink tram or trains and buses run by participating Wayfarer companies.

The Wayfarer ticket lets you travel on any participating bus at any time. You can also travel on Metrolink trams or any train run by a participating company after 9.30am Monday to Friday, and at any time on weekends and bank holidays.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: You can buy Wayfarer tickets from staffed railway stations and PayPoint outlets in the area of validity.

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

Validity.DayOutward: Monday-Friday
Validity.DayReturn: Monday-Friday
Validity.TimeOutward: After 09:30
Validity.TimeReturn: After 09:30

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
• Valid only for groups of at least 10 passengers. Each passenger is issued with their own ticket, but the group must travel together at all times.
  • The group can be made up of any combination of adults and paid-for children under 16, provided the total number is 10 or more (note that on Transport for London Rail and Bus services accompanied children under 11 travel free).
  • Unlimited travel after 09:30 Monday-Fridays (anytime at weekends) on National Rail, London Underground, DLR, Bus and Tram within the London Fares Zones.
  • Not valid on Heathrow Express
  • Not valid on Southeastern Highspeed services between St Pancras International and Stratford International unless routed 'Plus High Speed'.
  • Ticket available for London Zones 1-6 or Zones 1-9.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Buy Groupsave tickets to save 1/3 off adult tickets when you travel in a group of three to nine adults. Plus you can take up to four children with you for just £1 each.

Groupsave tickets are available as a standard discount and do not require a Railcard – just buy your tickets from any ticket office.

Validity.DayOutward:
What you need to know:

  • Groupsave tickets are available on selected routes only
  • The return journey must be completed on the same day
  • No further discounts (using Railcards, for example) are available for any of the passengers
  • They’re valid for travel after 09:30 on weekdays or anytime at weekends and bank holidays
  • They’re not valid for journeys starting or finishing at London St Pancras
  • They’re not available to buy online
  • All members of the group must travel together for both outward and return journeys
  • GroupSave cannot be used in conjunction with any other promotion or offer
  • Groups must travel together throughout their entire journey
  • Excess fares or Penalty Fares may be payable by the group if restrictions are broken
  • All members of the group must travel together for both outward and return journeys

There may be restrictions on the journeys, dates and times you can travel with Groupsave. For more details, check with our specialist sales team on 03457 125 678 (option 2, 2).

If you’re travelling from East Midlands Parkway, Kettering, Loughborough, Market Harborough or Wellingborough, you can also choose a Park & Ride ticket and save on your car parking and train travel.

There may be restrictions on the journeys, dates and times you can travel with Groupsave. For more details, check with our specialist sales team on 03457 125 678 (option 2, 2).
