In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
No child rate discount, but children can be counted as adults as part of the group.

In addition, two children under the age of five can travel free of charge with each fare paying passenger.

Passengers that form the group (and any accompanying children) must travel together at all times.

RailCard.Note: No discounts

Group.Note: This ticket is a Group product.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
GWR Corporate Fares are designed to be discounted for corporate clients under bulk purchase. 

Members of the public will not be able to benefit from these corporate discounts, so should find that walk up and Advance Purchase will offer better value.
TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Associated ticket codes for GWR Corporate tickets are:

  • FC1 - GWR CORP SGL1ST  for terms and conditions see https://internal.nationalrail.co.uk/times_fares/ticket_types/FDS.aspx
  • FC2 - GWR CORP RTN1ST for terms and conditions see https://internal.nationalrail.co.uk/times_fares/ticket_types/GUR.aspx
  • FC3 - GWR CORP SGL for terms and conditions see https://internal.nationalrail.co.uk/times_fares/ticket_types/SDS.aspx 
  • FC4 - GWR CORP RTN for terms and conditions see https://internal.nationalrail.co.uk/times_fares/ticket_types/GOR.aspx 
  • GWC -  FIRST CLASS CARNET
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
For more information on Business Direct and Business group travel please visit

https://www.gwr.com/your-tickets/ways-to-save/business-travel

TicketTypeDescription.Description: GWR Promotional Advance tickets are Single (one-way) tickets for many longer and some shorter distance journeys, are available in First Class and Standard Class where appropriate. These tickets are valid only on the specific booked train(s), and are non-refundable except under the terms of the National Rail Conditions of Travel. They can be changed (for a fee) for alternate dates / times subject to availability.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Customers may not start, break and resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Not applicable. Advance fares are Single tickets only.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets are valid only on the date and train service(s) shown on the ticket(s). Where applicable, valid only for travel in the class and reserved seat(s) shown on the ticket(s).
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company website and selected rail appointed travel agents. available at GWR.com/sale and GWR App.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Subject to availability and may not be available at certain times. Not valid with other offers, promotions or discounts. Selected routes only. Full terms and conditions at GWR.com/sale
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. Customers will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when they request a change. Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased. The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same. The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable, plus a £10 administration fee per person, per single ticket for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Advance tickets are non-refundable, except under the terms of the National Rail Conditions of Travel.

Where an excess fare has been paid up to the price of a walk-up ticket, the excess fare paid varies the terms under which the passenger can travel, but it does not alter the refund terms of the original Advance ticket. If the passenger subsequently asks for a refund on the combination of Advance ticket plus Excess Fare plus £10 fee, the Advance ticket and £10 fee remain non-refundable and a refund is only payable on the excess fare paid, less a £10 refund fee.

If the train the customer purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed by more than 60 minutes, special arrangements will be made to accommodate them on another train (although a seat cannot be guaranteed). If the train the customer purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed and as a result, customers decide not to travel, a refund will be offered on completely unused tickets and they will not be charged an administration fee.

There may be rare occasions where a cheaper Advance fare becomes available closer to the time of travel, for example if a group booking is cancelled and extra seats become available. Please note that in these circumstances Advance tickets that have already been bought cannot be refunded in part or in full.

Child.Note: Children aged 5 to 15 inclusive: 50% discount. Up to two children aged under 5 may travel free of charge with each fare-paying adult.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Hadrian's Wall Country Line Day Ranger
TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Rail Rover for unlimited travel (subject to time restrictions shown) within the Hadrian's Wall Line area of validity.

Valid for one day.

Further information can be found on Northern website.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Valid after 08:45 weekdays.

Valid any time Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Valid after 08:45 weekdays.

Valid any time Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, rail-appointed travel agents, onboard trains from the conductor if boarding at an unstaffed station, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Rover tickets may not be issued more than 3 days in advance of their validity (5 days for telesales and internet bookings).
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: The ticket has unlimited travel on the day(s) of validity but you cannot change the travel validity period of the ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are only given on Rail Rover tickets returned before the start date shown on them and are subject to an administration charge.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 50% Child discounts.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Rail Rover for unlimited travel (subject to time restrictions shown)

Available for seven consecutive days or as a Flexi Rover valid for any three days travel in a period of seven consecutive days.

Further information can be found on West Midlands Railway website.

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid for any seven consecutive days from the start date on the ticket or on the Flexi Rover for any three days from the start date on the ticket.

On the Flexi Rover, the ticket holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting the journey.
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid for any seven consecutive days from the start date on the ticket or on the Flexi Rover for any three days from the start date on the ticket.

On the Flexi Rover, the ticket holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting the journey.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Valid after 09:00 Monday to Fridays.

Valid any time Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Valid after 09:00 Monday to Fridays.

Valid any time Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid after 09:40 Monday-Friday and any time on Saturdays, Sundays and public holidays.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid after 09:40 Monday-Friday and any time on Saturdays, Sundays and public holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Highland Explorer Off Peak Day Return
TicketTypeDescription.Description:
As part of the launch of the new Highland Explorer carriage on Glasgow-Oban route, ScotRail have created new Highland Explorer products which permits travel within this carriage.

Highland Explorer tickets for same day travel on Glasgow to Oban services, travelling in Highland Explorer carriage with extra space and comfort.

Complimentary refreshments on the train subject to availability.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid for travel on date on Ticket
Validity.DayReturn: Valid for travel on date on Ticket
Validity.TimeOutward: Off-Peak
Validity.TimeReturn: Off-Peak

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Journeys can not be broken, other than for connectional purposes.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Journeys can not be broken, other than for connectional purposes.

• www.scotrail.co.uk
  • At staffed National Rail stations
  • National Rail Appointed Travel Agents

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 50% discount for children aged 5-15. Under 5s travel free.

RailCard.Note: Standard National Railcard discounts apply.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Rail Rover for unlimited travel (subject to time restrictions shown) throughout the Highland area of validity.

Available for four days travel within 8 consecutive days.

Valid by rail on the following routes:

  • Glasgow - Oban / Fort William / Mallaig
  • Inverness - Kyle of Lochalsh
  • Inverness - Wick/Thurso
  • Inverness - Aberdeen
  • Inverness - Aviemore

Also available for:

  • 10% discount on Strathspey Steam Railway standard fares
  • 10% discount on Keith & Dufftown Railway standard fares

Also valid on the following ferry routes:

  • Caledonian MacBrayne
    • Oban - Craignure or Tobermory, Isle of Mull
    • Mallaig to Armadale, Isle of Skye
  • Gourock and Dunoon with Argyll Ferries
  • 20% off these Northlink Ferries journeys when Seated Standard tickets are purchased on the day of travel:
    • Scrabster - Stromness
    • ​Aberdeen - Lerwick
    • Aberdeen - Kirkwall - Lerwick

Also valid on the following bus and coach routes:

  • Travel with Scottish Citylink Coaches and West Coast Motors between Oban / Fort William - Inverness​
  • Travel with Stagecoach in the Highlands between ​Thurso - Scrabster
  • 10% discount on Single and Day Return tickets with D & E Coaches between Inverness and Durness

Further information can be found on (http://www.scotrail.co.uk/offers/travel-passes/highland-rover#Highland Rover)

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid for 4 days out of 8 according to choice and within the validity of the ticket.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid for 4 days out of 8 according to choice and within the validity of the ticket.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Valid any time Mondays to Fridays, except on services timed to arrive in Aberdeen and Inverness before 09:00. 

Valid any time Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Valid any time Mondays to Fridays, except on services timed to arrive in Aberdeen and Inverness before 09:00. 

Valid any time Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Hull Leisure Return tickets are fully flexible walk-up tickets with no restrictions on when you can travel on Hull Trains services

Validity.DayOutward: Hull Leisure Return tickets are valid for outward travel at any time on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.DayReturn: Hull Leisure Return tickets are valid for return travel at any time up to one calendar month from the date shown on the outward ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Anytime
Validity.TimeReturn: Anytime

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Hull Leisure Return tickets can be used at any time but require you to travel via Hull Trains services only, which will be clearly stated on the ticket.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Only available at ticket offices and Hull Train website
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Standard Class Return ticket to London with added benefits.

The package includes:

  • Standard Class Return rail travel on London North Eastern Railway services.
  • One outward and one return free seat reservation.
  • A £5 complimentary catering voucher, which can be used on the either the outward or return journey.
  • Overnight parking at Lincoln Central Multi-story Car Park - Melville St, Lincoln LN5 7BP (two consecutive days only). If a longer stay is required this will need to be paid for when exiting at the car park kiosk.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day, as dated on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day within one calendar month.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid for the train specified on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid on any London North Eastern Railway service.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: You may not start, break and resume, or end your journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of journey is allowed at stations served by LNER services.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: The product will be offered to select corporates through corporate channels, following prior agreement with London North Eastern Railway.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
Available to selected corporate clients, booking through corporate channels. Sales channels include third-party travel agents and the LNER4Business corporate travel website. 

Not available online or at stations
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Can be bought up to 23.59 on the day before travel, subject to availability.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
• Change of outward date allowed, without penalty, if done in advance of travel date and within the validity of the ticket.
  • Change of outward train allowed, on the date shown on the ticket, without penalty.
  • Upgrade to First Class is allowed upon payment of the difference between the SOS and FOS fare for each leg excessed to/from London. No additional benefits are available
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
• Completely unused and returned complete - full refund less any local cancellation charge.
  • Unused outward leg and partially completed journeys - no refund.
  • Unused return legs - from the ticket value, deduct the equivalent Standard Class Anytime Single fare.
  • Deduct a further £20·00 plus any local cancellation fees, and refund the remainder.
  • Car parking used but ticket unused - deduct £20 plus any local cancellation fees and refund the remainder.
  • Buffet voucher not used - no refund.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Advance tickets are available on many longer and some shorter distance journeys, they are available in First Class and Standard Class and offer the best available price for each journey.

Tickets must be booked in advance of travel and are subject to availability.

Advance tickets are valid only on the specific booked train but can be changed before departure of the first reserved service.

Advance ITX Fares are used by agents.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Customers may not start, break and resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Customers may not start, break and resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets are valid only on the date and train service(s) shown on the ticket(s). Where applicable, valid only for travel in the class and reserved seat(s) shown on the ticket(s).
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Train company telesales office, online at most train company website and selected rail appointed travel agents.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased in advance of travel, subject to availability. Generally, the earlier the ticket is booked the better the deal. Tickets are available up to 12 weeks before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. You will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when they request a change.

Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased.

The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same.

The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable. An administration fee of no more than £10 may be charged per ticket for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded.

Please note: Where an Advance ticket has been purchased online and the ticket has either been self-printed at home or downloaded to a mobile device this may alter the rules for changing the ticket. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: • Advance tickets are non-refundable.* * * * * * * * * * * *Change of Journey/Excess Fare paidWhere an excess fare has been paid up to the price of a walk-up ticket, the excess fare paid varies the terms under which the passenger can travel, but it does not alter the refund terms of the original Advance ticket. If the passenger subsequently asks for a refund on the combination of Advance ticket, Excess Fare paid and any administration fees paid, the Advance ticket and change of journey fee remain non-refundable and a refund is only payable on the difference in fare paid. This may be subject to a refund fee of no more than £10.If your journey is cancelled or disruptedIf the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed by more than 60 minutes, special arrangements will be made to accommodate them on another train (although a seat cannot be guaranteed). If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed, and as a result you decide not to travel, a refund will be offered on completely unused tickets and you will not be charged an administration fee.There may be rare occasions where a cheaper Advance fare becomes available closer to the time of travel, for example if a group booking is cancelled and extra seats become available. Please note that in these circumstances Advance tickets that have already been bought cannot be refunded in part or in full.

The cost of a London North Eastern Railway Mid-Week Upgrade varies depending on the journey:

  • Between London/Stevenage and Doncaster/Selby/Brough/Hull or any stations in between - £39 per single journey.
  • Between London/Stevenage and York/Wakefield or any stations further north - £49 per single journey.
  • For LNER journeys wholly north of Stevenage - £39 per single journey.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on Off-Peak Mid Week Services
Validity.DayReturn: Valid on Off-Peak Mid Week services
Validity.TimeOutward: Off-Peak services. Subject to availability
Validity.TimeReturn: Off-Peak services. Subject to availability

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Monday to Friday except Bank Holidays, subject to availability
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: On board LNER services only, subject to availability and  capacity. Supplement may not always be available on busy Off-Peak services.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Can only be purchased onboard the train, subject to availability and capacity. Cannot be purchased in advance
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Can only be purchased onboard the train, subject to availability and capacity. Cannot be purchased in advance
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refunds are not offered

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A semi-flexible day return from selected Greater Anglia station to Stratford and London Liverpool Street. Available in First Class (OFK) and Standard Class (OSK) versions.

Validity.DayOutward: Booked train only
Validity.DayReturn: Same day as outward travel
Validity.TimeOutward: Available on trains departing after 13:59
Validity.TimeReturn: Any service departing after 19:04

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Not permitted.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: This is a semi-flexible return that is train-specific on the outward journey but flexible on the return journey, which has to be on the same day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Available at station booking offices and online through websites and apps.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Book by 23:59 on the day before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Changes before departure of the booked train are possible for a fee.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refunds are not available.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A semi-flexible overnight return ticket to London and Stratford (London) from stations on the Great Eastern Main Line (Norwich to Colchester) and branches. Travel outwards in the afternoon or evening towards London on a specified train and return the following day on any train departing before 12.01 except between 06.59 and 08.01. If the return day is a Saturday, Sunday or Bank Holiday, return at any time before 12.01.

Tickets are available in First Class (OFL) and Standard Class (OSL) versions.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on first day of validity only.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid to return on the day after the outward journey only.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid on the booked train only. Available trains are from early afternoon onwards. The exact time of the first valid train varies from station to station. Journey planners will show when the ticket is available.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid on any train scheduled to depart before 06.59 and after 08.01, but before 12.01 (any time before 12.01 if the return journey is on a Saturday, Sunday or Bank Holiday).

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Limited availability
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company websites and apps, and selected rail appointed travel agents.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Available until 23.59 on the day before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Outward journey time can be changed on payment of a fee and any difference in fare. Return journey time after the expiry of validity can be changed on payment of the difference in fare.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refunds are not available.

Child.Note:
Children aged 5 to 15 inclusive: 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 may travel free of charge with each fare-paying adult.

RailCard.Note:
1/3 off with the following Railcards:

  • 16-25,
  • Family & Friends,
  • Senior,
  • HM Forces,
  • Disabled Persons,
  • Veterans,
  • Two Together

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: London North Eastern Railway Family Return
TicketTypeDescription.Description: The London North Eastern Railway (LNER) Family Return provides a simple to understand return-journey price for a small group of up to two adults and four children, to travel onboard LNER services only.  The product is sold subject to availability, on a first-come, first-served basis, and seats are reserved for the whole party for both the outward and return journey.

Validity.DayOutward: Date on ticket
Validity.DayReturn: Date on ticket
Validity.TimeOutward: Time on ticket
Validity.TimeReturn: Time on ticket

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
London North Eastern Railway (LNER) Family Return tickets are only valid on the LNER services shown on the ticket, and are non-changeable and non-refundable once purchased. These tickets are not valid on the services of any other operator.

These tickets cover a minimum group of 1 Adult and 1 Child or maximum group of 2 Adults and 4 Children (aged 5-15 years).  Children aged 4 and under can be reserved a seat provided they are included as part of the overall number travelling.

The group size must match the specified group shown on the ticket.  Passengers must travel on the specified journey, date and time shown on the ticket, and sit in the seats allocated.  No further discounts are available.

Further information can be found here.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Sold subject to availability. Seat reservations are compulsory for both outward and return journeys and must be made at the point of booking.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Online, travel centres, third party travel agents and travel management companies.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Must be purchased by 23:59, 7 days prior to the outward departure date. Tickets are sold subject to availability.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: No changes are permitted.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Tickets are non-refundable.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: London North Eastern Railway Family Single
TicketTypeDescription.Description: The London North Eastern Railway (LNER) Family Single provides a single price for a small group of up to two adults and four children, to travel onboard LNER services only. The product is sold subject to availability, on a first-come, first-served basis, and seats are reserved for the whole party.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid only on the specific booked train(s).
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
Customers must not break their journey, i.e. they may not start, break/resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station en route. Customers risk being denied entry or exit at those stations or being charged for a new fare.

Customers are permitted to interchange at intermediate stations to/from valid connecting services.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
London North Eastern Railway (LNER) Family Single tickets are only valid on the LNER services shown on the ticket, and are non-changeable and non-refundable once purchased. These tickets are not valid on the services of any other operator.

These tickets cover a minimum group of 1 Adult and 1 Child or a maximum group of 2 Adults and 4 Children (aged 0-15 years). Children aged under 5 can be included as part of the overall group booked and therefore reserved a seat. Up to two children aged under 5 can otherwise travel unreserved for free with each booked adult, and may occupy a seat providing it is not required by another fare-paying customer.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: These tickets are available at any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online via most train company and third party websites, selected ticket vending machines and rail appointed travel agents.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased at least 7 days in advance of travel and are subject to availability. Tickets are generally available up to 12 weeks before travel, but will not be available in the extended booking horizon.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: No changes permitted.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Tickets are non-refundable.
TicketTypeDescription.SpecialConditions:
The group size must match the specified group shown on the ticket. Passengers must travel as per the specified journey, date and time shown on the ticket, and sit in the seats allocated. No further discounts are available.

Further information can be found here.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: London North Eastern Railway Flat Fare Standard
TicketTypeDescription.Description: London North Eastern Railway Flat Fare Standard

Validity.DayOutward: As shown on the ticket
Validity.TimeOutward: As shown on the ticket

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Travel on the date and time displayed on the ticket

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Prior to reservations closing

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Long Weekend Return allows travel outward on a Friday (WKD) or a Saturday (WKE).

Return travel valid Mondays only.

Not valid for use on Sundays

Validity.DayOutward: Long Weekend Return tickets are valid for outward travel on the date shown on the ticket which must be either a Friday (WKD) or Saturday (WKE).
Validity.DayReturn: Long Weekend Return tickets are valid for return travel Mondays only. (This includes travel up until 04:29 on Tuesday.) Not valid for use on Sundays
Validity.TimeOutward:
Friday (WKD) tickets are also valid for outward travel until 04:29 on the Saturday.

Saturday (WKE) tickets are also valid for travel until 04:29 on the Sunday.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Long Weekend Return tickets are valid for return travel Mondays only. (This includes travel up until 04:29 on Tuesday.)

Not valid for use on Sundays.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is allowed

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

-

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Child, National Railcard and Groupsave discounts all do apply. Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are entitled to a 50% discount. Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger. Standard railcard / Groupsave discounts apply.

RailCard.Note: Child, National Railcard and Groupsave discounts all do apply. Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are entitled to a 50% discount. Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger. Standard railcard / Groupsave discounts apply.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A Hull Trains only Off-Peak Return, only available for outward travel on Friday's and return travel Monday's. Saturday and Sunday travel is not permitted.

Validity.DayOutward: Friday only, not valid for travel on Saturday and Sunday.
Validity.DayReturn: Monday only, not valid for travel on Saturday and Sunday.
Validity.TimeOutward: Anytime
Validity.TimeReturn:
Not valid for travel on services arriving in London King's Cross between 08:30am and 10:00am.

Not valid for travel on services departing King's Cross Station between 15:00pm 17:00pm.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
Break of journey is permitted on the outward portion of an Off-Peak Return unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Overnight break of journey - if the journey cannot be completed in this time, the ticket may be used to continue the journey on the following day.

Unless otherwise indicated in the relevant restriction code, time restrictions apply as from the initial origin station on both days.

The appropriate restrictions for the actual day on which travel is being undertaken apply (for example, it may be that if day 1 is on Sunday, no restrictions apply, but on day 2, the Monday-Friday restrictions apply). All travel must be completed by 04:29 in the morning after this second day.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of journey is permitted on the return portion of an Off-Peak Return unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A Standard Class return ticket for travel on Train and Ferry which can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.

Validity.DayOutward:
The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket.

This type of ticket may have restrictions on the time of day that you can travel. These are generally in the early morning, but may also be in the early evening.
Validity.DayReturn:
The return journey must take place within one calendar month(s) of the date shown on the ticket. 

This type of ticket may have restrictions on the time of day that you can travel. These are generally in the early morning, but may also be in the early evening.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is not allowed with this ticket except for changing trains at intermediate stations. If the route shown on the ticket is prefixed with a + the ticket includes the cost of travelling across London on the London Underground, Docklands Light Railway or Thameslink services as appropriate to the journey.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: A break of journey is not allowed with this ticket except for changing trains at intermediate stations. If the route shown on the ticket is prefixed with a + the ticket includes the cost of travelling across London on the London Underground, Docklands Light Railway or Thameslink services as appropriate to the journey.

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: This ticket can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: You may change your travel plans at any time within the validity of the ticket without having to pay an additional fare. Restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket may apply to both the outward and return journeys - please check before travelling.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

A full refund is available if you return the completely unused ticket to the place of purchase within 28 days of expiry of the ticket. The amount of the refund will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): 50% discount.

Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): 50% discount.Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger. Railcard discounts: Disabled Persons Railcard: 1/3 off, Family & Friends Railcard: 1/3 off, HM Forces Railcard: No discount, Network Railcard: No discount, Senior Railcard: 1/3 offand 16-25 Railcard: 1/3 off. Other discount cards: Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: No discount and Gold Cards: No discount.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
• One-way Standard Class ticket offering the best available price for each journey.
  • Non-refundable.
  • Change of journeys, available up to the day before travel, may incur a fee (See ‘Changes’ section below).
  • Tickets must be booked in advance of travel and are subject to availability. 
  • Valid only on the specific booked train.

Luggage:

Permitted luggage onboard is 1 suitcase of max dimensions 63cm x 41cm x 27cm and a small bag which can fit under the seat or on an overhead rack or lap. See T&Cs for full details.

https://www.lumo.co.uk/plan-your-journey/onboard-luggage-policy

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: N/A
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: N/A

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Customers may not start, break and resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Not applicable. LumoFixed are single tickets only.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Where an excess fare has been paid up to the price of a walk-up ticket, the excess fare paid varies the terms under which the passenger can travel, but it does not alter the refund terms of the original LumoFixed ticket. If the passenger subsequently asks for a refund on the combination of LumoFixed ticket, Excess Fare paid and any administration fees paid, the LumoFixed ticket and change of journey fee remain non-refundable and a refund is only payable on the difference in fare paid. This may be subject to a refund fee of no more than £10 if your journey is cancelled or disrupted.

If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed by more than 60 minutes, special arrangements will be made to accommodate you on another train (although a seat cannot be guaranteed). If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed, and as a result you decide not to travel, a refund will be offered on completely unused tickets, and you will not be charged an administration fee.

There may be rare occasions where a cheaper LumoFixed fare becomes available closer to the time of travel, for example if a group booking is cancelled and extra seats become available. Please note that in these circumstances LumoFixed tickets that have already been bought cannot be refunded in part or in full.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets are valid only on the date and train service(s) shown on the ticket(s). Where applicable, valid only for travel in the class and reserved seat(s) shown on the ticket(s).
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company website and selected rail appointed travel agents.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased in advance of travel, subject to availability. Generally, the earlier the ticket is booked the better the deal. Tickets are available up to 24 weeks before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged the day before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. You will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when a change is requested.

Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased. Changes can be applied until the day before travel, NOT on the day.

  • The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same.
  • The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable.
  • An administration fee of no more than £10 may be charged per ticket for each change to a journey.
  • If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded.

Please note:

Where a LumoFixed ticket has been purchased online and the ticket has either been self-printed at home or downloaded to a mobile device this may alter the rules for changing the ticket. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: LumoFixed tickets are non-refundable

Child.Note: Children aged 5 to 15 inclusive: 50% discount. Up to two children aged under 5 may travel free of charge with each fare-paying adult.

RailCard.Note:
1/3 off with the following Railcards:

  • 16-25,
  • 26-30,
  • Family & Friends,
  • Senior,
  • HM Forces,
  • Disabled Persons,
  • Two Together Railcards.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
• One-way Standard Class ticket with additional flexibility.
  • Refundable fee-free until 23:59 the day prior to departure. (See ‘Refunds’ section).
  • Fee-free Change of Journey included. (See ‘Changes’ section).
  • Tickets must be booked in advance of travel and are subject to availability. 
  • Valid only on the specific booked train.

Luggage:

Permitted luggage onboard is 1 suitcase of max dimensions 63cm x 41cm x 27cm and a small bag which can fit under the seat or on an overhead rack or lap. See T&Cs for full details.

https://www.lumo.co.uk/plan-your-journey/onboard-luggage-policy

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: N/A
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: N/A

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Customers may not start, break and resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Not applicable. LumoFlex are single tickets only.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Where an excess fare has been paid up to the price of a walk-up ticket, the excess fare paid varies the terms under which the passenger can travel, but it does not alter the refund terms of the original LumoFlex ticket. If the passenger subsequently asks for a refund on the combination of LumoFlex ticket, Excess Fare paid and any administration fees paid, the LumoFlex ticket and change of journey fee become non-refundable and a refund is only payable on the difference in fare paid. This may be subject to a refund fee of no more than £5 if your journey is cancelled or disrupted.

If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed by more than 60 minutes, special arrangements will be made to accommodate you on another train (although a seat cannot be guaranteed). If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed, and as a result you decide not to travel, a refund will be offered on completely unused tickets, and you will not be charged an administration fee.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets are valid only on the date and train service(s) shown on the ticket(s). Where applicable, valid only for travel in the class and reserved seat(s) shown on the ticket(s).
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company website and selected rail appointed travel agents.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased in advance of travel, subject to availability. Generally, the earlier the ticket is booked the better the deal. Tickets are available up to 26 weeks before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: If you wish to change your time of travel, date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make up to 23:59 the day before you are due to travel, no admin fee will be applied. In many cases, no additional fare will be payable.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable or exchangeable up to 23:59 the day prior to travel. If you decide not to use your ticket then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales, or travel agents) before the day of travel. You will not be required to pay an administration fee.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

You will not be required to pay an administration fee.

Child.Note:
• Children aged 5 to 15 inclusive: 50% discount.
  • Up to two children aged under 5 may travel free of charge with each fare-paying adult.

RailCard.Note:
1/3 off with the following Railcards:

  • 16-25,
  • 26-30,
  • Family & Friends,
  • Senior,
  • HM Forces,
  • Disabled Persons,
  • Two Together Railcards.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
The Luton Airport DUO Return is available on selected journeys to and from Luton Airport and gives a 25% discount* on the train journey between your station and Luton Airport Parkway for two Adults travelling together at Off-Peak times, compared to the price of two Standard Class Off-Peak Return tickets for the same journey.

Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are only valid in Standard Class and when both people travel together, they're not valid if you travel on different services.

The times when you can use your Luton Airport DUO Return ticket will depend on the journey you're making and you will be advised when buying your ticket.

The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are not available for travel in First Class.

* The 25% discount applies to the rail journey to and from Luton Airport Parkway railway station, there is no discount on the price of the journey on the Luton DART between Luton Airport Parkway railway station and Luton Airport.

Validity.DayOutward:
Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are valid for outward travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.

If you cannot complete your journey on the same day you will be allowed to finish it the following day - see 'Break of journey' below.

Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are only valid when both people travel together, they're not valid if you travel on different services.
Validity.DayReturn:
Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are valid for return travel within one calendar month from the start date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 in the morning after last day of validity.

Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are only valid when both people travel together, they're not valid if you travel on different services.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Luton Airport DUO Return ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Luton Airport DUO Return ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Overnight break of journey - if the journey cannot be completed on the same day, the ticket may be used to continue the journey on the following day.

Unless otherwise indicated in the relevant restriction code, time restrictions apply as from the initial origin station on both days.

The appropriate restrictions for the actual day on which travel is being undertaken apply (for example, it may be that if day 1 is on Sunday, no restrictions apply, but on day 2, the Monday-Friday restrictions apply). All travel must be completed by 04:29 in the morning after this second day.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of journey is permitted on the return portion off an Luton Airport DUO Return unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The Luton Airport DUO Return is available on selected journeys to and from Luton Airport and gives a 25% discount* for two Adults travelling together at Off-Peak times, compared to the price of two Off-Peak Return tickets for the same journey.

Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are only valid in Standard Class and when both people travel together, they're not valid if you travel on different services.

Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are not available for travel in First Class.

The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

Luton Airport DUO Return tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Luton Airport DUO Return ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.

* The 25% discount applies to the rail journey to and from Luton Airport Parkway railway station, there is no discount on the price of the journey on the Luton DART between Luton Airport Parkway railway station and Luton Airport.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability:
Luton Airport DUO Return tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel. The outward part of the ticket is valid on date shown on ticket. Return travel within one calendar month.

There are no child fares or discounts available for Railcard holders, Annual Gold Card holders or holders of other discount cards.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Luton Airport DUO Return tickets can be sold at Ticket Offices and Travel Centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the Luton Airport DUO Return ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make.

In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your Luton Airport DUO Return ticket is refundable.

If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
There are no child discounts available with these tickets.

But up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

Plus on journeys where the Thameslink Kids for £2 ticket offer is available, up to 4 children travelling with each Luton Airport DUO Return ticket holder can travel for £2 each.

RailCard.Note: There are no additional discounts for Railcard holders on Luton Airport DUO Return tickets.

Group.Note: There are no additional discounts for larger groups on Luton Airport DUO Return tickets.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Zonal Season Ticket for trains throughout Merseyside and beyond.

Railpass is a season ticket for regular travel on trains within Merseyside (and beyond towards Chester, Ellesmere Port and Ormskirk). The area is split into a number of zones and areas, so a Railpass is sold for the specific zones or areas through which the regular journey is taken.

Railpass is valid for travel at any time of the day and on any day of the week for an unlimited number of journeys within the zones and expiry date on the ticket.

Railpass tickets are only valid with a photocard.

Valid on all TOCs within the zone/area.

MRW - 7 day

MRM - 1 month

MRY - Annual

(only the above periods of availability are offered)

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid at any time within the zones and the expiry date of the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid at any time within the zones and the expiry date of the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Station ticket offices in the Merseyrail area, also Chester, Hooton, Ellesmere Port and Ormskirk stations.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: A specific RailPass Photocard is required.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refer to the issuing office or Merseyrail Customer Relations.

Child.Note:
Children aged 5-15 receive the standard 50% reduction on weekly tickets only.

Teenagers aged 16 or 17 in full time education are entitled to purchase a child-rate weekly Railpass. This needs to be verified before issuing the ticket by having the educational establishment stamping and certifying the Railpass application form. The child rate age concession for 16 and 17 year olds only applies to Merseytravel Railpass season tickets and not for any other rail season tickets.

RailCard.Note: Child discounts.

Group.Note: Child discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Carnet product of 12 Identical point-to-point single journeys for travel in either direction. Each ticket must be activated to make it valid for travel.

Validity.DayOutward: Multiflex has an overall validity for three calendar months from the start date of the product. Each of the twelve individual journeys can be made at any time during the validity period. Each ticket can only be activated once.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid for travel on any day without time restriction.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is not allowed but can be used for alighting short.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Travel on the services of all Train Companies is subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Available for purchase via the Transport for Wales Rail Services app and mobile web.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Needs to be purchased prior to travel and activated prior to making the journey.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Change of validity period is not permitted.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Multiflex tickets are non-refundable.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Rail Rover for unlimited travel (subject to time restrictions shown) within the North Country area of validity.

Available for flexible travel - 4 days within 8.

Further information can be found on Northern website.

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid for 4 days out of 8.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid for 4 days out of 8.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid on trains timed to depart after 08:45 Mondays to Fridays and at any time weekends and bank holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid on trains timed to depart after 08:45 Mondays to Fridays and at any time weekends and bank holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Rover tickets may not be issued more than 3 days in advance of their validity (5 days for telesales and internet bookings).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are only given on Rail Rover tickets returned before the start date shown on them.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 50% Child discounts.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with Two Together, Disabled Persons Railcard and Senior Railcard.

Group.Note: Family ticket available which is valid for up to four people travelling together. Maximum two adults. Two adults can travel without a child.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: North Downs Day Ranger

For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. Valid any time Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn:

Valid any time Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: The ticket has unlimited travel on the day(s) of validity but you cannot change the travel validity period of the ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

A day ranger ticket, allowing unlimited travel in the ranger area, in particular to allow a circular journey that takes in the Settle & Carlisle railway.

Validity.TimeReturn:

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Non-refundable ticket, only valid for the date, time and trains specified.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Subject to availability.

If you change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded. Not possible to change the origin or destination.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on the date printed on the ticket. There are no time-of-day restrictions.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid on the date printed on the ticket. There are no time-of-day restrictions.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.
