TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Season Ticket for the 'network' (Network West Midlands PTE area).

ntrain is a Season Ticket which allows unlimited rail travel (only) in the zone or zones in which it is valid. Tickets can be bought in the following combination of zones:

Zone 1

Zone 1 & 2

Zone 1, 2 & 3

Zone 1, 2, 3 & 4

Zone 1, 2, 3, 4, & 5 [all zones]

Zone 2, 3, 4 & 5

Tickets that cover Zone 5 can also be used beyond Bloxwich North to all stations to Rugeley Trent Valley.

Tickets can be bought for periods 7 days, 4 weeks, 1 month and then any period up to one year.

nTrain Season Tickets are not valid on Metro or bus services.

Full details of the full range of tickets, periods of validity, zones and maps can be found on the Network West Midlands website.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day within the ticket's validity.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day within the ticket's validity.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid any time within the zones stated on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid any time within the zones stated on the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Available from the 'N-network' Travel Shop at Birmingham New Street station as well as at any staffed station in the Network West Midlands area.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Photocard required for purchase of Season Tickets.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
You can apply for a refund on a nTrain Season Ticket valid for seven days or more as long as your ticket still has at least seven days validity remaining or three days for seven day season tickets.

The outstanding validity of the ticket will be refunded and is calculated from the date we receive the ticket less a £10 administration fee.

To get a refund please follow the instructions below. Please note you will need to supply your original ticket not a copy:

- For tickets bought at the station you can apply for a refund at your local station

- For tickets bought online, log on to your online account fill in the relevant form

- For tickets bought online at www.networkwestmidlands.com or via the Corporate Sales Scheme can write to: Direct Sales, Centro House, 16 Summer Lane, Birmingham B19 3SD.

- For tickets bought through the Direct Debit Scheme refunds are not available, you must give at least one months notice by telephone, email or in writing of your intention to cease Direct Debit payments.  For further details call 0121 214 7550.

Child.Note: Child version available for all zones and only for the periods of 1 week, 1 month or 1 term.

RailCard.Note: Child version available for all zones.

Group.Note: Child version available for all zones.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Off Peak Day Return (South Western Railway)
TicketTypeDescription.Description: Off Peak Day Return.

Validity.DayOutward: This promotional ticket is valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.DayReturn: This promotional ticket is valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The South Western Railway Journey Planner will automatically work out which journeys are valid for your ticket. 

The times when you can use this ticket is indicated by the Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha numeric) code. It is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].
Validity.TimeReturn:
The South Western Railway Journey Planner will automatically work out which journeys are valid for your ticket. 

The times when you can use this ticket is indicated by the Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha numeric) code. It is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is permitted on the outward portion of this ticket.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: A break of journey is permitted on the return portion of this ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

This ticket may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Online, through the South Western Railway website or app, and other online retailers. Also available at ticket offices.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

RailCard.Note:
1/3 off with the following Railcards:

  • Disabled Persons Railcard
  • Veterans Railcard
  • Family & Friends Railcard
  • HM Forces Railcard
  • Senior Railcard
  • Two Together Railcard
  • 16-25 Railcard
  • 26-30 Railcard

50% discount for:

  • 16-17 Saver

See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Please note Flexi Season tickets have replaced our Peak (Anytime) Carnet tickets.

Off-Peak Carnet tickets offer a 10% discount on Standard Class travel, compared to the price of Off-Peak Day Return fares between the following stations and London:

Cambridge* - Royston* - Huntingdon* - St Neots* - Welwyn Garden City* - Hatfield* - Potters Bar* - Hertford North* - Bedford** - Luton Airport Parkway** - Harpenden** - St Albans City**

You can buy a Off-Peak Carnet book of either 5 or 10 single journey tickets.

Your Off-Peak Carnet tickets will need to be used within three months.

Off-Peak Carnet tickets are one-way, so you will need a separate book of Off-Peak Carnet tickets for your return journey.

You cannot combine your 10% discount with Railcard or GroupSave discounts.

No First Class, Off-Peak Carnet tickets are available. 

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*From these stations travel is available to / from the following London stations:

London Kings Cross - London St Pancras International - Old Street - Moorgate

**From these stations travel is available to / from the following London stations:

London St Pancras - Farringdon - City Thameslink - London Blackfriars - London Bridge.

Validity.DayOutward: The tickets are valid for 3 months from the date of issue.
Validity.DayReturn: n/a
Validity.TimeOutward:
The following weekday time restrictions apply (there are no time restrictions on weekends or Bank Holidays):

Journeys towards London -

  • These tickets can be used on trains arriving into London after 10:00

Journeys leaving London -

  • These tickets can be used on trains departing London after 09:30.
  • Tickets to the following destinations cannot be used on trains leaving London between 16:30 and 19:00:

Cambridge* - Royston* - Huntingdon* - St Neots* - Bedford** - Luton Airport Parkway** - Harpenden** - St Albans City**

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Tickets are valid for three months from the date of issue. 

​Passengers must validate the ticket using permanent ink before commencing their journey.

Passengers must use the ticket in the direction of their journey stated on the ticket.

Passengers can break their journey. 

Attempted fraudulent use will result in withdrawal of this ticket, payment of full fare for the journey and a possible criminal charge.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
You can buy Off-Peak Carnet tickets at Thameslink and Great Northern stations or they can be purchased online from either the Great Northern or Thameslink websites. If you are ordering online, you will need to select a single adult ticket and the Off-Peak Carnet options will be displayed.

COP - Carnet Off-Peak

CO5 - Carnet Off-Peak 5

0BJ - Smart Carnet Off-Peak

0BK - Smart Carnet Off-Peak 5
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: No changes.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Only a complete set of either 5 or 10 unused Carnet tickets (consecutively numbered) will be considered for a refund.

​Lost or stolen individual tickets will not be replaced.

Child.Note: Child discounts but no Railcard discounts.

RailCard.Note: Child discounts but no Railcard discounts.

Group.Note: Child discounts but no Railcard discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Off-Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may need to travel at specific times of the day, days of the week and sometimes on specific routes or operators.

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket will depend on the journey you are making and you will be advised when buying your ticket. The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

Validity.DayOutward: Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.DayReturn: Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

Off-Peak Day tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Victoria and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel. You can reserve a seat on many longer distance services. Reservations are recommended on busy services. If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable. If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see below).
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, change the date of travel, or change the time you are allowed to travel.

Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey.

NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) 50% discount. Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

Railcard discounts: Two Together, 16-25 Railcard: 1/3 off, Disabled Persons Railcard: 1/3 off, Family & Friends Railcard: 1/3 off, HM Forces Railcard: 1/3 off, Network Railcard: 1/3 off and Senior Railcard: 1/3 off on Standard. Other discount cards: Gold Cards: 1/3 off and Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: 50% discount. NOTE: Minimum fares / time restrictions may apply to tickets bought with a Railcard or other discount card. See terms and conditions of the appropriate Railcard or discount card for details.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note:
16-25 Railcard, Family & Friends, Two Together, Disabled Persons Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Network and GoldCard all offer 1/3 off.

Minimum fares / time restrictions may apply to tickets bought with a Railcard or other discount card. See terms and conditions of the appropriate Railcard or discount card for details.

Railcard holders travelling on a discounted ticket must carry their Railcard when they travel. If a Railcard holder fails to produce their valid Railcard with their ticket, they will be required to pay a full priced ticket for their journey as if no Railcard and/or no ticket were held.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
An Off-Peak Family Travelcard allows a minimum of 1 Adult and 1 Child (5-15 years old inclusive) return travel to London and enjoy unlimited travel throughout London on National Rail, London Underground, DLR, London Trams, and London Bus services within Zones 1-6.

All group members must travel together at all times.

Validity.DayOutward: Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

Eligibility

Off-Peak Family Travelcard is valid only when at least 1 Adult (16+) and 1 Child (5-15 years) is travelling together. Maximum group size is 2 Adults and 4 Children

Time Restrictions

Off-Peak Day tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. If you board a train at a time when your Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

More Information

For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Off-Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may need to travel at specific times of the day, days of the week and sometimes on specific routes or operators.

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket will depend on the journey you are making and you will be advised when buying your ticket. The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

Validity.DayOutward:
Off-Peak Return tickets are valid for outward travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.

If you cannot complete your journey on the same day you will be allowed to finish it the following day - see 'Break of journey' below.
Validity.DayReturn: Off-Peak Return tickets are valid for return travel within one calendar month from the start date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 in the morning after last day of validity.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Overnight break of journey - if the journey cannot be completed on the same day, the ticket may be used to continue the journey on the following day.

Unless otherwise indicated in the relevant restriction code, time restrictions apply as from the initial origin station on both days.

The appropriate restrictions for the actual day on which travel is being undertaken apply (for example, it may be that if day 1 is on Sunday, no restrictions apply, but on day 2, the Monday-Friday restrictions apply). All travel must be completed by 04:29 in the morning after this second day.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of journey is permitted on the return portion off an Off-Peak Return unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

Off-Peak tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel. Valid on date shown on ticket. Return travel within one calendar month
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, change the date of travel, or change the time you are allowed to travel.

Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey.

NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day, as dated on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Return valid any day within two calendar months of the date on the ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Outward travel valid at any time on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day.
Validity.TimeReturn: Return travel valid at any time until 04:29 the following day after the last day of validity.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
One-Ticket is a travel scheme offering you unlimited travel on buses and rail in East Central Scotland, centered around the Edinburgh area.

One-Ticket is valid within the zone where it is issued plus all other zones of a lower number, (eg. Travelarea4 = 4 + 3 + 2 + 1a + 1).

One-Ticket is valid for 1 week (OTW), 1 month (OTM) and 1 year (OTA).

For more information, visit One-Ticket's own website.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day within the ticket's validity.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day within the ticket's validity.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid any time within the ticket's validity.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid any time within the ticket's validity.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: For more details see www.one-ticket.co.uk
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Staffed National Rail stations and bus stations within the validity area. Also from PayPoint outlets and online at the One-Ticket website.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: If buying a 7-day or longer Season Ticket for the first time, a recent passport-type photograph for the accompanying photocard is required.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: One-Tickets are non refundable and non transferable.

Child.Note: No further discounts

RailCard.Note: No further discounts

Group.Note: No further discounts

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Passport to Freedom in Cornwall (3 days in 7)
TicketTypeDescription.Description: Flexi Rover for train travel in Cornwall

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid on trains after 0900 Monday-Friday and all day Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays. Valid on buses at any time.

Allows travel on the main line between Plymouth and Penzance and branch lines off it.

Can be used on all main bus routes of First Western National, Truronian, Western Greyhound, Hopleys and Sunset Coaches and can be used to reach many interesting spots including Lands End, The Eden Project, Lizard Point, Perranporth, Boscastle and Bude.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid on trains after 0900 Monday-Friday and all day Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays. Valid on buses at any time.

Allows travel on the main line between Plymouth and Penzance and branch lines off it.

Can be used on all main bus routes of First Western National, Truronian, Western Greyhound, Hopleys and Sunset Coaches and can be used to reach many interesting spots including Lands End, The Eden Project, Lizard Point, Perranporth, Boscastle and Bude.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Yes

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No reservations are needed.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refunds are only given on Rail Rover tickets returned before the start date shown on them and are subject to an administration charge.

Child.Note: Discounts for children and holders of Disabled Adult Railcards, Disabled Child Railcards, Senior Railcards and 16-25 Railcards.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Off-peak contactless and Oyster fares are pay as you go ticket types that are charged if travel only involves off-peak times.

Validity.DayOutward: Touch in and out on the day of travel.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: No break of journey allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Daily and Monday to Sunday capping may apply. Only touch 1 card or device on the reader. 1 card per person. Standard Class only.

For pay as you go with Oyster, Railcards do apply, except Two Together, 16-17 Saver, Family and Friends and Network Railcards.

For pay as you go with contactless, use the same card or device to touch in and out. Railcards and other discounts do not apply.

Find out more at nationalrail.co.uk/payasyougo
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: No ticket purchase necessary. Touch card or device on reader at start and end of journey. Railcards and discounts do not always apply. Daily and Monday to Sunday capping available.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Touch Oyster card, contactless card or device on yellow card reader at validator or gate.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Credit on Oyster card required. No pre-booking requirement for contactless.

Child.Note: Child discounts are not currently available on pay as you go with contactless, however there are a range of discounts available for children and young people available on Oyster.

RailCard.Note: Selected Railcards only. Visit www.railcard.co.uk for details

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Peak contactless and Oyster fares are pay as you go ticket types that are charged if travel involves peak times.

Validity.DayOutward: Touch in and out on the day of travel.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: No break of journey allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Daily and Monday to Sunday capping may apply. Only touch 1 card or device on the reader. 1 card per person. Standard Class only.

For pay as you go with Oyster, Railcards do apply, except Two Together, 16-17 Saver, Family and Friends and Network Railcards.

For pay as you go with contactless, use the same card or device to touch in and out. Railcards and other discounts do not apply.

Find out more at nationalrail.co.uk/payasyougo
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: No ticket purchase necessary. Touch card or device on reader at start and end of journey. Railcards and discounts do not always apply. Daily and Monday to Sunday capping available.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Touch Oyster card, contactless card or device on yellow card reader at validator or gate.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Credit on Oyster card required. No pre-booking requirement for contactless.

Child.Note: Child discounts are not currently available on pay as you go with contactless, however there are a range of discounts available for children and young people available on Oyster.

RailCard.Note: Selected Railcards only. Visit www.railcard.co.uk for details

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Pay as you go with contactless (Off-Peak)
TicketTypeDescription.Description: Off-peak contactless fares are a pay as you go ticket type that are charged if travel only involves off-peak times.

Validity.DayOutward: Touch in and out with card or device on the day of travel.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: No break of journey allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Daily and Monday to Sunday capping may apply. Only touch 1 card or device on the reader. 1 card per person. Standard Class only. Use the same card or device to touch in and out. Railcards and other discounts do not apply.

Find out more at www.nationalrail.co.uk/contactless
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: No ticket purchase necessary. Touch card or device on reader at start and end of journey. Adult fares only. Railcards and discounts do not apply. Daily and Monday to Sunday capping available.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Not needed. Touch contactless card or device on yellow card reader at validator or gate.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: If you choose not to travel (tap in and out at the same station), or have been charged the maximum fare, you may be eligible for a full or partial refund. Contact TfL Customer Services or visit the TfL website for more information.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Pay as you go with contactless (Peak)
TicketTypeDescription.Description: Peak contactless fares are a pay as you go ticket type that are charged if travel involves peak times.

Validity.DayOutward: Touch in and out with card or device on the day of travel.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: No break of journey allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Daily and Monday to Sunday capping may apply. Only touch 1 card or device on the reader. 1 card per person. Standard Class only. Use the same card or device to touch in and out. Railcards and other discounts do not apply.

Find out more at www.nationalrail.co.uk/contactless
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: No ticket purchase necessary. Touch card or device on reader at start and end of journey. Adult fares only. Railcards and discounts do not apply. Daily and Monday to Sunday capping available.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Not needed. Touch contactless card or device on yellow card reader at validator or gate.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: If you choose not to travel (tap in and out at the same station), or have been charged the maximum fare, you may be eligible for a full or partial refund. Contact TfL Customer Services or visit the TfL website for more information.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
If customers are not travelling by train but wish to access the station platform - for example where there are ticket barriers in operation and they wish to assist someone onto the train or if they are a railway enthusiast - they may need to buy a Platform Ticket, depending on the discretion of staff.

Not all train companies still issue Platform Tickets but where they are still sold they cost ten pence (10p) and can only be purchased from the station ticket office. There is no charge to assist a disabled passenger to a train.

At other stations without barriers, unstaffed stations or at stations managed by certain train companies a Platform Ticket may not be required and customers are advised to check with the ticket office, where possible, before entering the platform. Each train company will advise their retail staff of their policy on Platform Tickets.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid for one hour from time of issue or until otherwise specified by the issuer.
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Not applicable.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Valid only at the station where it was issued.

Not valid on trains.

Not valid when asked to leave the platform for whatever reason by a member of staff.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available to purchase at any time, where these are still issued.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Train company ticket offices, where they are still issued.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Not applicable.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Not refundable.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
PlusBus is a discount price bus pass (like a Travelcard) for unlimited bus and tram travel around the whole urban area of the town that's at the start and/or end of a rail journey.

PlusBus is available about 300 towns & cities in Britain as a Day ticket or as a Season Ticket for fixed periods, as follows:

PBD - PlusBus Day [PLUSBUS DAY]

PB7 - 7 Day Season [PLUSBUS 7-DAY]

BMS - 1-month Season [BUS SEASN 1MTH]

BQS - 3-month Season [BUS SEASN 3MTH]

BAS - Annual Season [BUS ANNUAL SEAS]

Validity.DayOutward:
PlusBus Day or Season Tickets can be used for unlimited journeys within the defined area for each town and on participating bus operators.

PlusBus is an add-on to a rail ticket and does alter the conditions or any restrictions applicable to the rail portion of the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn:
PlusBus Day or Season Tickets can be used for unlimited journeys within the defined area for each town and on participating bus operators.

PlusBus is an add-on to a rail ticket and does alter the conditions or any restrictions applicable to the rail portion of the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: To check PlusBus ticket prices, participating bus and tram operators & view town travel zone maps visit: www.plusbus.info
TicketTypeDescription.Availability:
PlusBus can be purchased in advance or on the day.

It is usually bought at the same time as a rail ticket but can be issued to a customer who already has a valid

PlusBus can be issued with all “advance”& “walk-up” train fares, as follows:

  • Single rail ticket = issue a PlusBus Day ticket for origin &/or destination town
  • Day Return rail ticket = issue a PlusBus Day ticket for origin &/or destination town
  • Period Return rail ticket = issue 2x PlusBus Day tickets for origin &/or destination town, one for the outward date and a second for the return date. If the return date is not known, you can only issue a PlusBus Day ticket for the customers outward journey.
  • Season Tickets = issue the relevant period PlusBus Season Ticket - 7-day, I month, 3 months or Annual.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
PlusBus can be issued at all ticket offices and by some telesales.

A PlusBus ticket must not be issued if both the origin and destination stations of the rail journey are located wholly within the same towns' PlusBus travel zone or within the same metropolitan region (e.g. Greater Manchester; South Yorkshire; West Midlands; West Yorkshire).
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: National Rail conditions apply. Changes or refunds are only available at the issuing ticket office.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: National Rail refund conditions apply. Refunds are only available from original retailer. Replacement & duplicate PlusBus Season Tickets (Monthly, Quarterly or Annual) can be issued, in accordance with the normal National Rail conditions.

Child.Note:
Children pay half the adult PlusBus Day ticket fare to the nearest 5p above.

Some destinations also offer child Season Tickets at half the adult fare. The standard child discount that applies to rail season tickets must not be applied to child rate PlusBus season tickets. Specific prices are loaded into the fares system for child rate PlusBus season tickets and these prices must be used. In some cases these prices will be the same as the adult season ticket price where there is no discount for child seasons.

RailCard.Note:
National Railcard holders get one-third off adult PlusBus day ticket prices. Some Railcards have restrictions, please see individual entries below.

Minimum fare restrictions do not apply to the sale of discount price PlusBus day tickets (even if the minimum fare does apply to the rail ticket).

Network Railcard (& Gold Card) holders get one-third off the adult PlusBus day ticket price upon presentation of their valid Network Railcard:

  • after 10:00 Monday to Friday;
  • anytime on Saturday, Sunday & Public Holidays.

The £13 minimum fare restriction does not apply.

16-25 Railcard, Two Together & HM Forces Railcard holders get one-third off the adult PlusBus Day ticket price upon presentation of their valid Railcard at all times.

The £12 minimum fare restriction does not apply.

Senior Railcard holders get one-third off the adult PlusBus day ticket price upon presentation of their valid Senior Railcard:

  • at all times outside the South East of England;
  • after the morning peak* period Monday to Friday (excluding Public Holidays) for a journey wholly within the South East of England.

* times of off-peak services vary by route, but are normally related to the availability of Off-Peak tickets.

Family & Friends Railcard adult travellers get one-third off the adult PlusBus day ticket price upon presentation of their valid Family & Friends Railcard:

  • at any time for a journey outside the South East of England;
  • after the morning peak period* Monday to Friday (excludes Public Holidays) for a journey wholly within the South East of England.

* times of off-peak services vary by route, but are normally related to the availability of Off-Peak tickets.

Children travelling as part of a Friends & Family Group must be charged the normal child PlusBus fare for the destination (and not £1).

Disabled Persons Railcard holders get one-third off the adult PlusBus day ticket price upon presentation of their valid Disabled Persons Railcard available at all times, when purchased with the following ticket types:

  • Off-Peak Day Singles & Off-Peak Day Returns.
  • Anytime Singles & Anytime Returns.
  • Advance fares.

Disabled Persons Railcard discounts are valid during the morning peak hours.

There is no Railcard discount off the price of PlusBus season ticket prices.

No other type of Railcard gives a discount off PlusBus day ticket prices.

Holders of Staff Travel/Privilege Passes can buy PlusBus, but must not get a discount.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Corporate Standard Premium fare

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on date on ticket

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available within 72 hrs up to departure
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: TMCs
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Yes
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Non-refundable

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Corporate Standard Premium fare

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on date on ticket

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available up to 72 hrs before departure
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: TMCs
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Yes
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refundable

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Southeastern Railway Promotional Day Return

Validity.TimeOutward:
Valid for departure after 09.29 Mon-Fri

Valid all day weekends
Validity.TimeReturn:
Valid for departure after 09.29 Mon-Fri

Valid all day weekends

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Available for sale at all Southeastern staffed railway stations and at https://www.southeasternrailway.co.uk/
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Changes to travel are allowed, subject to purchase of the correct excess fare and any administration fee that may be applicable.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are allowed before travel, subject to the normal administration fee.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Child discount of 50%. Kids for a Quid tickets ARE valid with this promotional offer.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Rail Air Return tickets are fully flexible walk-up tickets with no restrictions on when you can travel.

Validity.DayOutward: Rail Air Return tickets are valid for outward travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day.
Validity.DayReturn: Rail Air Return tickets are valid for return travel within one calendar month from the start date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 after last day of validity.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid to travel at any time of the day.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid to travel at any time of the day.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
• The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.
  • Off-Peak tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  
  • If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.
  • All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Only available to customers booking ticket between the Channel Islands and Southampton Airport through FlyBe/Blue Islands. Tickets must be purchased online and are not available at stations or self-service ticket machines.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Special fares for customers of certain airlines flying to Southampton International Airport.

Validity.DayOutward: Rail Air tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid at any time.
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is permitted

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Rail Air tickets can be used at any time but may require you to travel via a specific route or on a specific train company and this will be clearly stated on the ticket.

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Only available to customers booking flights to Southampton Airport through specific airlines Tickets must be purchased online and are not available at stations or self-service ticket machines.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Multi-journey bus and train ticket for travel in Cornwall.

Travel on all trains and most bus services throughout Cornwall using just one ticket. The area of validity covers all of Cornwall which includes the lines to Penzance, St Ives, Falmouth, Looe, Newquay and Gunnislake plus it is also valid as far east as Plymouth in Devon.

The Ride Cornwall ticket also enables visits to the Eden Project near St Austell, Land's End or the National Maritime Museum in Falmouth (admission tickets must be purchased separately).

A family version is also available for two adults and up to three children.

Further information can be found on GWR.com

RCW - Ride Cornwall Day RangerRCF - Ride Cornwall Day Ranger (Family)

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid for one day on the date shown on the ticket.

Except on Sleeper services, this ticket is valid for travel until 04:29 of the morning immediately following the expiry date shown on the ticket.

All travel must be completed by this time.

Sleeper Services towards London

Only valid on the date shown on the ticket (and only within the area covered by the ticket)

Sleeper Services towards Penzance (Saturday Morning Arrivals only)

For Sleeper services arriving on a Saturday morning only, this ticket must be valid on the date of arrival at your destination but need not be valid on the date of departure of the Sleeper service. The date of arrival is the first date of validity of the ticket. (Only valid within the area covered by the ticket.)
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid for one day on the date shown on the ticket.

Except on Sleeper services, this ticket is valid for travel until 04:29 of the morning immediately following the expiry date shown on the ticket.

All travel must be completed by this time.

Sleeper Services towards London

Only valid on the date shown on the ticket (and only within the area covered by the ticket)

Sleeper Services towards Penzance (Saturday Morning Arrivals only)

For Sleeper services arriving on a Saturday morning only, this ticket must be valid on the date of arrival at your destination but need not be valid on the date of departure of the Sleeper service. The date of arrival is the first date of validity of the ticket. (Only valid within the area covered by the ticket.)
Validity.TimeOutward:
AREA

All rail services and most buses in the county of Cornwall, including to and from Plymouth.

Only available within the area shown on the map.

When is my ticket valid?

RAIL - Valid from 08:45 on weekdays*

*Also valid on the following trains:

  • On all services on the St Ives branch line
  • 08:28 Plymouth to Gunnislake (any journey on this train)
  • 08:09 service from Plymouth to Penzance (valid from Plymouth and all stations west of Plymouth)
  • 08:16 service from Liskeard to Looe (any journey on this train)

Note: when train times change, Ride Cornwall becomes valid on the equivalent service.

*Easements are in place over Christmas and New Year. Check back for exact dates.

Valid only on services of the following Train Operating Companies, within the area shown on the map below:

  • CrossCountry
  • Great Western Railways

BUS

Available all day from Monday to Sunday, including bank holidays.

Available on all bus routes operated by Go Cornwall.

Key destinations within the area of validity include Lands End, The Eden Project, Lizard Point, Perranporth, Boscastle and Bude. Use of buses to/from Plymouth is restricted to direct bus services to/from Cornwall only, for journeys originating or terminating in Cornwall (i.e. not valid for local journeys within Plymouth).
Validity.TimeReturn:
AREA

All rail services and most buses in the county of Cornwall, including to and from Plymouth.

Only available within the area shown on the map.

When is my ticket valid?

RAIL - Valid from 08:45 on weekdays*

*Also valid on the following trains:

  • On all services on the St Ives branch line
  • 08:28 Plymouth to Gunnislake (any journey on this train)
  • 08:09 service from Plymouth to Penzance (valid from Plymouth and all stations west of Plymouth)
  • 08:16 service from Liskeard to Looe (any journey on this train)

Note: when train times change, Ride Cornwall becomes valid on the equivalent service.

*Easements are in place over Christmas and New Year. Check back for exact dates.

Valid only on services of the following Train Operating Companies, within the area shown on the map below:

  • CrossCountry
  • Great Western Railways

BUS

Available all day from Monday to Sunday, including bank holidays.

Available on all bus routes operated by Go Cornwall.

Key destinations within the area of validity include Lands End, The Eden Project, Lizard Point, Perranporth, Boscastle and Bude. Use of buses to/from Plymouth is restricted to direct bus services to/from Cornwall only, for journeys originating or terminating in Cornwall (i.e. not valid for local journeys within Plymouth).

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.

Can also be purchased on board buses within the Cornwall area.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines:
No reservations are needed.

Rover and Ranger tickets may not be issued more than 3 days in advance of their validity (5 days for telesales and internet bookings).
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: The ticket has unlimited travel but you cannot change the travel validity period of travel passes.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are only available on unused tickets surrendered before the day of travel and may be subject to an administration fee.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Approximately 25% discount.

RailCard.Note: No Railcard discounts for Ride Cornwall

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Advance tickets are single (one-way) tickets for selected journeys available in First Class and Standard Class, offering the best available price for each journey.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
No break of journey is allowed.

Customers may not start, break and resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets are valid ONLY on the date and train service(s) shown on the ticket(s). Where applicable, you must travel in the class and reserved seat(s) shown on the ticket(s).
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel agents.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased in advance of travel, subject to availability. Book as far in advance as possible to get the cheapest fares.

Child.Note: Child and Group discounts are available. Group travel must be booked in advance.

RailCard.Note: Child and Group discounts are available. Group travel must be booked in advance.

Group.Note: Child and Group discounts are available. Group travel must be booked in advance.

TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

-

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Standard Class Rail & Ferry Return ticket to the Scilly Isles

Validity.DayOutward: Valid for outward travel on the date on the ticket and by the reserved train and for return travel within 1 month of that date and by the reserved train.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid for outward travel on the date on the ticket and by the reserved train and for return travel within 1 month of that date and by the reserved train.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of train journey is not permitted.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of train journey is not permitted.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available for journeys from selected Great Western Railway stations to the Isles of Scilly.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Only available from Great Western Railway telesales.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Must be purchased at least 7 days before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Changes to booked ferry crossing must be agreed with Great Western Railway telesales before the starting.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Wholly unused tickets are refunded. An administration charge is deducted. to the rail portion of the ticket only. The ferry portion is only refunded if ferry reservations are cancelled with FGW telesales before the ferry leaves.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: No further discounts.

RailCard.Note: No further discounts.

Group.Note: No further discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Standard Class Rail & Helicopter Short Stay Return ticket to the Scilly Isles

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid for a maximum of three nights' stay on the Scilly Isles.

The rail portion of the ticket is valid for 1 month but the outward journey must be on the date on the ticket. Outward leg must be on the reserved train.
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid for a maximum of three nights' stay on the Scilly Isles.

The rail portion of the ticket is valid for 1 month and the return journey must be within 1 month of the date on the date on the ticket . Return leg must be on the reserved train.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of train journey is not permitted.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of train journey is not permitted.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available for journeys from selected Great Western Railway stations to the Isles of Scilly.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Only available from Great Western Railway telesales.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Must be bought at least 7 days before travel. Reservations for outward and return journeys must be made at the time of purchase.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Changes to the booked helicopter flight must be agreed with FGW telesales before the start of the journey.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Wholly unused tickets are refunded. An administration charge is deducted. to the rail portion of the ticket only. The helicopter portion is only refunded if helicopter reservations are cancelled with Great Western Railway telesales before the helicopter leaves.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

The helicopter portion is only refunded if helicopter reservations are cancelled with Great Western Railway telesales before the helicopter leaves.

Child.Note: No further discounts.

RailCard.Note: No further discounts.

Group.Note: No further discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Standard Class single rail travel by London North Eastern Railway services plus an upgrade to First Class, subject to availability.

One free seat reservation.

Complimentary food and drink in First Class, according to the day, time of day and journey. Subject to availability.

Complimentary access to London North Eastern Railway First Class lounges.

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid only on date of ticket.

Valid on London North Eastern Railway services only.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: You may not start, break and resume, or end your journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: This ticket is a hybrid ticket aimed mainly at business travellers. It is issued as a Standard Class ticket (as many Corporate travel policies filter out First Class travel) but contains an upgrade to First Class, to be used with the ticket. Reservations are booked into First Class.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased by 23:59 the day before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
You may change your travel plans at any time on the date shown on your ticket without having to pay an additional fare. There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.

Where the date of travel is changed, the ticket should be re-issued by the retailer showing the correct date.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date. You may be required to pay an administration fee. The refund amount will take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Standard Class return rail travel by London North Eastern Railway and connecting services, plus an upgrade to First Class, subject to availability.

Complimentary food and drink in First Class, according to the day, time of day and journey. Subject to availability.

Mandatory reservation required on the outward journey,  however the return leg can be flexible (to within one calendar month, if required), though reservations are recommended as the First Class upgrade is only guaranteed with a valid seat reservation on a chosen service. 

Complimentary access to London North Eastern Railway First Class lounges.

For journeys originating in the Scotland (heading to London), an Underground return ticket included to Zones 1 and 2.

Validity.DayOutward: You must be at the departure station shown on the ticket in good time to catch the train.If you miss the first train on which you are booked for any reason, a new ticket must be purchased. If delays occur while travelling, you will be allowed to take the next available train(s) to complete your journey.
Validity.DayReturn: You must be at the departure station shown on the ticket in good time to catch the train.If you miss the first train on which you are booked for any reason, a new ticket must be purchased. If delays occur while travelling, you will be allowed to take the next available train(s) to complete your journey.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: You may not start, break and resume, or end your journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: You may not start, break and resume, or end your journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability:
This ticket is a hybrid ticket aimed mainly at business travellers.  It is issued as a Standard Class ticket (as many Corporate travel policies filter out First Class travel) but contains an upgrade to First Class, to be used with the ticket.  Reservations are booked into First Class.

Customers travelling on Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays may find cheaper tickets available.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased by 23:59 the day before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Changes to travel plan are allowed, without penalty, on or until the date of travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date. You may be required to pay an administration fee. The refund amount will take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: No further discounts apply.

RailCard.Note: No further discounts apply.

Group.Note: No further discounts apply.
