TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Season Tickets allow unlimited travel between two stations for a specified period of time. Season Tickets are usually available for periods of seven days or for any period from one month to one year.

Season Tickets offer great savings for regular travellers and where savings can usually be made even if  travelling fewer than 5 days a week. An Annual Season ticket gives offers 52 weeks' travel for the price of 40 already-discounted Weekly Season Tickets.

Validity.DayOutward: Any day and up to 04:29 after expiry date on ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Any day and up to 04:29 after expiry date on ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Any time
Validity.TimeReturn: Any time

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of Journey may not be allowed on all permitted routes - see the National Routeing Guide for full details.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of Journey may not be allowed on all permitted routes - see the National Routeing Guide for full details.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Season Ticket holders' names and addresses are recorded.  You may be contacted for survey purposes about the journeys you make with your Season Ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability:
To prevent any misuse, Season Tickets and Travelcards are not generally issued in advance, although Seasons on Smartcard and Travelcard Season on Oyster, can be issued up to 30 days in advance of the first day of validity.

To minimise inconvenience to customers, the following rules apply to Season Tickets issued on printed stock:

NEW TICKETS

(including renewing Season Tickets following a break of continuity)           

A Ticket may be issued:

7-Day Seasons &

Travelcards

  •  on the start date;
  • if the start date is a Monday (or a Tuesday after a Bank Holiday), the Ticket may be issued from 06:00 on the previous Sunday.

Period Seasons & Travelcards

(including Annual & Gold Cards)

  • if the start date is a Saturday, Sunday or Monday (also Tuesday after a Bank Holiday), the Ticket may be issued from 12:00 (noon) on the previous Friday;
  • if the start date is another day, the Ticket may be issued after 12:00 (noon) on the previous day.
  • over the Christmas and New Year holiday period these rules are generally relaxed. Please refer to local TOC instructions.

RENEWALS

Where the current ticket is SURRENDERED* with NO BREAK OF CONTINUITY

A Ticket may be issued:

7-Day Seasons & Travelcards

  • if the start date is a Sunday or Monday (or Tuesday after a Bank Holiday), the ticket may be issued from 12:00 (noon) on the previous Friday.
  • if the start date is another day, the ticket may be issued after 12:00 (noon) on the previous day.

Period Seasons & Travelcards (including Annual & Gold Cards)

  •  the Ticket may be issued any time up to seven days in advance.
  • over the Christmas and New Year holiday period these rules are generally relaxed. Please refer to local TOC instructions.

*Surrendered to retailer used for current Season Ticket
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
• Season Tickets can be bought at any staffed National Rail ticket office or on the website from the appropriate Train Company.
  • 7-Day Season Tickets can also be bought at some self-service ticket machines.
  • Season Tickets can also be bought over the telephone from some retailers.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines:
If buying a Season Ticket for the first time, a recent passport-type photograph for the accompanying photocard is required.

In addition, if buying a monthly or longer Season Ticket for the first time, an application form will need to be completed so that a Record Card can be held by the issuing Train Company.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: If the Season Ticket held does not cover the journey in full, has been left at home or is for Standard Class accommodation when travelling in First Class, the appropriate additional ticket must be purchased at the ticket office before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: For refund information and all Season Ticket terms and conditions, Click here

Child.Note:
Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): 50% discount.

Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note:
• 16-17 Saver: 50% discount
  • Job Centre Plus Travel Discount Card: 50% discount

Retailers will publicise details of any other Season Ticket discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Add-on to the Network [Network West Midlands] Season Ticket.

Add-ons can be added to Season Tickets that already cover the following combination of zones:

Zone 1

Zone 1 & 2

Zone 1, 2 & 3

Zone 1, 2, 3 & 4

Zone 1, 2, 3, 4, & 5 [all zones]

Zone 2, 3, 4 & 5

Tickets that cover Zone 5 can also be used beyond Bloxwich North to all stations to Rugeley Trent Valley.

Tickets can be bought for periods 1 week, 2 weeks, 4 weeks, 13 weeks and 52 weeks (annual). A Student variation is available for specific term-times.

Full details of the full range of tickets, periods of validity, zones and maps can be found on the Network West Midlands website.

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid any day within the ticket's validity.

Valid any time within the zones stated on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid any day within the ticket's validity.

Valid any time within the zones stated on the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
You can apply for a refund on a

Network Season Ticket valid for seven days or more as long as your ticket still has at least seven days validity remaining or three days for seven day season tickets.

The outstanding validity of the ticket will be refunded and is calculated from the date we receive the ticket less a £10 administration fee.

To get a refund please follow the instructions below. Please note you will need to supply your original ticket not a copy:

- For tickets bought at the station you can apply for a refund at your local station

- For tickets bought online, log on to your online account and follow on-screen instructions

- For tickets bought online at www.networkwestmidlands.com or via the Corporate Sales Scheme can write to: Direct Sales, Centro House, 16 Summer Lane, Birmingham B19 3SD.

- For more information visit www.londonnorthwesternrailway.co.uk or www.westmidlandsrailway.co.uk
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
Available from the '

network' Travel Shop at Birmingham New Street station as well as at any staffed station in the Network West Midlands area.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Photocard required for purchase of Season Tickets
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Day tickets bought by passengers who forget their Season Tickets will not be refunded.

Child.Note: Child version available for all zones and only for the periods of 1 week, 1 month or 1 term.

RailCard.Note: No discounts.

Group.Note: No discounts.

Full details of the full range of tickets, periods of validity, zones and maps can be found on the Network West Midlands website.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day within the ticket's validity.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day within the ticket's validity.

Validity.TimeReturn: Valid any time within the zones stated on the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Single (one-way) ticket which offers travel in the seated coach on a Caledonian Sleeper service. Available for any journey on the Caledonian Sleeper route and also for selected connecting services to / from other Scottish stations.

Ticket also includes:

  • Reclining spacious seats
  • Buffet service available with hot and cold food and drink, but no access to the lounge car

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on the booked Caledonian Sleeper service only. Travel must be made on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn:
Not applicable.

For a return journey, buy a single ticket in each direction.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid on the booked Caledonian Sleeper service only. Travel must be made on the service shown on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Not applicable.

For a return journey, buy a single ticket in each direction.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: No break of journey is allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability:
Tickets must be bought in advance.

Reservations are compulsory on Sleeper services.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
Tickets can be booked direct with Caledonian Sleepers at www.sleeper.scot or by calling 0330 060 0500.

Tickets can also be purchased at most major National Rail stations.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Can be booked in advance or purchased on the day of travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: There are no change of travel arrangements. Guests wishing to change their travel arrangements should refund their ticket and purchase a new ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Refunds are available on a sliding scale depending on when the refund is requested.

  • 7 days or more before travel - a full refund is available with no admin fee
  • Less than 7 days and before midday the day before travel (2 days before for departures between 00:01 and 04:30) a 50% refund is available
  • After midday the day before departure (2 days before for departures between 00:01 and 04:30) refunds are no longer available

Child.Note:
Infants aged 0-4 inclusive travel free of charge provided they share a seat or sleeper berth with an accompanying adult. A maximum of one infant can share with one adult. Two infants must be accompanied by two adults.

Children aged 5 to 15 travel with 50% off full fares.

RailCard.Note: 16 -25 Railcard, Senior Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, Disabled Persons Railcards and Two Together Railcard all offer 1/3 off.

Group.Note: By prior arrangement only -  contact Caledonian Sleepers direct on 0330 060 0500.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Semi Flex Return is a partially flexible ticket where the outward journey is only valid on booked train(s), the return journey is flexible and can be made on any day within one month of the outward journey.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Semi Flex Return tickets are valid for return travel within one calendar month from the start date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 after last day of validity.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid only at the specified time(s) and train(s) printed on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Customers cannot start, break and resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: A break of journey is permitted on the return portion of a Semi Flex Return.

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Online through a website or app, at any National Rail ticket office or via telesales.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased by 23:59 the day before travel, subject to availability. Generally, the earlier the ticket is booked the better the deal. Tickets are available up to 12 weeks before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. Customers will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when they request a change.

Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased.

The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same.

The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable, plus a £10 administration fee per person, per ticket for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded.

Please note that where a Semi Flex Return ticket has been purchased online and the ticket has been downloaded to a mobile device this may alter the rules for changing the ticket. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Semi Flex Return tickets are non-refundable, but changes may be made - see above.

RailCard.Note: Normal railcard discounts apply, with no discount for Network Railcard, Annual Gold Card holders or any other regional railcard.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: One-day multi-journey ticket to travel between Settle-Carlisle via Appleby

Validity.DayOutward: Valid after 0845 Monday-Friday and any time Saturdays, Sundays and public holidays. Allows travel between Settle and Carlisle via Appleby.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid after 0845 Monday-Friday and any time Saturdays, Sundays and public holidays. Allows travel between Settle and Carlisle via Appleby.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Yes

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No reservations are needed.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are only given on Rail Rover tickets returned before the start date shown on them and are subject to an administration charge.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Discounts for children and holders of Senior, 16-25, Family & Friends and Disabled Persons Railcards. Discounts are also available to holders of Staff Travel and New Entrant Cards.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note: Discounts for children and holders of Senior, 16-25, Family & Friends and Disabled Persons Railcards. Discounts are also available to holders of Staff Travel and New Entrant Cards.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Multi-journey ticket on the line between Bristol Temple Meads and Severn Beach

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on the date printed on the ticket after 0900.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid on the date printed on the ticket after 0900.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Yes

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Monday-Friday only.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No reservations are needed. Tickets may be bought in advance or on the day of travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refunds are available on unused tickets surrendered before the day of travel.

Child.Note: No Child or Railcard discounts.

RailCard.Note: No Child or Railcard discounts.

Group.Note: No Child or Railcard discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A Rover ticket that allows one outward and return journey from London Marylebone or Paddington to any of the following stations:

  • Leamington Spa
  • Warwick
  • Warwick Parkway
  • Hatton
  • Claverdon
  • Bearley
  • Wilmcote
  • Stratford-upon-Avon Parkway
  • Stratford-upon-Avon

The ticket also allows unlimited travel between these stations for the period of validity.

In addition, break of journey is permitted at stations between London Marylebone and Leamington Spa when travelling to or from the Rover area.

Available as either a 1 or 4 day return.

  • SH1 - 1 Day Adult/ Child: use Rover type
  • SH2 - 1 Day Senior: use Rover type
  • SH3 - 4 Day Adult/ Child: use Rover type
  • SH4 - 4 Day Senior: use Rover type

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day - outward journey as dated on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid any day.

Return journey as dated on the ticket for day returns or within four days of the date date on the ticket for the four day return.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid from London Marylebone at any time.
Validity.TimeReturn: Not valid for return travel to London on trains timed to arrive London Marylebone or Paddington before 10:00 Mondays to Fridays.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
Yes, but only within the area shown above, Leamington Spa to Stratford-upon-Avon.

Not valid for break of journey anywhere between London Marylebone or Paddington to Leamington Spa.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote:
Yes, but only within the area shown above, Leamington Spa to Stratford-upon-Avon.

Not valid for break of journey anywhere between London Marylebone or Paddington to Leamington Spa.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refunds are only given on Rail Rover tickets returned before the start date shown on them and are subject to an administration charge.

Child.Note: 50% discount.

RailCard.Note:
Senior discounts are available to any person who has reached the qualifying age for a Senior Railcard. It is however not a requirement that they are actually a Railcard holder.

In addition, Family & Friends Railcard discounts apply on the Four Day ticket only.

Group.Note: GroupSave offers discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Sleeper Advance Solo - Great Western Railway Night Riviera
TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Single (one-way) ticket which offers exclusive use of a standard class sleeper cabin.

Ticket also includes:

  • Exclusive use of standard class twin-berth cabin
  • Accommodation on board the Night Riviera Sleeper service is Standard Class only and consists of a bed with a duvet, wash basin with a shaver point, towel, bottled water, personally controlled lighting and air conditioning.
  • Access to Lounge car to purchase refreshments or there is a Customer Host for in-cabin room service. subject to space
  • Breakfast is served in the cabin or in the Lounge Car depending on preference.
  • Use of the Great Western Railway First Class Lounge located on platform 1 at London Paddington from 21:30.
  • Use the Network Rail showers located on platform 12 at London Paddington.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on the booked Night Riviera service only. Travel must be made on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn:
Not applicable.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Sleeper Advance Twin - Great Western Railway Night Riviera
TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Single (one-way) ticket which offers shared use of a standard class twin-berth sleeper cabin. The two passengers in that booking will be placed together and will not be required to share accommodation with other passengers.*

Ticket also includes:

  • Shared use of standard class twin-berth cabin
  • Accommodation on board the Night Riviera Sleeper service is Standard Class only and consists of a bed with a duvet, wash basin with a shaver point, towel, bottled water, personally controlled lighting and air conditioning.
  • Access to Lounge car to purchase refreshments or there is a Customer Host for in-cabin room service. subject to space
  • Breakfast is served in the cabin or in the Lounge Car depending on preference.
  • Use of the Great Western Railway First Class Lounge located on platform 1 at London Paddington from 21:30.
  • Use the Network Rail showers located on platform 12 at London Paddington.  

*Bookings into Twin berth accommodation made by individual passengers with walk-up tickets using additional Sleeper Supplement XBD "GWR Sleeper Twin" may be required to share accommodation with fellow passengers of the same sex. This does not apply to Sleeper Twin Advance fares - these include both beds within the same Twin berth.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on the booked Night Riviera service only. Travel must be made on the date shown on the ticket.

Not applicable.

Validity.TimeReturn:

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Sleeper On-board Supplement - Great Western Railway Night Riviera
TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Sleeper Berth Supplement if a ticket for the same route is already held.

If a ticket for daytime travel or travel in the seated accommodation of the Night Riviera is already held and you wish to upgrade to a Sleeper Berth whilst on the train, a Berth Supplement can be purchased.

Sleeper Berth On-Board Supplement is a single (one-way) supplement to an existing ticket which offers the opportunity to use either one berth in a standard class twin-berth sleeper cabin or both berths for sole occupancy. If only one berth is purchased than that person may need to share the cabin with another person of the same gender.

Supplement includes:

  • Shared use (one Berth Supplement) or sole use (two Berth Supplements) of a standard class twin-berth cabin
  • Accommodation on board the Night Riviera Sleeper service is Standard Class only and consists of a bed with a duvet, wash basin with a shaver point, towel, bottled water, personally controlled lighting and air conditioning.
  • Access to Lounge car to purchase refreshments or there is a Customer Host for in-cabin room service. subject to space
  • Breakfast is served in the cabin or in the Lounge Car depending on preference.
  • Use of the Great Western Railway First Class Lounge located on platform 1 at London Paddington from 21:30.
  • Use the Network Rail showers located on platform 12 at London Paddington. 

XBK - Twin Berth On-Board Supplement [sole occupancy]XBL - Single Berth On-Board Supplement [shared occupancy]

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the Night Riviera service for where the on-board supplement has been purchased.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid only on the Night Riviera service for where the on-board supplement has been purchased.
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: No break of journey is allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Subject to availability.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Can only purchased on board the Night Riviera service.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Cannot be purchased in advance. Other Sleeper Berth tickets are available to purchase in advance.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Not applicable.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: No refunds.

Child.Note: 50% discounts

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
Tickets can be booked direct with Great Western Railway or by calling 0345 7000 125.

Tickets can also be purchased at most major National Rail stations.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines:
Tickets must be bought by 18:00 the day before travel.

Reservations are obligatory.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
Changes are only allowed before the departure of the first reserved train, provided origin and destination are the same.

A change fee is payable plus any difference in ticket prices.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Unused tickets may be surrendered for a refund.

Refunds available up to 28 days after the date on the unused ticket.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 50% discounts.

RailCard.Note: Child discounts. No Railcard discounts.

Group.Note: Child discounts. No Railcard discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Sleeper Twin Supplement - Great Western Railway Night Riviera
TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Sleeper Berth Supplement if a ticket for the same route is already held.

If a ticket for daytime travel or travel in the seated accommodation of the Night Riviera is already held but plans change and travel in a Sleeper Berth is required instead, a Berth Supplement can be purchased.

Sleeper Twin Supplement is a single (one-way) supplement to an existing ticket which offers shared use of a standard class twin-berth sleeper cabin. If one person is travelling alone, they may need to share the cabin with another person of the same gender.*

Supplement includes:

  • Shared use of standard class twin-berth cabin
  • Accommodation on board the Night Riviera Sleeper service is Standard Class only and consists of a bed with a duvet, wash basin with a shaver point, towel, bottled water, personally controlled lighting and air conditioning.
  • Access to Lounge car to purchase refreshments or there is a Customer Host for in-cabin room service. subject to space
  • Breakfast is served in the cabin or in the Lounge Car depending on preference.
  • Use of the Great Western Railway First Class Lounge located on platform 1 at London Paddington from 21:30.
  • Use the Network Rail showers located on platform 12 at London Paddington.  

*__Sharing Twin Cabins

The National Reservation System recognises three gender options for bookings using Sleeper Twin Supplements:

  • Together
  • Male
  • Female

When booking Sleeper Twin Supplements (XBD) for 2 customers travelling together, choose Gender “Together” (not Male / Female), otherwise the reservation system may place them in separate single sex Twin Berths.

Single customers buying this Supplement also have the option of sharing a Twin berth with another customer (of the same sex) who has also booked to share. Where single passengers book shared use of Twin Berth accommodation using the Twin Supplement, t__he reservations system will only book two separate passengers into the same Twin Berth if their stated gender, joining and alighting stations are all the same.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Small Group Day offers groups of 3 to 9 people travelling together a 15% discount on Standard Day Returns. ​​​​​​​

Small Group Day tickets are available on all routes priced by Transport for Wales and are the TfW Group product equivalent to Groupsave. They can be used on other operators' services as listed below.

​​​​​​​

Validity.DayOutward: Valid within the Transport for Wales network where a Standard Day Return is available Monday to Friday, all day Saturday, Sunday and Bank Holidays.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid within the Transport for Wales network where a Standard Day Return is available Monday to Friday, all day Saturday, Sunday and Bank Holidays.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid for journeys 09:30-15:59 and from 18:30 M-F
Validity.TimeReturn: Not valid for journeys starting 16:00-18:29 M-F

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
A minimum of 3 people travelling together is required for Small Group Day discounted tickets to be valid for travel. Usual excess fare arrangements apply,

All members of the group must travel together at all times to validate the product.

If there are 10 or more traveling together, you need contact the Group Travel Department on 02920 320 002 (Opening hours: Mon-Fri 08:30 - 16:00)
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: This ticket can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company website and selected rail appointed travel agents.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: You may change your travel plans at any time within the validity of the ticket without having to pay an additional fare. Restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket may apply to both the outward and return journeys - please check before travelling
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Small Group Day ticket are refundable. If you decide not to use some or all of your ticket (s) to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

If the number of tickets requested for refund reduces the group size that travel to below 3 (1 or 2 travelers), then the undiscounted Standard Day return ticket price will be used to calculate journeys made.  ​​​​​​​

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

If the number of tickets requested for refund reduces the group size that travel to below 3 (1 or 2 travelers), then the undiscounted Standard Day return ticket price will be used to calculate journeys made.  ​​​​​​​

Group.Note: This ticket is a Group product

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A day ranger ticket, allowing unlimited travel in the ranger area which covers most of the South Pennines area and a little further afield.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Valid after 08:45 Monday to Friday.

Valid any time at weekends and on Bank Holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.

TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refundable only if surrendered before the start date.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A Standard Class weekend return ticket which can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.

Validity.DayOutward:
WK4 The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket (which must be a Friday).

WK3 The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket (which must be a Friday, or a Saturday on a Bank Holiday weekend).

WK2 The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket (which must be a Saturday, or a Sunday on a Bank Holiday weekend).
Validity.DayReturn:
WK4 The return journey must be made on Saturday or Sunday of the same weekend, or a Bank Holiday Monday.

WK3 The return journey must be made on Saturday or Sunday of the same weekend, or a Bank Holiday Monday.

WK2 The return journey must be made on Sunday of the same weekend, or a Bank Holiday Monday.
Validity.TimeOutward: Time restrictions apply for outward travel on a Friday unless it's a Bank Holiday, there are no time restrictions on when you can travel on a Saturday or Sunday.
Validity.TimeReturn: There are no time restrictions for return journeys on a Saturday, Sunday or Bank Holiday Monday.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Unlimited travel (subject to time restrictions shown) throughout Scotland and between Carlisle and Berwick-upon-Tweed.

Available for flexible periods, either 3 days within 7 [ST9] or 8 days within 15 [ST8].

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid for 3 days out of 7 or 8 days out of 15 according to choice.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid for 3 days out of 7 or 8 days out of 15 according to choice.

The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Mondays to Fridays valid from 09:15.

There are no restrictions on the following routes:

  • Glasgow Queen Street - Oban/Fort William/Mallaig
  • ​Inverness - Kyle/Wick/Thurso
  • Glasgow Central - Stranraer
  • Direct services from Lockerbie to Carlisle, Carstairs, Motherwell or Glasgow Central

Valid at any time on Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Mondays to Fridays valid from 09:15.

There are no restrictions on the following routes:

  • Glasgow Queen Street - Oban/Fort William/Mallaig
  • ​Inverness - Kyle/Wick/Thurso
  • Glasgow Central - Stranraer
  • Direct services from Lockerbie to Carlisle, Carstairs, Motherwell or Glasgow Central

Valid at any time on Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
• The Spirit of Scotland Travelpass is not subject to availability and does not need to be purchased/booked in advance.
  • Spirit of Scotland Travelpass is valid for travel in Standard class only. 
  • Upgrades to First Class are not available
  • Weekday and Weekend First Supplements are not valid in conjunction with Spirit of Scotland Travelpass tickets.
  • Reservations can be made up to 12 weeks in advance.
  • 50% discount for children aged 5-15.  Under 5's travel free.
  • Standard Railcard discounts apply.
  • Refunds are available, subject to a £10 administration fee, for each unused ticket surrendered before the first date of travel.
  • Customers can change their travel plans, as they have unlimited travel, but they cannot change the travel validity period.
  • Customers can break their journey as they have unlimited travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Rover tickets may not be issued more than 3 days in advance of their validity (5 days for telesales and internet bookings).
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: The Rover has unlimited travel but you cannot change the travel validity period of travel passes.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are only given on Spirit of Scotland Travelpasses returned to the original retailer before the start date shown on them and are subject to an administration fee of no more than £10.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 1/2 price of Adult fares

RailCard.Note:
1/3 discount with the following Railcards

  • 16-25 Railcard
  • 26-30 Railcard
  • Disabled Persons Railcard
  • Family & Friends Railcard
  • Senior Railcard
  • Veterans Railcard

TicketTypeDescription.SpecialConditions:
Spirit of Scotland Travelpass holders can also enjoy included or discounted travel on some travel partners.

  • 10% discount on Strathspey Steam Railway standard fares
  • 10% discount on Keith & Dufftown Railway standard fares
  • Travel between Gourock and Dunoon with Argyll Ferries
  • 20% off these Northlink Ferries journeys when Seated Standard tickets are purchased on the day of travel:
    • Scrabster - Stromness
    • ​Aberdeen - Lerwick
    • Aberdeen - Kirkwall - Lerwick
  • Travel on the following routes with Scottish Citylink Coaches and West Coast Motors:
    • Oban / Fort William - Inverness
    • Oban - Campbeltown
    • ​Kyle of Lochalsh - Uig
    • Inverness - Ullapool
  • Travel with Stagecoach East Scotland and Stagecoach in the Highlands on the following routes:
    • Dundee / Leuchars - St Andrews
    • Services on the Isle of Skye
    • Wick - John O'Groats
    • Thurso - John O'Groats
    • ​Thurso - Scrabster
  • ​Travel with First Scotland East on the following routes:
    • Melrose - Galashiels - Peebles - Edinburgh
    • Carlisle - Langholm - Hawick - Galashiels - Edinburgh
  • 10% discount on Single and Day Return tickets with D & E Coaches between Inverness and Durness

Further information can be found on ScotRail's website.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Standard Class Return ticket to London with added benefits.

The package includes:

  • Standard class Return rail travel by London North Eastern Railway and connecting services.
  • One outward and one return free seat reservation.
  • complimentary catering, which can be used outward and/or return.
  • Up to 3 days free car parking at selected stations subject to availability

Note: Restaurants, where carried, are subject to availability. There are no restaurants and no access to First Class accommodation for Standard Class customers except on payment of the upgrade.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day, as dated on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day within one calendar month.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid for the train specified on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid on any London North Eastern Railway service.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Not available on Saturdays, Sundays and public holidays as there is no restaurant car.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be bought by 18:00 the day before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
• Change of outward date allowed, without penalty, if done in advance of travel date and within the validity of the ticket.
  • Change of outward train allowed, on the date shown on the ticket, without penalty.
  • Upgrade to First Class is allowed upon payment of the difference between the SOS and FOS fare for each leg excessed to/from London. No additional benefits are available.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
• Completely unused and returned complete - full refund less any local cancellation charge.
  • Unused outward leg and partially completed journeys - no refund.
  • Unused return legs - from the ticket value, deduct the equivalent Standard Class Anytime Single fare.
  • Deduct a further £35·00 plus any local cancellation fees, and refund the remainder.
  • Car parking used but not ticket - deduct £30 plus any local cancellation fees and refund the remainder.

Note: restaurants, where carried, are subject to availability. There are no restaurants and no access to First Class accommodation for Standard Class customers except on payment of the upgrade.

  • Up to 3 days free car parking at selected stations subject to availability (XC1 tickets only).
  • London Underground Zones 1 & 2 return tickets (XC1, XD1 tickets to London only).

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Multi-modal Season Ticket for Strathclyde PTE zones.

The ZoneCard is a flexible season ticket for unlimited travel by train, Glasgow Subway, most buses (not night buses or X4) and some ferries in the Strathclyde region. ZoneCard may be used wherever you see the ZoneCard logo.

There are 12 zones in the Strathclyde PTE area. Minimum purchase is 2 zones; (If you hold a valid ticket for either G1 or G2 and wish to travel in an outer Glasgow zone (G3 to G8) a minimum of Three zones is required). Maximum purchase is all zones.

ZoneCard is valid from Sunday to Saturday.

ZNW - weekly

ZN4 - 4 weekly

ZNT - 10 weeks

ZNA - Annual

(only the above periods of availability are offered)

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day in the Strathclyde PTE area. The ticket-holder's photocard is needed to validate the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day in the Strathclyde PTE area. The ticket-holder's photocard is needed to validate the ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid any time in the Strathclyde PTE area. The ticket-holder's photocard is needed to validate the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid any time in the Strathclyde PTE area. The ticket-holder's photocard is needed to validate the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability:
ZoneCard is valid from Sunday to Saturday.

For a first time purchase, you can buy one on the Saturday prior to its validity (although the ticket will not be valid for use until the following day). First time purchase will require production of a passport sized photograph.

Existing users can renew their Zonecard from the Thursday preceding the expiry date of your ticket. Existing Photocard required for renewals.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
Scotrail station ticket offices or travel centres.

SPT Travel Shops or SPT website.

Additionally, existing card holders can renew at selected newsagents and convenicence stores in the Glasgow area.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No pre-booking requirement except as detailed above for first time purchases.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Refunds or replacements for lost or stolen ZoneCards cannot be given, therefore insurance is recommended.

No refunds will be given on weekly tickets.

Refunds are only available on unused complete weeks of all other tickets and an administration fee of £3.00 will be charged. To obtain a refund you should post your ZoneCard to:

The Survey and Data Section,

SPT, Consort House,

12 West George Street

Glasgow

G2 1HN

or deliver your ZoneCard to any SPT travel centre.

Child.Note:
Only 1 and 4 week Season Zonecards.

Only 2, 3, 4, 5 or all zones Zonecards.

See SPT website for more details.

RailCard.Note: No discounts.

Group.Note: No discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Sunday Out Day Travelcard is available on shorter distance journeys to London on Sundays, only.

It allows customers to travel to London and enjoy unlimited travel throughout London on National Rail, London Underground, Docklands Light Railway, Tramlink and London Bus services within Fare Zones 1-6

Validity.DayOutward: Sunday Out Day Travelcard tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.DayReturn: Sunday Out Day Travelcard tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Sunday Out Day Travelcard ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Sunday Out Day Travelcard ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
A break of journey is permitted on the outward portion off a Sunday Out Day Travelcard unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Within the London Fares Zones, the ticket is valid for unlimited travel.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote:
A break of journey is permitted on the return portion off a Sunday Out Day Travelcard unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Within the London Fares Zones, the ticket is valid for unlimited travel.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: This ticket can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable.

If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Sunday Out Return is offered on shorter distance journeys on Sundays only.

Validity.DayOutward: Sunday Out Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.DayReturn: Sunday Out Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Sunday Out ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Sunday Out ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is permitted on the outward portion off a Sunday Out Return unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: A break of journey is permitted on the return portion off a Sunday Out Return unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

Sunday Out tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Sunday Out ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Off-Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may need to travel at specific times of the day, days of the week and sometimes on specific routes or operators.

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket will depend on the journey you are making and you will be advised when buying your ticket. The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

Validity.DayOutward: Super Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.DayReturn: Super Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

Super Off-Peak Day tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Super Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, change the date of travel, or change the time you are allowed to travel.

Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey.

NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.

For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable.

If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Group.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) 50% discount. Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

Other discount cards: Gold Cards: 1/3 off and Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: 50% discount. NOTE: Minimum fares / time restrictions may apply to tickets bought with a Railcard or other discount card. See terms and conditions of the appropriate Railcard or discount card for details.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
• Standard Class Day Return ticket for Off-Peak travel.
  • Super Off-Peak Day Return tickets can be purchased in advance or on the day of travel.
  • Super Off-Peak Day Return tickets can be loaded on to a ScotRail Smartcard or an mTicket offering a seamless journey experience.
  • Super Off-Peak Day Return tickets are available to buy via Smartcard or paper ticket from stations or on-train when boarding at a station where no ticket office is available.
  • Available on journeys to/from Glasgow, Edinburgh, Aberdeen and Inverness that already offer an Off-Peak Day Return (CDR).
  • Super Off-Peak Day Return tickets are not subject to availability and do not need to be booked in advance.
  • Valid on ScotRail services only.
  • Super Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel Monday - Fridays only, they are not valid for travel at weekends.
  • Not valid Monday to Friday on trains departing before 11:00.
  • Not valid for boarding in Edinburgh, Glasgow, Aberdeen and Inverness Monday to Friday between 15:00 and 20:00.
  • Outward and return travel must be made on the same day.

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid Monday to Fridays.

Not valid at weekends.
Validity.DayReturn:
Valid Monday to Fridays.

Not valid at weekends.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Not valid Monday to Fridays on trains timed to depart before 11:00.

Not valid Monday to Fridays from boarding in Edinburgh, Glasgow, Aberdeen or Inverness from 15:00 to 20:00.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Not valid Monday to Fridays on trains timed to depart before 11:00.

Not valid Monday to Fridays for boarding in Edinburgh, Glasgow, Aberdeen or Inverness from 15:00 to 20:00.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Journeys can be broken at stations along the route of travel.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Journeys can be broken at stations along the route of travel.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Super Off-Peak Day Return tickets are available to buy via Smartcard or paper ticket from stations or on-train when boarding at a station where no ticket office is available.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: At stations and on-train only
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Travel plans can be changed before the outward journey subject to a £10 administration fee online at www.scotrail.co.uk.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refunds are available via www.scotrail.co.uk, subject to a £10 administration fee, on unused tickets surrendered before the date of outward travel.

Child.Note:
Under 5's travel free.

50% discount for children aged 5-15.

For child discount, a Child Smartcard is required.

RailCard.Note: Normal Railcard discounts apply, but customer must have a ScotRail Smartcard.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Super Off-Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may need to travel at specific times of the day, days of the week and sometimes on specific routes or operators.

The times when you may use your Super Off-Peak ticket will depend on the journey you are making and you will be advised when buying your ticket. The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

Validity.DayOutward: Super Off-Peak Day Single tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Super Off-Peak Day tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Super Off-Peak Day ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, change the date of travel, or change the time you are allowed to travel.

Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey.

NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable.

If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Super Off-Peak Day tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Super Off-Peak Day ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Online only at Greater Anglia website
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Children (aged 5-15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount

RailCard.Note:
1/3 off with:

  • 16-25 Railcard
  • Family & Friends
  • Two Together
  • Disabled Persons Railcard
  • HM Forces Railcard
  • Senior Railcard
  • Network Railcard
  • Annual Gold Card

Minimum fares / time restrictions may apply to tickets bought with a Railcard or other discount card. See terms and conditions of the appropriate Railcard or discount card for details.

Railcard holders travelling on a discounted ticket must carry their Railcard when they travel. If a Railcard holder fails to produce their valid Railcard with their ticket, they will be required to pay a full priced ticket for their journey as if no Railcard and/or no ticket were held.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Off-Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may need to travel at specific times of the day, days of the week and sometimes on specific routes or operators.

Validity.DayOutward:
Super Off-Peak Single (Online) tickets are valid for outward travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.

If you cannot complete your journey on the same day you will be allowed to finish it the following day - see 'Break of journey' below.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
A break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Overnight break of journey - if the journey cannot be completed in this time, the ticket may be used to continue the journey on the following day. Unless otherwise indicated in the relevant restriction code, time restrictions apply as from the initial origin station on both days. The appropriate restrictions for the actual day on which travel is being undertaken apply (for example, it may be that if day 1 is on Sunday, no restrictions apply, but on day 2, the Monday-Friday restrictions apply). All travel must be completed by 04:29 in the morning after this second day.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

This ticket is only valid as part of a return journey. If the other portion of the return journey is cancelled, this ticket (Super Off-Peak Single (Online)) must be cancelled at the same time.
