The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Overnight break of journey - if the journey cannot be completed on the same day, the ticket may be used to continue the journey on the following day.

Unless otherwise indicated in the relevant restriction code, time restrictions apply as from the initial origin station on both days.

The appropriate restrictions for the actual day on which travel is being undertaken apply (for example, it may be that if day 1 is on Sunday, no restrictions apply, but on day 2, the Monday-Friday restrictions apply). All travel must be completed by 04:29 in the morning after this second day.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

Off-Peak tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel. Outward journey valid on date(s) shown. Return within 1 calendar month.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, change the date of travel, or change the time you are allowed to travel.

Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey.

NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable.

If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) 50% discount. Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

Railcard discounts: Two Together, 16-25 Railcard: 1/3 off, Disabled Persons Railcard: 1/3 off, Family & Friends Railcard: 1/3 off, HM Forces Railcard: 1/3 off, Network Railcard: 1/3 off and Senior Railcard: 1/3 off on Standard. Other discount cards: Gold Cards: 1/3 off and Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: 50% discount. NOTE: Minimum fares / time restrictions may apply to tickets bought with a Railcard or other discount card. See terms and conditions of the appropriate Railcard or discount card for details.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Super Off-Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may need to travel at specific times of the day, days of the week and sometimes on specific routes or operators.

The times when you may use your Super Off-Peak ticket will depend on the journey you are making and you will be advised when buying your ticket. The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

Validity.DayOutward:
Super Off-Peak Single tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day.

If you cannot complete your journey on the same day you will be allowed to finish it the following day - see 'Break of journey' below.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
A break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code. 

Overnight break of journey - if the journey cannot be completed in this time, the ticket may be used to continue the journey on the following day. Unless otherwise indicated in the relevant restriction code, time restrictions apply as from the initial origin station on both days. The appropriate restrictions for the actual day on which travel is being undertaken apply (for example, it may be that if day 1 is on Sunday, no restrictions apply, but on day 2, the Monday-Friday restrictions apply). All travel must be completed by 04:29 in the morning after this second day.

Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.

This ticket is only valid as part of a return journey. If the other portion of the return journey is cancelled, this ticket (online Super Off-Peak single) must be cancelled at the same time. A £10 charge will apply for any refund.

This ticket is only valid as part of a return journey. If the other portion of the return journey is cancelled, this ticket (online Super Off-Peak single) must be cancelled at the same time.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Travelling towards London: Not valid if alighting at Clapham Junction, Queenstown Road, Vauxhall or London Waterloo 07:00-09:29 inclusive, Monday to Friday. Travelling away from London: Not valid if boarding at London Waterloo, Vauxhall, Queenstown Road or Clapham Junction between 17:00-18:59 inclusive, Monday to Friday.

No restrictions if joining the train after Clapham Junction or for travelling towards London during this period.

Travel at any time on Saturday, Sunday and public holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Travelling towards London: Not valid if alighting at Clapham Junction, Queenstown Road, Vauxhall or London Waterloo 07:00-09:29 inclusive, Monday to Friday. Travelling away from London: Not valid if boarding at London Waterloo, Vauxhall, Queenstown Road or Clapham Junction between 17:00-18:59 inclusive, Monday to Friday.

No restrictions if joining the train after Clapham Junction or for travelling towards London during this period.

Travel at any time on Saturday, Sunday and public holidays.

The Advance Flex Max is a semi-flexible ticket where the outward journey is only valid on booked train(s), the return journey is flexible but must be completed within 5 days of the outward journey. This product is only available on Transport for Wales services.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid up to 5 days from start of outward journey.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid only at the specified time(s) and train(s) printed on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid on any Transport for Wales train.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Customers cannot start, break and resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Customers cannot start, break and resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Limited availability.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company website and selected rail appointed travel agents.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased in advance of travel, subject to availability.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. Customers will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when they request a change.

Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased.

The origin, destination or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same.

The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable. An administration fee of no more than £10 may be charged per ticket for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: The Advance Flex tickets are non-refundable, but changes may be made - see below.

RailCard.Note: Normal railcard discounts apply, with no discount for Network Railcard, Annual Gold Card holders or any other regional railcard.

Group.Note: Not applicable.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
The Transport for Wales concession event ticket offers free return travel from Cardiff Central to stations within the Wales and Borders boundary, the ticket is only valid on a TfW service. The product will ONLY be made available to key workers who will be working at category A events taking place in Cardiff. The product will be managed through the fares scheme calendar functionality in PMS, it will only appear to issue for the event day itself under route code 44 (TFW ONLY).

The product will only be retailable through the TFW Travel Management Service, TFW RS booking offices and, by exception, TfW RS conductors on-board TfW RS services where TfW RS booking offices don’t exist at origin station. To qualify for the free ticket the customer must present their relevant ID to the front-line colleague. Details on which key workers will be eligible for an event day will be cascaded to front line colleagues ahead of time.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid within the Transport for Wales network, tickets are valid for one return journey. The outward portion is valid on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid within the Transport for Wales network, tickets are valid for one return journey. The outward portion is valid on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid to travel at any time of the day.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid to travel at any time of the day.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Available to buy from the Travel Management Service, any staffed ticket office, or on-board from a Transport for Wales Conductor.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Multi-journey ticket for travel within the Thames Valley area.

The area of validity covers all lines west out of London Paddington as far west as Reading West and Tilehurst, including the branches to Greenford, Windsor & Eton Central, Marlow and Henley-on Thames also the line from Reading as far as Winnersh. Not vlaid to Heathorw Airport.

Available as either a three or seven day Rover.

Further information can be found on First Great Western's website.

TR3 - Thames Rover (3-day)TR7 - Thames Rover (7-day)

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day within the ticket's validity, subject to the time and train service restriction shown below.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day within the ticket's validity, subject to the time and train service restriction shown below.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Valid after 09:30 on weekdays.

Not valid on High Speed services departing London Paddington after 16:14 and until 19:16.

Valid any time at weekends and bank holidays.

Not valid at any time on Heathrow Connect services between Hayes & Harlington and Heathrow Airport.

Not valid at any time on any Heathrow Express services.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Valid after 09:30 on weekdays.

Not valid on High Speed services departing London Paddington after 16:14 and until 19:16.

Valid any time at weekends and bank holidays.

Not valid at any time on Heathrow Connect services between Hayes & Harlington and Heathrow Airport.

Not valid at any time on any Heathrow Express services.

TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are only available on unused tickets surrendered before the day of travel and may be subject to an administration fee.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 50% discount.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Standard Class fixed fare group ticket for up to 4 adults or children for travel between any station served by Thameslink and either Gatwick Airport or Luton Airport Parkway

Validity.DayOutward: The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket. The return journey must take place within one calendar month(s) of the date shown on the ticket. There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket. The return journey must take place within one calendar month(s) of the date shown on the ticket. There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is allowed on both portions of the ticket, as described in the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: A break of journey is allowed on both portions of the ticket, as described in the National Rail Conditions of Travel.

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: This ticket can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: You may change your travel plans at any time within the validity of the ticket without having to pay an additional fare. There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

A full refund is available if you return the completely unused ticket to the place of purchase within 28 days of expiry of the ticket. The amount of the refund will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: No further discounts but for each FlyAway ticket, a further 4 children may accompany the group for a flat fare of a pound each.

RailCard.Note: No further discounts but for each FlyAway ticket, a further 4 children may accompany the group for a flat fare of a pound each.

Group.Note: No further discounts but for each FlyAway ticket, a further 4 children may accompany the group for a flat fare of a pound each.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A return ticket valid to return between one and three days after your outbound journey. (E.g. if your outbound journey is on Monday, your ticket allows you to return once on Tuesday, Wednesday or Thursday.)

Validity.DayOutward: Every day
Validity.DayReturn: Within 3 days of Outward date

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Anytime tickets can be used at any time but may require you to travel via a specific route or on a specific train company and this will be clearly stated on the ticket.

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: c2c Travel app, c2c website or at a c2c ticket office.p>
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note:
16-25 Railcard, 26-30 Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Two Together, Disabled Persons Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Network Railcard and Annual Gold Card all offer 1/3 off.

Minimum fares / time restrictions may apply to tickets bought with a Railcard or other discount card. See terms and conditions of the appropriate Railcard or discount card for details.

Railcard holders travelling on a discounted ticket must carry their Railcard when they travel. If a Railcard holder fails to produce their valid Railcard with their ticket, they will be required to pay a full priced ticket for their journey as if no Railcard and/or no ticket were held.

TicketTypeDescription.SpecialConditions: Only valid on c2c services, and between stations served by c2c.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A Standard Class return ticket which can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.

Validity.DayOutward: The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket (which must be a Friday or a Saturday). The return journey must be made within four days of the date shown on the ticket, but not on the Friday. There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket (which must be a Friday or a Saturday). The return journey must be made within four days of the date shown on the ticket, but not on the Friday. There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is not allowed on the outward portion of the ticket.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: A break of journey is allowed on the return portion of the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: This ticket can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: You may change your travel plans at any time within the validity of the ticket without having to pay an additional fare. There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: A full refund is available if you return the completely unused ticket to the place of purchase within 28 days of expiry of the ticket. The amount of the refund will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

Child.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): 50% discount. Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger. Railcard discounts: Disabled Persons Railcard: 1/3 off, Family & Friends Railcard: 1/3 off, HM Forces Railcard: 1/3 off, Network Railcard: 1/3 off, Senior Railcard: 1/3 off and Two Together, 16-25 Railcard: 1/3 off. Other discount cards: Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: 50% discount and Gold Cards: 1/3 off.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): 50% discount. Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger. Railcard discounts: Disabled Persons Railcard: 1/3 off, Family & Friends Railcard: 1/3 off, HM Forces Railcard: 1/3 off, Network Railcard: 1/3 off, Senior Railcard: 1/3 off and Two Together, 16-25 Railcard: 1/3 off. Other discount cards: Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: 50% discount and Gold Cards: 1/3 off.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Valid for 3 adults and up to 6 children (15 years old and under) travelling together between Heathrow Airport and London on Heathrow Express services
Return tickets with compulsory outward reservations and no sales on the day of travel

Validity.DayOutward: TPE Corporate (Standard) tickets are valid for outward travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day.
Validity.DayReturn: TPE Corporate (Standard) tickets are valid for return travel within one calendar month from the start date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 after last day of validity.

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Online and Telesales
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
• If you wish to change your date or time of travel you can do so through your retailer up until one day before departure. Change of Train fees may apply.
  • On the day of departure if you choose not to travel on your booked outward train you will need to purchase the appropriate walk-up ticket for the train on which you travel.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Trainlink C tickets are a fare that covers both the 319 bus from Skemersdale and unlimited train travel on Merseyrail trains in Area C.

Trainlink C tickets may be used on any services after 09.29 Monday to Friday, excluding bank holidays and anytime at weekends.

Validity.DayOutward: Trainlink C tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket only.
Validity.DayReturn: Trainlink C tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket only.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid for travel outside of peak time is 06.31 – 09.29 Monday – Friday, excluding Bank Holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid for travel outside of peak time is 06.31 – 09.29 Monday – Friday, excluding Bank Holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: The bus portion of the ticket is only valid for a return journey, the rail portion of the ticket is valid off peak all day on the date stated on the ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: The bus portion of the ticket is only valid for a return journey, the rail portion of the ticket is valid off peak all day on the date stated on the ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and on the 319 stage coach bus.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
If you bought a ticket and then decide not to travel, you may be entitled to a refund.

To request a refund please complete the Comments form, available from any of our staffed stations or you can download one via the Merseyrail website.

Please send your completed form with your ticket to:

Freepost MERSEYRAIL CUSTOMER RELATIONS

Please ensure that if you are posting a high value ticket that it is sent by recorded delivery. If you hand in your refund form at any of our stations, staff will arrange for it to be forwarded to our Customer Relations Team. If you no longer have your ticket, please contact us.

All applications for refunds requested due to a change of plan will be subject to a £10.00 administration charge.

Child.Note: Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

TicketTypeDescription.SpecialConditions: The bus portion of the ticket is only valid for a return journey, the rail portion of the ticket is valid off peak all day on the date stated on the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Season Ticket to travel by train, bus and some trams in the Greater Manchester area.

Valid on all rail services within the Transport for Greater Manchester (TfGM) area and also beyond TfGM boundary to Disley and New Mills Central / New Mills Newtown.

Valid on most bus services, including night buses, within the TfGM area, anytime to 4am, any day of the week including Bank Holidays.

Can also be used on Metrolink trams but only in the 'City Zone', not the wider tram network.

7CA - 7 day

SCM - 1 month

SCA - Annual

(only the above periods of availability are offered)

Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day.

Validity.TimeReturn: Valid any time on trains, trams (city zone only) and buses (including night buses) in the Greater Manchester area until 4am the following morning.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Season Tickets and Travelcards allow unlimited travel between two stations for a specified period of time. Season Tickets are usually available for periods of seven days or for any period from one month to one year.

Season Tickets and Travelcards offer great savings for regular travelers and where savings can usually be made even if  travelling fewer than 5 days a week. An Annual Season Ticket offers 52 weeks' travel for the price of 40 already-discounted 7-Day Season Tickets and Travelcards.

A Travelcard allow customers to travel to London and enjoy unlimited travel throughout London on National Rail (including Elizabeth line and London Overground services), London Underground, DLR, London Trams and London Bus services within the Zones covered by the ticket.

Validity.DayOutward: Any day and up to 04:29 after expiry date on ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Any day and up to 04:29 after expiry date on ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Unless otherwise stated, Season Tickets and Travelcards are valid at any time
Validity.TimeReturn: Unless otherwise stated, Season Tickets and Travelcards are valid at any time

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of Journey may not be allowed on all permitted routes - see the National Routeing Guide for full details.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of Journey may not be allowed on all permitted routes - see the National Routeing Guide for full details.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Season Ticket holders' names and addresses are recorded.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability:
To prevent any misuse, Season Tickets and Travelcards are not generally issued in advance unless fulfilled to a smartcard or sTicket (barcode), although Travelcard Season Tickets on Oyster can be purchased up to 30 days in advance of the first day of validity.

To minimise inconvenience to customers, the following rules apply to Seasons Tickets and Travelcards issued on printed stock:

NEW TICKETS or RENEWALS following a Break of Continuity

A ticket may be issued:

7-Day Seasons & Travelcards

  • on the start date;
  • if the start date is a Monday (or a Tuesday after a Bank Holiday), the ticket may be issued from 06:00 on the previous Sunday.

Period Seasons & Travelcards

(including Annual & Gold Cards)

  • if the start date is a Saturday, Sunday or Monday (also Tuesday after a Bank Holiday), the ticket may be issued from noon on the previous Friday;
  • if the start date is another day, the ticket may be issued after noon on the previous day.
  • over the Christmas and New Year holiday period these rules are generally relaxed. Please refer to local TOC instructions.

RENEWALS - Where the current ticket is SURRENDERED with NO BREAK OF CONTINUITY

A ticket may be issued:

7-Day Seasons & Travelcards

  • if the start date is a Sunday or Monday (or Tuesday after a Bank Holiday), the ticket may be issued from noon on the previous Friday.
  • if the start date is another day, the ticket may be issued after noon on the previous day.

Period Seasons & Travelcards (including Annual & Gold Cards)

  •  the ticket may be issued any time up to seven days in advance.
  • over the Christmas and New Year holiday period these rules are generally relaxed. Please refer to local TOC instructions.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
Season Tickets and Travelcards can be bought at staffed ticket offices and online.

7-Day Season Tickets and Travelcards can also be bought at some self-service ticket machines.

PLEASE NOTE: Travelcard Seasons valid solely within the London Zones can only be issued on Oyster or train company-issued Smartcard. They are no longer available as a physical ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines:
If buying a physical Season Ticket or Travelcard for the first time, a recent passport-type photograph for the accompanying photocard may be required.

In addition, if buying a monthly or longer Season Ticket for the first time, an application form may need to be completed so that a record can be held by the issuing retailer.

Season Ticket holders' names and addresses are recorded, as well as the validity of the Season Ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If the Season Ticket held does not cover the journey in full, has been left at home or is for Standard Class accommodation when travelling in First Class, the appropriate additional ticket must be purchased at the ticket office before travel.

Season Tickets and Travelcards can be amended during the validity to extend or decrease the area of validity. See Season Ticket Terms and Conditions for details
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
If you no longer need your Season Ticket or Travelcard, it can be returned to the original retailer to be refunded. All Season Ticket or Travelcards must still be within their period of validity to be refunded (exceptions for sickness do not apply on evidence of non-use). You should be aware that a physical Season Ticket or Travelcard will need to be surrendered back to the original issuer, so a refund should only be requested once you have finished using it.

Any refund is calculated from the date after the Season Ticket or Travelcard was returned, and will be the difference between the price paid and the cost of a ticket or tickets for the period for which you have actually used the Season Ticket or Travelcard, up to and including the date a refund is requested. There may also be an administration charge of no more than £10. Because of the discounts on longer term Season Tickets and Travelcards, refunds are not made pro rata to the periods before and after surrender, and annual Season Tickets or Travelcards may have no cash value after 10 months and 12 days.

For Season Tickets or Travelcards held on a smartcard or mobile device, contact the original retailer for advice.

Child.Note:
Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive):  50% discount.

Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note:
• Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: 50% discount
  • 16-17 Saver: 50% discount

Group.Note: None

TicketTypeDescription.SpecialConditions: Full terms and conditions available at https://www.nationalrail.co.uk/tickets-railcards-and-offers/ticket-types/season-tickets/season-tickets-terms-and-conditions/

Freepost MERSEYRAIL CUSTOMER RELATIONS

Please ensure that if you are posting a high value ticket that it is sent by recorded delivery. If you hand in your refund form at any of our stations, staff will arrange for it to be forwarded to our Customer Relations Team. If you no longer have your ticket, please contact us.

All applications for refunds requested due to a change of plan will be subject to a £10.00 administration charge.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Trnlink LIV is a peak combination ticket that covers both the 319 bus to/from Skemersdale and train leg via Headbolt Lane to/from Liverpool.

Validity.DayOutward: Trnlink LIV tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket only.
Validity.DayReturn: Trnlink LIV tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket only.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid for travel at anytime.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid for travel at anytime.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and on the 319 Stagecoach bus.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
If you bought a ticket and then decide not to travel, you may be entitled to a refund.

To request a refund please complete the Comments form, available from any of our staffed stations or you can download one via the Merseyrail website.

Please send your completed form with your ticket to:

Please ensure that if you are posting a high value ticket that it is sent by recorded delivery. If you hand in your refund form at any of our stations, staff will arrange for it to be forwarded to our Customer Relations Team. If you no longer have your ticket, please contact us.

Child.Note: Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Valid for 2 adults and up to 7 children travelling together between Heathrow Airport and London on Heathrow Express services
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Flexible single ticket available from Heathrow Express

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Rail Rover for unlimited travel (subject to time restrictions shown) within the North East Round Robin area of validity.

Valid for one day.

Further information can be found on Northern Rail's website.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Valid after 08:45 weekdays.

Valid any time Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Valid after 08:45 weekdays.

Valid any time Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, rail-appointed travel agents, onboard trains from the conductor if boarding at an unstaffed station, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Rover tickets may not be issued more than 3 days in advance of their validity (5 days for telesales and internet bookings).
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: The ticket has unlimited travel on the day(s) of validity but you cannot change the travel validity period of the ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refunds are only given on Rail Rover tickets returned before the start date shown on them and are subject to an administration charge.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: 50% Child discounts.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A Standard Class return ticket which can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.

Validity.DayOutward: Super Off-Peak Day tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket. Outward travel is valid on any train after 18.30 every day.  Return travel is valid upto and including the first departing train next morning back to the ticket origin. Not valid to any other destination.
Validity.DayReturn: Super Off-Peak Day tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket. Outward travel is valid on any train after 18.30 every day.  Return travel is valid upto and including the first departing train next morning back to the ticket origin. Not valid to any other destination.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is allowed on Super-Off Peak Day tickets unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: A break of journey is allowed on Super-Off Peak Day tickets unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: This ticket can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: You may change your travel plans at any time within the validity of the ticket without having to pay an additional fare. There are time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

A full refund is available if you return the completely unused ticket to the place of purchase within 28 days of expiry of the ticket. The amount of the refund will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): No discount. Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger.  Railcard discounts: Disabled Persons Railcard: No discount, Family & Friends Railcard: No discount, HM Forces Railcard: No discount, Network Railcard: No discount, Senior Railcard: No discount, Two Together: No discount and 16-25 Railcard: No discount. Other discount cards: Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: No discount and Gold Cards: No discount.

RailCard.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): No discount. Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger.  Railcard discounts: Disabled Persons Railcard: No discount, Family & Friends Railcard: No discount, HM Forces Railcard: No discount, Network Railcard: No discount, Senior Railcard: No discount, Two Together: No discount and 16-25 Railcard: No discount. Other discount cards: Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: No discount and Gold Cards: No discount.

Group.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): No discount. Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger.  Railcard discounts: Disabled Persons Railcard: No discount, Family & Friends Railcard: No discount, HM Forces Railcard: No discount, Network Railcard: No discount, Senior Railcard: No discount, Two Together: No discount and 16-25 Railcard: No discount. Other discount cards: Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: No discount and Gold Cards: No discount.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Web Duo Gatwick Express Anytime Return
TicketTypeDescription.Description: Anytime Return offer for two people travelling together from London Victoria to Gatwick Airport on Gatwick Express services with no restrictions on when you can travel.

Validity.DayOutward: The outward portions of the Web Duo Gatwick Express Anytime Return are valid for five days including the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Return portions of the Web Duo Gatwick Express Anytime Return tickets are valid for travel until 04:29 in the early morning following the last day of validity (i.e. one calendar month from outward journey). All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid anytime on weekdays, weekends and bank holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid anytime on weekdays, weekends and bank holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Web Duo Gatwick Express Anytime Return tickets are only available for journeys originating from London Victoria to Gatwick Airport.

They are not valid for journeys originating at Gatwick Airport.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Web Duo Gatwick Express Anytime Return tickets are only available online from the Gatwick Express website www.gatwickexpress.com.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be bought online before boarding the train at London Victoria from the Gatwick Express website www.gatwickexpress.com.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

See 'Refunds' section

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Weekend Return on South Western Railway servcies
Greater Anglia are introducing the Weekend Return valid Friday or Saturday - Monday. Travel from London to a number of destinations across the Greater Anglia network at any time, and return any time.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability:
Available to purchase in advance and on the day.

Return travel permitted ant any time Saturday, Sunday or Monday.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Rail Rover for unlimited travel (subject to time restrictions shown) within the West Midlands area of validity.

Further information can be found on West Midland Railway's website.

This Rover is not valid on buses or Midland Metro tram services. If travel is required on these services then a 'nNetwork Daytripper' may be more appropriate.

Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day, as dated on the ticket.

Valid after 09:00 Monday to Fridays.

Valid any time Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

A Family ticket is available for up to 2 adults and 4 children. There are no additional discounts for Family & Friends Railcard holders.

Validity.DayReturn: Valid for travel on trains and buses in the West Yorkshire PTE area centred on Leeds from 0930 Monday-Friday and any time on Saturdays, Sundays and public holidays. Not valid in Rail and Bus Zones 6 & 7.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Standard Class single rail travel by London North Eastern Railway services plus an upgrade to First Class, subject to availability.

One free seat reservation.

Complimentary food and drink in First Class, according to the day, time of day and journey. Subject to availability.

Complimentary access to London North Eastern Railway First Class lounges.

Validity.DayOutward:
Valid only on date of ticket. 

Valid on London North Eastern Railway services only.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
You may not start, break and resume, or end your journey at any intermediate station except to change to/from connecting trains as shown on the ticket(s) or other valid travel itinerary.

Exceptions: A ticket to or from Leeds can start, end or break journey at Wakefield Westgate.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: This ticket is a hybrid ticket aimed mainly at business travellers. It is issued as a Standard class ticket (as many Corporate travel policies filter out First Class travel) but contains an upgrade to First Class, to be used with the ticket. Reservations are booked into First Class.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Can be bought up to 23.59 on the day before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
You may change your travel plans at any time on the date shown on your ticket without having to pay an additional fare. There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.

Where the date of travel is changed, the ticket should be re-issued by the retailer showing the correct date.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Completely unused and returned complete - full refund less any local cancellation charges.

Partially completed journeys - deduct the equivalent First Class Anytime Single fare plus any local cancellation fees and refund the remainder.

Travel by a different London North Eastern Railway train to the one reserved and no First Class seats available - flat £20 refund.

Complimentary food and drink not provided - no refund, as complimentary.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Season Ticket valid for unlimited travel on trains and buses in West Yorkshire PTE zonal areas.

West Yorkshire Metrocard is valid for unlimited travel on trains between all stations in the West Yorkshire PTE area and most bus services throughout the county.

There are a number of different zones (or combination of zones) available as well as a 'Bus Only' version which is not valid on trains. For more details, see the West Yorkshire Metro website.

MCW - 7 days

MCM - 1 month

MCQ - 3 month

MCA - Annual

Validity.DayOutward: Any day within the ticket's validity and within the zones chosen.
Validity.DayReturn: Any day within the ticket's validity and within the zones chosen.
Validity.TimeOutward: No time restriction.
Validity.TimeReturn: No time restriction.

Metro, Concessions and Integrated Ticketing DepartmentWest Yorkshire Combined Authority40-50 Wellington StreetLeedsLS1 2DE

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
The Wherry Line Ranger offers one day's unlimited travel on Greater Anglia services from Norwich, through the Norfolk Broads to the coastal resorts of Lowestoft and Great Yarmouth (via Reedham and via Acle).

Tickets are valid from 08:45 on Mondays to Fridays and at any time on Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

There are also Family and Senior versions available.

WRR - Adult

WRF - Family (no Railcard required)

WRS - Senior (no Railcard required)

Validity.DayOutward: Available any day. Valid on date on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Available any day. Valid on date on the ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid after 08:45 Monday-Friday and all day Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid after 08:45 Monday-Friday and all day Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Station ticket offices, some travel agents, Greater Anglia (and selected other train company) telesales, on board the Norwich to Great Yarmouth or Lowestoft trains if boarding at an unstaffed station.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Reservations are not required.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Tickets are refunded if surrendered before the date shown on the ticket. An administration charge may be deducted.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Unaccompanied children pay half-fare.

Accompanied children pay £2.00 each.

RailCard.Note:
All national Railcards (except JobCentre Plus cards) give their normal discount.

Annual Gold Cards give 1/3 discount.

GA Club 50 Cards give 10% discount.

There are Senior and Family versions of this ticket that do not require a Railcard.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Anytime Carnet ticket for 10 return journeys

Validity.DayOutward: Outward portions of Carnet tickets are available for travel on any day within one month of the date on the ticket. The ticket must be validated before travel by entering the date of travel in the boxes shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Return portions of Carnet tickets are available for travel on any day within one month of the date on the ticket. The ticket must be validated before travel by entering the date of travel in the boxes shown on the ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid on any train
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid on any train

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Generally the same as 'Anytime day tickets', except that tickets are valid for use within two months, but are not refundable.  Expiry date is shown on each ticket.

Route and/or Train Company validity is as shown on the ticket routeing.

Can be bought at any time up to departure but cannot be bought on board trains.

Each carnet coupon must be validated for travel before the customer boards the train by filling in the date in the boxes provided.  When self-validating a Carnet/flexi-season ticket, customers must endorse the date with 2-digit numerical values only, e.g. 22 02 for travel on 22 February, using permanent black ink. If a customer has not dated a carnet ticket before boarding, they will be charged the full fare for the journey being made.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Can currently only be purchased at station Ticket Offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: No changes
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Non refundable

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Carnet Ticket for 5 return journeys

Validity.DayOutward: Each carnet ticket is valid for travel any day within 3 months of the date on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Each carnet ticket is valid for travel any day within 3 months of the date on the ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid on any London Northwestern Railway or West Midlands Railway service
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid on any London Northwestern Railway or West Midlands Railway service

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Generally the same as 'Anytime tickets', except that tickets are valid for three months, but are not refundable

Route and/or Train Company validity is as shown on the ticket routeing.

Can be bought at any time up to departure but cannot be bought on board trains.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any Day
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Available through business sales channels only - currently suspended
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: No changes
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Non refundable

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Xmas Market Day Return (South Western Railway)
TicketTypeDescription.Description: This is an Off Peak Day Return ticket for travel on South Western Railway services.

Validity.DayOutward: Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.DayReturn: Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeOutward: This ticket is valid at any time.
Validity.TimeReturn: This ticket is valid at any time.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is permitted on the outward portion off an Off-Peak Day Return unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: A break of journey is permitted on the return portion off an Off-Peak Day Return unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: • The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.Off-Peak Day tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare. All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
