TicketTypeDescription.Description: LNER Complimentary Return (First Class) products offer a complimentary first class return journey for travel on a specified LNER services.  They are only available directly through the LNER website, and are only offered at the sole discretion of LNER.  Tickets are not valid on the services of any other operator.

Validity.DayOutward: Date shown on ticket
Validity.DayReturn: Date on ticket
Validity.TimeOutward: Time shown on ticket
Validity.TimeReturn: Time shown on ticket

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: LNER Complimentary Return (First Class) products are to be offered at the sole discretion of LNER, offered only through the LNER website and are valid for use on the date/train services shown within First Class.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Subject to availability
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: London North Eastern Railway (LNER) website only at the discretion of LNER only.  Not for general sale.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Offered subject to availability, until reservations close for the train requested
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Tickets should be cancelled and rebooked.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Tickets are complimentary - no policy required

TicketTypeDescription.Description: LNER Complimentary Single (First Class) products offer a complimentary first class single journey for travel on a specified LNER service.  They are only available directly through the LNER website, and are only offered at the sole discretion of LNER.  Tickets are not valid on the services of any other operator.

Validity.DayOutward: Date shown on ticket
Validity.TimeOutward: Shown on ticket

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: LNER Complimentary Single (First Class) products are to be offered at the sole discretion of LNER, offered only through the LNER website and are valid for use on the date/train service shown within First Class.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Subject to availability
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: London North Eastern Railway (LNER) website only at the discretion of LNER only.  Not for general sale.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Offered subject to availability, until reservations close for the train requested
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Tickets should be cancelled and rebooked.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Tickets are complimentary - no policy required

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Inclusive day out ticket available on Saturdays only.

Validity.DayOutward: Date shown on ticket
Validity.DayReturn: Date shown on ticket
Validity.TimeOutward: Time shown on ticket
Validity.TimeReturn: Time shown on ticket

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Available on East Midlands Trains services to London Fares Zones 1-6 only.

Valid on Saturdays ONLY.

London Day out ticket includes:

First class day return rail travel on East Midlands Trains services

Breakfast on selected East Midlands Trains services to London St Pancras International. Breakfast services originate at Sheffield departing at 07:29, 07:37 or 08:29.

All Zones One Day Travelcard.

24-hour Car Parking (except at London St. Pancras).

Car park vouchers

Customers should be advised to display the car park voucher in car windscreen. At barrier operated car parks, customers will need to present their voucher together with the machine issued ticket at the booking office for validation.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets are valid ONLY on the date and train service(s) shown on the ticket(s). Where applicable, you must travel in the class and reserved seat(s) shown on the ticket(s).
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: 19:00 day before
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: No refunds

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note: Child discounts, Senior, 16-25 , HM Forces and Disabled Persons Railcards discounts apply.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A semi-flexible day return from selected Greater Anglia station to Stratford and London Liverpool Street. Available in First Class (OFK) and Standard Class (OSK) versions.

Validity.DayOutward: Booked train only
Validity.DayReturn: Same day as outward travel
Validity.TimeOutward: Available on trains departing after 13:59
Validity.TimeReturn: Any service departing after 19:04

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Not permitted.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: This is a semi-flexible return that is train-specific on the outward journey but flexible on the return journey, which has to be on the same day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Available at station booking offices and online through websites and apps.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Book by 23:59 on the day before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Changes before departure of the booked train are possible for a fee.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refunds are not available.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A semi-flexible day return from selected Greater Anglia station to Stratford (London) and London Liverpool Street.

Validity.DayOutward: Booked train only
Validity.DayReturn: Same day as outward travel
Validity.TimeOutward: Available on trains departing after 13:59
Validity.TimeReturn: Available on trains departing after 19:04

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: This is a semi-flexible return that is train-specific on the outward journey but flexible on the return journey, which has to be on the same day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Available at station booking offices and online through websites and apps.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Book by 23:59 on the day before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Changes before departure of the booked train are possible for a fee.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refunds are not available

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
A semi-flexible overnight return ticket to London and Stratford (London) from stations on the Great Eastern Main Line (Norwich to Colchester) and branches. Travel outwards in the afternoon or evening towards London on a specified train and return the following day on any train departing before 12.01 except between 06.59 and 08.01. If the return day is a Saturday, Sunday or Bank Holiday, return at any time before 12.01.

Tickets are available in First Class (OFL) and Standard Class (OSL) versions.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on first day of validity only.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid to return on the day after the outward journey only.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid on the booked train only. Available trains are from early afternoon onwards. The exact time of the first valid train varies from station to station. Journey planners will show when the ticket is available.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid on any train scheduled to depart before 06.59 and after 08.01, but before 12.01 (any time before 12.01 if the return journey is on a Saturday, Sunday or Bank Holiday).

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Limited availability
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company websites and apps, and selected rail appointed travel agents.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Available until 23.59 on the day before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Outward journey time can be changed on payment of a fee and any difference in fare. Return journey time after the expiry of validity can be changed on payment of the difference in fare.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refunds are not available.

Child.Note:
Children aged 5 to 15 inclusive: 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 may travel free of charge with each fare-paying adult.

RailCard.Note:
1/3 off with the following Railcards:

  • 16-25,
  • Family & Friends,
  • Senior,
  • HM Forces,
  • Disabled Persons,
  • Veterans,
  • Two Together

TicketTypeDescription.Description: London North Eastern Railway Flat Fare First Class

Validity.DayOutward: As shown on ticket
Validity.TimeOutward: As shown on ticket

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Travel on the date and time displayed on the ticket
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets sold subject to availability.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Online and via app only.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Prior to the close of reservations
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: No changes
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: No refunds.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: London North Eastern Railway Promo First Class

Validity.DayOutward: On date shown
Validity.TimeOutward: At time shown on reservation

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Travel on the date and time displayed on the ticket
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Subject to availability
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: London North Eastern Railway Website
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Prior to the close of reservations
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: No Changes allowed
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: No refunds.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: London North Eastern Railway Weekend First Supplement
TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Supplement to upgrade a Standard Class ticket to First Class on London North Eastern Railway at weekends.

The cost of a London North Eastern Railway Weekend Upgrade varies depending on the journey:

  • Between London/Stevenage and Doncaster/Selby/Brough/Hull or any stations in between - £39 per single journey.
  • Between London/Stevenage and York/Wakefield or any stations further north - £49 per single journey.
  • For LNER journeys wholly north of Stevenage - £39 per single journey.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid only on Saturday, Sunday or Bank Holiday.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid only on Saturday, Sunday or Bank Holiday.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid only on the specific train that it was puchased and any connecting London North Eastern Railway service.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid only on the specific train that it was puchased and any connecting London North Eastern Railway service.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is not allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays, subject to availability and capacity.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Onboard a London North Eastern Railway service only, subject to availability and capacity. Supplement may not be available if the service is already very busy.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Can only be purchased onboard the train, subject to availability and capacity. Cannot be purchased in advance.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Supplement is valid only on the service that it has been purchased.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Refunds are not offered.

Child.Note: No discounts.

RailCard.Note: No discounts.

Group.Note: No discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Off-Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may need to travel at specific times of the day, days of the week and sometimes on specific routes or operators.

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket will depend on the journey you are making and you will be advised when buying your ticket. The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

Validity.DayOutward: Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.DayReturn: Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

Off-Peak Day tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, change the date of travel, or change the time you are allowed to travel.

Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey.

NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note:
Two Together, Disabled Persons Railcard, HM Forces Railcard and Senior Railcard all offer 1/3 off.

Minimum fares / time restrictions may apply to tickets bought with a Railcard or other discount card. See terms and conditions of the appropriate Railcard or discount card for details.

Railcard holders travelling on a discounted ticket must carry their Railcard when they travel. If a Railcard holder fails to produce their valid Railcard with their ticket, they will be required to pay a full priced ticket for their journey as if no Railcard and/or no ticket were held.

Group.Note: Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) 50% discount. Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger. Railcard discounts: Two Together, 16-25 Railcard: No discount, Disabled Persons Railcard: 1/3 off, Family & Friends Railcard: No discount, HM Forces Railcard: 1/3 off, Network Railcard : No discount and  Senior Railcard: 1/3 off. Other discount cards: Gold Cards : No discount and Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: No discount. NOTE: Minimum fares / time restrictions may apply to tickets bought with a Railcard or other discount card. See terms and conditions of the appropriate Railcard or discount card for details.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Off-Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may need to travel at specific times of the day, days of the week and sometimes on specific routes or operators.

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket will depend on the journey you are making and you will be advised when buying your ticket. The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

An Off-Peak Day Travelcard allow customers to travel to London and enjoy unlimited travel throughout London on National Rail, London Underground, DLR, London Trams and London Bus services within Zones 1-6.

Validity.DayOutward: Off-Peak Day Travelcards are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.DayReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Off-Peak Day Travelcards are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Within the London Fares Zones, the ticket is valid for unlimited travel.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote:
Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Within the London Fares Zones, the ticket is valid for unlimited travel.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Off-Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may need to travel at specific times of the day, days of the week and sometimes on specific routes or operators.

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket will depend on the journey you are making and you will be advised when buying your ticket. The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

Validity.TimeOutward: Off-Peak Return tickets are valid for return travel within one calendar month from the start date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 in the morning after last day of validity.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Overnight break of journey - if the journey cannot be completed on the same day, the ticket may be used to continue the journey on the following day.

Unless otherwise indicated in the relevant restriction code, time restrictions apply as from the initial origin station on both days.

The appropriate restrictions for the actual day on which travel is being undertaken apply (for example, it may be that if day 1 is on Sunday, no restrictions apply, but on day 2, the Monday-Friday restrictions apply). All travel must be completed by 04:29 in the morning after this second day.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote:
Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Overnight break of journey - if the journey cannot be completed on the same day, the ticket may be used to continue the journey on the following day.

Unless otherwise indicated in the relevant restriction code, time restrictions apply as from the initial origin station on both days.

The appropriate restrictions for the actual day on which travel is being undertaken apply (for example, it may be that if day 1 is on Sunday, no restrictions apply, but on day 2, the Monday-Friday restrictions apply). All travel must be completed by 04:29 in the morning after this second day.

Validity.TimeOutward:

Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability:
Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.

Tickets can be purchased in advance of, or immediately before, travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, change the date of travel, or change the time you are allowed to travel.

Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey.

NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Rail Air Return tickets are fully flexible walk-up tickets with no restrictions on when you can travel.

Validity.DayOutward: Rail Air Return tickets are valid for outward travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day.
Validity.DayReturn: Rail Air Return tickets are valid for return travel within one calendar month from the start date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 after last day of validity.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid to travel at any time of the day.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid to travel at any time of the day.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
• Rail Air tickets can be used at any time but may require you to travel via a specific route or on a specific train company and this will be clearly stated on the ticket.
  • If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made.
  • Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day.
  • Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.
  • All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Only available to customers booking ticket between the Channel Islands and Southampton Airport through FlyBe/Blue Islands. Tickets must be purchased online and are not available at stations or self-service ticket machines.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable.

If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Special fares for customers of certain airlines flying to Southampton International Airport.

Validity.DayOutward: Rail Air tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid to travel at any time of the day.
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is allowed.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Rail Air tickets can be used at any time but may require you to travel via a specific route or on a specific train company and this will be clearly stated on the ticket.

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Only available to customers booking flights to Southampton Airport through specific airlines Tickets must be purchased online and are not available at stations or self-service ticket machines.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: First Class Rail & Helicopter Extended Stay Return ticket to the Scilly Isles

Validity.DayOutward: Valid for outward travel on the date on the ticket and by the reserved train and for return travel within 1 month of that date and by the reserved train.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid for outward travel on the date on the ticket and by the reserved train and for return travel within 1 month of that date and by the reserved train.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of train journey is not permitted.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of train journey is not permitted.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Available for journeys from selected Great Western Railway stations to the Isles of Scilly.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Only available from Great Western Railway telesales.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Must be purchased at least 7 days before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Changes to the booked helicopter flight must be agreed with Great Western Railway telesales before the start of the journey.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Wholly unused tickets are refunded. An administration charge is deducted. to the rail portion of the ticket only. The helicopter portion is only refunded if helicopter reservations are cancelled with Great Western Railway telesales before the helicopter leaves.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: No further discounts.

RailCard.Note: No further discounts.

Group.Note: No further discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Scilly Isles Holiday Co Inclusive Ticket (First Class)
TicketTypeDescription.Description: First Class Return ticket to Penzance

Validity.DayOutward: Contact Scilly Isles Holiday Company.
Validity.DayReturn: Contact Scilly Isles Holiday Company.
Validity.TimeOutward: Contact Scilly Isles Holiday Company.
Validity.TimeReturn: Contact Scilly Isles Holiday Company.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Contact Scilly Isles Holiday Company.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Only available through Isles of Scilly Holiday Company.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Contact Scilly Isles Holiday Company.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Contact Scilly Isles Holiday Company.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Contact Scilly Isles Holiday Company.

Child.Note: Child discounts only.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Season Ticket - First Plus add-on (Network West Midlands)
TicketTypeDescription.Description:
First Plus Rail Add-On to your all zones

network ticket, valid on a number of First Class services between all rail zones in the 'network' (Network West Midlands PTE area.

Tickets can be bought for periods 1 week, 4 weeks and 52 weeks (annual).

Full details of the full range of tickets, periods of validity, zones and maps can be found on the Network West Midlands website.

1WK - 1 week

4WK - 4 weeks

ACP - Annual

Validity.DayOutward: Valid any day within the ticket's validity.
Validity.DayReturn: Valid any day within the ticket's validity.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid any time within the zones stated on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid any time within the zones stated on the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
You can apply for a refund on a

Network Season Ticket valid for seven days or more as long as your ticket still has at least seven days validity remaining or three days for seven day season tickets.

The outstanding validity of the ticket will be refunded and is calculated from the date we receive the ticket less a £10 administration fee.

To get a refund please follow the instructions below. Please note you will need to supply your original ticket not a copy:

- For tickets bought at the station you can apply for a refund at your local station

- For tickets bought online, log on to your online account and follow on-screen instructions

- For tickets bought online at www.networkwestmidlands.com or via the Corporate Sales Scheme can write to: Direct Sales, Centro House, 16 Summer Lane, Birmingham B19 3SD.

- For more information visit www.londonnorthwesternrailway.co.uk or www.westmidlandsrailway.co.uk
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
Available from the '

network' Travel Shop at Birmingham New Street station as well as at any staffed station in the Network West Midlands area.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Photocard required for purchase of Season Tickets.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Day tickets bought by passengers who forget their Season Tickets will not be refunded.

Child.Note: No discounts.

RailCard.Note: No discounts.

Group.Note: No discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Season Tickets allow unlimited travel between two stations for a specified period of time. Season Tickets are usually available for periods of seven days or for any period from one month to one year.

Season Tickets offer great savings for regular travelers and where savings can usually be made even if  travelling fewer than 5 days a week. An Annual Season ticket gives offers 52 weeks' travel for the price of 40 already-discounted Weekly Season Tickets.

Validity.DayOutward: Any day and up to 04:29 after expiry date on ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Any day and up to 04:29 after expiry date on ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Any time of day.
Validity.TimeReturn: Any time of day.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of Journey may not be allowed on all permitted routes. Restrictions will be advised at point of purchase.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Season Ticket holders' names and addresses are recorded (Monthly or longer).
TicketTypeDescription.Availability:
To prevent any misuse, Season Tickets and Travelcards are not generally issued in advance, although Seasons on Smartcard and Travelcard Season on Oyster, can be issued up to 30 days in advance of the first day of validity.

To minimise inconvenience to customers, the following rules apply to Season Tickets issued on printed stock:

NEW TICKETS

(including renewing Season Tickets following a break of continuity)           

A Ticket may be issued:

7-Day Seasons &

Travelcards

  •  on the start date;
  • if the start date is a Monday (or a Tuesday after a Bank Holiday), the Ticket may be issued from 06:00 on the previous Sunday.

Period Seasons & Travelcards

(including Annual & Gold Cards)

  • if the start date is a Saturday, Sunday or Monday (also Tuesday after a Bank Holiday), the Ticket may be issued from 12:00 (noon) on the previous Friday;
  • if the start date is another day, the Ticket may be issued after 12:00 (noon) on the previous day.
  • over the Christmas and New Year holiday period these rules are generally relaxed. Please refer to local TOC instructions.

RENEWALS

Where the current ticket is SURRENDERED* with NO BREAK OF CONTINUITY

A Ticket may be issued:

7-Day Seasons & Travelcards

  • if the start date is a Sunday or Monday (or Tuesday after a Bank Holiday), the ticket may be issued from 12:00 (noon) on the previous Friday.
  • if the start date is another day, the ticket may be issued after 12:00 (noon) on the previous day.

Period Seasons & Travelcards (including Annual & Gold Cards)

  •  the Ticket may be issued any time up to seven days in advance.
  • over the Christmas and New Year holiday period these rules are generally relaxed. Please refer to local TOC instructions.

*Surrendered to retailer used for current Season Ticket
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Season Tickets can be bought at any staffed National Rail ticket office or on the website from the appropriate Train Company. 7-Day Season Tickets can also be purchased at most self-service ticket machines. Season Tickets can also be bought over the telephone from some retailers.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines:
If buying a Season Ticket for the first time, a recent passport-type photograph for the accompanying photocard is required.

In addition, if buying a monthly or longer Season Ticket for the first time, an application form will need to be completed so that a Record Card can be held by the issuing Train Company.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: If the Season Ticket held does not cover the journey in full, or has been left at home, or is for Standard class accommodation when travelling in First Class, the appropriate additional ticket must be purchased before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: For refund information and all Season Ticket terms and conditions, - https://www.nationalrail.co.uk/tickets-railcards-and-offers/ticket-types/season-tickets/season-tickets-terms-and-conditions/

RailCard.Note: In very limited circumstances. See Railcard terms and conditions for details of applicable discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.SpecialConditions: Season Tickets held as 'smart' tickets (barcoded or on a smartcard) may have their own special terms and conditions that differ to physical 'paper' Season Tickets. More information can be found at https://www.nationalrail.co.uk/tickets-railcards-and-offers/ticket-types/season-tickets/

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A First Class weekend return ticket which can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.

Validity.DayOutward: The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket (which must be a Friday or a Saturday).
Validity.DayReturn: The return journey must be made on a Saturday or Sunday of the same weekend.
Validity.TimeOutward: Time restrictions apply for outward travel on a Friday, there are no time restrictions on when you can travel on a Saturday.
Validity.TimeReturn: There are no time restrictions for return journeys on a Saturday or Sunday.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is not allowed on the outward portion of the ticket.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: A break of journey is allowed on the return portion of the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: This ticket can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: You may change your travel plans at any time within the validity of the ticket without having to pay an additional fare.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

A full refund is available if you return the completely unused ticket to the place of purchase within 28 days of expiry of the ticket. The amount of the refund will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): 50% discount. Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger. Railcard discounts: Disabled Persons Railcard: No discount, Family & Friends Railcard: No discount, HM Forces Railcard: No discount, Network Railcard: No discount, Senior Railcard: No discount and 16-25 Railcard: No discount. Other discount cards: Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: No discount and Gold Cards: No discount.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): 50% discount. Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger. Railcard discounts: Disabled Persons Railcard: No discount, Family & Friends Railcard: No discount, HM Forces Railcard: No discount, Network Railcard: No discount, Senior Railcard: No discount and 16-25 Railcard: No discount. Other discount cards: Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: No discount and Gold Cards: No discount.

TicketTypeDescription.TicketTypeName: Super Off Peak Day Single (First Class)
TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Super Off Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may need to travel at specific times of the day, days of the week and sometimes on specific routes or operators.

The times when you may use your Super Off Peak ticket will depend on the journey you are making and you will be advised when buying your ticket. The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

Validity.DayOutward: Super Off-Peak Day Single tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: • Super Off Peak tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Super Off Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, change the date of travel, or change the time you are allowed to travel.

Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey.

NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.

For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable.

If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Off-Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may be required to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route.

Validity.DayOutward:
Super Off-Peak Return tickets are valid for outward travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day.

If you cannot complete your journey on the same day you will be allowed to finish it the following day - see 'Break of journey' below.
Validity.DayReturn: Super Off-Peak Return tickets are valid for return travel within one calendar month from the start date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 after last day of validity.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
Break of journey is permitted on the outward portion of a Super Off-Peak Return unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Overnight break of journey - if the journey cannot be completed in this time, the ticket may be used to continue the journey on the following day. Unless otherwise indicated in the relevant restriction code, time restrictions apply as from the initial origin station on both days. The appropriate restrictions for the actual day on which travel is being undertaken apply (for example, it may be that if day 1 is on Sunday, no restrictions apply, but on day 2, the Monday-Friday restrictions apply). All travel must be completed by 04:29 in the morning after this second day.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote:
Break of journey is permitted on the return portion of a Super Off-Peak Return unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Overnight break of journey - if the journey cannot be completed in this time, the ticket may be used to continue the journey on the following day. Unless otherwise indicated in the relevant restriction code, time restrictions apply as from the initial origin station on both days. The appropriate restrictions for the actual day on which travel is being undertaken apply (for example, it may be that if day 1 is on Sunday, no restrictions apply, but on day 2, the Monday-Friday restrictions apply). All travel must be completed by 04:29 in the morning after this second day.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: • The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.Off-Peak tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: None

Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey.

NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Off-Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may need to travel at specific times of the day, days of the week and sometimes on specific routes or operators.

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket will depend on the journey you are making and you will be advised when buying your ticket. The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

Validity.DayOutward: Super Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.DayReturn: Super Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.

Super Off-Peak Day tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Super Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare.  

If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted.

All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.

Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, change the date of travel, or change the time you are allowed to travel.

Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey.

NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable.

If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket will depend on the journey you are making and you will be advised when buying your ticket. The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

Validity.DayOutward: Super Off-Peak Day Travelcards are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning. All travel must be completed by this time.

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
A break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Within the London Fares Zones, the ticket is valid for unlimited travel.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:

The times when you may use your Super Off-Peak ticket will depend on the journey you are making and you will be advised when buying your ticket. The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey.

Validity.DayOutward:
Super Off-Peak Single tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning.

If you cannot complete your journey on the same day you will be allowed to finish it the following day - see 'Break of journey' below.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward:
The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. 

The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection.

The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code].

You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
Break of journey is permitted unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

Overnight break of journey - if the journey cannot be completed on the same day, the ticket may be used to continue the journey on the following day.

Unless otherwise indicated in the relevant restriction code, time restrictions apply as from the initial origin station on both days.

The appropriate restrictions for the actual day on which travel is being undertaken apply (for example, it may be that if day 1 is on Sunday, no restrictions apply, but on day 2, the Monday-Friday restrictions apply). All travel must be completed by 04:29 in the morning after this second day.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.
