Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

RailCard.Note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

Group.Note: Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) 50% discount. Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger. Railcard discounts: Two Together, 16-25 Railcard: No discount, Disabled Persons Railcard: 1/3 off, Family & Friends Railcard: No discount, HM Forces Railcard: 1/3 off, Network Railcard : No discount and Senior Railcard: 1/3 off. Other discount cards: Gold Cards : No discount and Jobcentre Plus Travel Discount Card: No discount. NOTE: Minimum fares / time restrictions may apply to tickets bought with a Railcard or other discount card. See terms and conditions of the appropriate Railcard or discount card for details.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Return tickets with compulsory outward reservations and no sales on the day of travel

Validity.DayOutward: TPE Corporate (First Class) tickets are valid for outward travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day.
Validity.DayReturn: TPE Corporate (First Class) tickets are valid for return travel for one calendar month from the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day.

TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Online and Telesales
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
• If you wish to change your date or time of travel you can do so through your retailer up until one day before departure. Change of Train fees may apply.
  • On the day of departure if you choose not to travel on your booked outward train you will need to purchase the appropriate walk-up ticket for the train on which you travel.

​​​​​​​
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Season Tickets and Travelcards allow unlimited travel between two stations for a specified period of time. Season Tickets are usually available for periods of seven days or for any period from one month to one year.

Season Tickets and Travelcards offer great savings for regular travelers and where savings can usually be made even if  travelling fewer than 5 days a week. An Annual Season Ticket offers 52 weeks' travel for the price of 40 already-discounted 7-Day Season Tickets and Travelcards.

A Travelcard allow customers to travel to London and enjoy unlimited travel throughout London on National Rail (including Elizabeth line and London Overground services), London Underground, DLR, London Trams and London Bus services within the Zones covered by the ticket.

Validity.DayOutward: Any day and up to 04:29 after expiry date on ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Any day and up to 04:29 after expiry date on ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Unless otherwise stated, Season Tickets and Travelcards are valid at any time
Validity.TimeReturn: Unless otherwise stated, Season Tickets and Travelcards are valid at any time

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of Journey is permitted

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Season Ticket holders' names and addresses are recorded.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability:
To prevent any misuse, Season Tickets and Travelcards are not generally issued in advance unless fulfilled to a smartcard or sTicket (barcode), although Travelcard Season Tickets on Oyster can be purchased up to 30 days in advance of the first day of validity.

To minimise inconvenience to customers, the following rules apply to Seasons Tickets and Travelcards issued on printed stock:

NEW TICKETS or RENEWALS following a Break of Continuity

A ticket may be issued:

7-Day Seasons & Travelcards

  • on the start date;
  • if the start date is a Monday (or a Tuesday after a Bank Holiday), the ticket may be issued from 06:00 on the previous Sunday.

Period Seasons & Travelcards

(including Annual & Gold Cards)

  • if the start date is a Saturday, Sunday or Monday (also Tuesday after a Bank Holiday), the ticket may be issued from noon on the previous Friday;
  • if the start date is another day, the ticket may be issued after noon on the previous day.
  • over the Christmas and New Year holiday period these rules are generally relaxed. Please refer to local TOC instructions.

RENEWALS - Where the current ticket is SURRENDERED with NO BREAK OF CONTINUITY

A ticket may be issued:

7-Day Seasons & Travelcards

  • if the start date is a Sunday or Monday (or Tuesday after a Bank Holiday), the ticket may be issued from noon on the previous Friday.
  • if the start date is another day, the ticket may be issued after noon on the previous day.

Period Seasons & Travelcards (including Annual & Gold Cards)

  •  the ticket may be issued any time up to seven days in advance.
  • over the Christmas and New Year holiday period these rules are generally relaxed. Please refer to local TOC instructions.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
Season Tickets and Travelcards can be bought at staffed ticket offices and online.

7-Day Season Tickets and Travelcards can also be bought at most self-service ticket machines.

PLEASE NOTE: Travelcard Seasons valid solely within the London Zones can only be issued on Oyster or train company-issued Smartcard. They are no longer available as a physical 'paper' ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines:
If buying a physical Season Ticket or Travelcard for the first time, a recent passport-type photograph for the accompanying photocard may be required.

In addition, if buying a monthly or longer Season Ticket for the first time, an application form may need to be completed so that a record can be held by the issuing retailer.

Season Ticket holders' names and addresses are recorded, as well as the validity of the Season Ticket.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
If the Season Ticket held does not cover the journey in full, has been left at home or is for Standard Class accommodation when travelling in First Class, the appropriate additional ticket must be purchased at the ticket office before travel.

Season Tickets and Travelcards can be amended during the validity to extend or decrease the area of validity. See Season Ticket Terms and Conditions for details
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
If you no longer need your Season Ticket or Travelcard, it can be returned to the original retailer to be refunded. All Season Ticket or Travelcards must still be within their period of validity to be refunded (exceptions for sickness do not apply on evidence of non-use). You should be aware that a physical Season Ticket or Travelcard will need to be surrendered back to the original issuer, so a refund should only be requested once you have finished using it.

Any refund is calculated from the date after the Season Ticket or Travelcard was returned, and will be the difference between the price paid and the cost of a ticket or tickets for the period for which you have actually used the Season Ticket or Travelcard, up to and including the date a refund is requested. There may also be an administration charge of no more than £10. Because of the discounts on longer term Season Tickets and Travelcards, refunds are not made pro rata to the periods before and after surrender, and annual Season Tickets or Travelcards may have no cash value after 10 months and 12 days.

For Season Tickets or Travelcards held on a smartcard or mobile device, contact the original retailer for advice.
TicketTypeDescription.SpecialConditions: Full terms and conditions available at https://www.nationalrail.co.uk/tickets-railcards-and-offers/ticket-types/season-tickets/season-tickets-terms-and-conditions/

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Holders of all Standard Class tickets and passes (including Staff Travel facilities, UK and foreign) may travel in extra comfort on many Great Western Railway services on Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays in First Class accommodation.

Validity.DayOutward: Saturdays, Sundays, and Bank Holidays
Validity.TimeOutward: Anytime

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
For journeys which involves a change of train, one Weekend First (GWR) is payable for the entire journey, rather than per leg, provided that the entire journey is made on Great Western Railway trains - for example, a journey from Exeter St Davids to Marlow (two Great Western Railway services) can be upgraded with one Weekend First (GWR). 

Weekend First (GWR) is only available to purchase on board, and is subject to availability of accommodation.

Please note that Weekend First (GWR) is not valid on trains operated by any other Train Operating Company.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Services which carry First Class accommodation include all High Speed services from London Paddington to the Cotswolds / Hereford, Cheltenham Spa, South Wales, the West Country and the South West of England; most local and semi-fast services from London Paddington to the area bounded by Banbury, Didcot Parkway, Bedwyn, Basingstoke; Reading - Gatwick Airport services; most services on the Thames Branches (Henley-on-Thames, Marlow, Windsor & Eton Central, Greenford).
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Onboard eligible GWR services only. Cannot be purchased online or at stations
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: N/A
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Changeable for a fee
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Refundable for a fee

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Supplement to upgrade a standard ticket to first class.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid for the journey on the date on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: n/a

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Weekend only.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel agents
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No reservations are needed.

Child.Note: No discounts.

RailCard.Note: No discounts.

Group.Note: No discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: A supplement to upgrade from Standard to First Class.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid at any time on Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays. Also valid at off peak times on weekdays from 27 - 31 December.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: This upgrade is sold on-train, only.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Available on-train, from the guard, subject to available space in First Class.

Child.Note: Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: One-way supplement to upgrade a Standard Class ticket to First Class.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on the date on the ticket only.
Validity.DayReturn: n/a

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Break of journey is permitted if ticket allows.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any weekend.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: On train only.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No reservations are needed.

Validity.DayReturn: Valid for the journey on the date on the ticket.

TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No reservations are needed.

RailCard.Note: No discounts.

Group.Note: No discounts.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Upgrade Standard class tickets to First Class.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Pay onboard
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Yes

TicketTypeDescription.Description: First Class Carnet Ticket for 5 return journeys

Validity.DayOutward: Each carnet ticket is valid for travel any day within 3 months of the date on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn: Each carnet ticket is valid for travel any day within 3 months of the date on the ticket.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid on any train
Validity.TimeReturn: Valid on any train

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Generally the same as 'Anytime tickets', except that tickets are valid for three months, but are not refundable

Route and/or Train Company validity is as shown on the ticket routeing.

Can be bought at any time up to departure but cannot be bought on board trains.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Any day
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Available through business sales channels only - currently suspended
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: No
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: No changes
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Non refundable

TicketTypeDescription.Description: This is an Off Peak Day Return ticket for travel on South Western Railway services.

Validity.DayOutward: Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.DayReturn: Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
Validity.TimeOutward: This ticket is valid at any time.
Validity.TimeReturn: This ticket is valid at any time.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: A break of journey is permitted on the outward portion off an Off-Peak Day Return unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.
BreakOfJourney.ReturnNote: A break of journey is permitted on the outward portion off an Off-Peak Day Return unless otherwise indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: • The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket.Off-Peak Day tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned.  In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare. All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: None
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026:

Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date.

You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026:

Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel.

Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies.

In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due.

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets.

An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

Child.Note:
Children (aged 5 to 15 inclusive) are offered a 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 can travel free with each fare paying passenger.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
‘70min Flex’ fares are Single (one-way) tickets offering great value for money on many longer distance journeys.

Customers must book in advance of travel and fares are sold in limited numbers subject to availability.

The further ahead these tickets are purchased, the cheaper the fares will typically be.

Customers can mix and match ‘70min Flex’ fares or combine with other Single fares such as ‘Anytime’ or ‘Advance’ to carry out a return journey.

Customers can travel according to the booked itinerary but are also permitted to travel on alternative services to carry out the same journey within the time period defined by the ticket.

Validity.DayOutward: ‘70min Flex’ fares are valid only on the date shown on the ticket and within a fixed time period defined around the original booked itinerary.
Validity.DayReturn: Not applicable.
Validity.TimeOutward:
Customers can either travel as booked, or draw on the freedom to start the journey up to 70 minutes earlier or later than originally scheduled when they first purchased the ticket.

If travelling on alternative trains, the new scheduled arrival time must be within three hours of the original scheduled arrival time. A customer’s new travel itinerary may be quicker or slower than the one booked.

Customers may not use this product as a rover ticket upon departure from their origin station.
Validity.TimeReturn: Not applicable.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote:
Customers must not break their journey, i.e. they may not start, break/resume, or end their journey at any intermediate station en route. Customers risk being denied entry or exit at those stations or being charged for a new ticket.

Customers are permitted to interchange at intermediate stations to/from valid connecting services.

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
Customers may use the connecting services of other operators including ScotRail, CrossCountry, Northern, TransPennine Express and Tyne & Wear Metro, where applicable. Customers may also use Thameslink, Great Northern or Transport for London services, where applicable.

Tickets are not valid with Lumo, Grand Central, Hull Trains or Avanti West Coast.

Customers must travel in the Class shown on the ticket and should sit in their reserved seat(s) where these have been provided.

If delays occur while travelling and a connecting service is missed as a result, customers will be permitted to travel via the next available service to complete their journey using the same operator(s).
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets are valid only on the date and train service(s) shown on the ticket(s) or alternative services within the time period defined around the original booked itinerary. Tickets are valid only for travel in the class shown.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
‘70min Flex’ fares are available at any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online via most train company and third party websites, and selected ticket vending machines and rail appointed travel agents.

Please note that customers who do not purchase online and fulfil to eTicket will not be able to digitally amend their booking. These customers may be able to reserve a seat on alternative services via the LNER seat reservation tool or by otherwise getting in touch with LNER, subject to availability.

These fares are not available on-board either for current or for future travel.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased in advance of travel and are subject to availability. Tickets are generally available up to 12 weeks before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
Customers have the ability to change the date or time of travel, beyond the level of flexibility provided by the ticket, but this must be prior to expiry of the booked departure validity window. After this, the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. The new journey must include the same origin and destination stations, as well as the same route and operator conditions. Customers will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when they request a change.

Any difference in fare plus an administration fee will be payable prior to travel, though there may be times where the administration fee does not apply. If the new travel itinerary has a cheaper fare available, the difference will not be refunded.

Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket valid for that service, a new ticket or excess fare must be purchased.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
‘70min Flex’ fares are non-refundable.

However, if booked services are cancelled or retimed and as a result the customer decides not to travel, a refund may be offered on completely unused tickets and the customer would not be charged any administration fee.

Where an excess fare has been paid up to the price of a flexible ticket, the excess fare paid varies the terms under which the passenger can travel, but it does not alter the refund terms of the original ticket.

Child.Note:
Children aged 5 to 15 inclusive: 50% discount.

Up to two children aged under 5 may travel free of charge with each fare-paying adult.

RailCard.Note:
Discounts are available with the following Railcards:

  • 16-25, 26-30,
  • Senior,
  • HM Forces, Veterans,
  • Disabled Persons,
  • Two Together (Customers cannot depart before 09:30 Mon-Fri, even with a 70min Flex ticket originally booked to depart before 10:40).

Discounts are available in Standard Class only with the following Railcards:

  • 16-17 Saver,
  • Family & Friends.

Limited discounts are also available in Standard Class with the Job Centre Plus Railcard.

Group.Note:
Generally, group discounts are not available.

However, the LNER Small Group discount is available with these fares.

TicketTypeDescription.SpecialConditions:
Should a customer wish to make use of the flexibility available, they are advised to contact the retailer to amend the booking and obtain seat reservations for the new travel itinerary. At present, only tickets purchased online via the LNER website and App can be digitally amended.

Customers are not required to amend their booking to make use of the flexibility included in the ticket. However, the customer cannot be guaranteed a seat on any alternative services if they do not formally amend their booking.

Customers who have booked through retailers other than LNER may use the LNER seat reservation tool to reserve a seat on any valid alternative services.

No additional fare is due where the customer amends their booking and plans their journey within the defined time period surrounding the original booked itinerary. No fees are payable to make or amend any seat reservations.

There is no limitation on the number of times a customer can amend their booking for travel within the defined period of flexibility provided by the original ticket.

Customers who exercise this flexibility at short notice are advised to reserve a seat online for their new services where possible, subject to availability.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Caledonian Sleeper Group Travel
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: This tickets can only be purchased through the Caledonian Sleeper Group travel team.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Limited changes and refunds.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: Details available from the Group Travel team.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Online Sleeper Flexipass

Validity.DayOutward: Reserved train only
Validity.TimeOutward: Reserved train only

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Flexipass is only valid on Caledonian Sleeper and not on any other operator.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: The ticket can be purchased up to one month in advance of the first booking. It is not possible to make reservations for travel until the product becomes valid.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: This product can only be purchased and managed through the Caledonian Sleeper website www.sleeper.scot. In order to purchase this product you must register for an online account.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: This product can only be used with confirmed reservations. Tickets are fulfilled by Self-Print or e-ticket only and this will only be generated once a confirmed booking as been made. Reservations are possible until the train closed for reservations. This product cannot be used if it is not possible to obtain reservations for example if the reservations are closed or the train is fully booked.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Change of travel plans are free of change. All changes must be made through an online account and must be made before midday two days before arrival in destination.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: There are no refunds on Flexipass, however if a new Flexipass is purchased before the old one expires the remaining coupons are extended the match the expiry date of the new pass.

TicketTypeDescription.Description: Sleeper supplements are available to Guests who already hold travel tickets and wish to travel in sleeper accommodation.

Validity.DayOutward: Reserved train only
Validity.TimeOutward: Reserved train only

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
A Room Supplements are not a travel ticket. They are an accommodation supplement and work in conjunction with a travel ticket. The class of travel is determined by the travel ticket held. Guest who hold Standard travel tickets can only purchase a Classic Room Supplement, holders of First Class Tickets can purchase either a Club or Classic Room Supplement. Guests who hold Standard Tickets and wish to travel in a Club room will be required to upgrade their travel ticket from Standard to First Class.

Travel tickets or combinations of travel tickets that can be used with a berth supplement must cover the whole journey being made. Examples of tickets that can be used are Anytime, Off-Peak, Super Off-Peak, Britrail passes or All Line Rovers. Advance Purchase tickets valid on other operators cannot be used as a valid travel ticket. Rover Tickets, Britrail, Inter-Rail, EuroRail, FIP Coupons tickets need to be valid for travel on arrival in destination. They do not necessarily need to be valid when boarding the train, if the departure is before midnight.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Room Supplements can only be purchased in advance while reservations are available.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: At stations or online.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Reservations must be made in advance of travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans:
For tickets purchased before 07 June 2023

For travel to be changed the Room Supplement will need to be refunded and a new one purchased. A £10 admin applies to the refund.

For tickets purchased on or after 07 June 2023
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
For tickets purchased before 07 June 2023

Full refunds are available minus a £10 admin fee is available up to midday the day before travel. For after midnight departures this is midday 2 days before travel.

For tickets purchased on or after 07 June 2023

Refunds are available on a sliding scale depending on when the refund is requested.

  • 7 days or more before travel - a full refund is available with no admin fee
  • Less than 7 days and before midday the day before travel (2 days before for departures between 00:01 and 04:30) a 50% refund is available
  • After midday the day before departure (2 days before for departures between 00:01 and 04:30) refunds are no longer available

TicketTypeDescription.Description: These ticket types should be used for making reservations for Scotrail staff and Gold Status pass holders. SUS should be used for Standard Class facility holders, FUS should be used for First Class facility holders.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability: These are available in limited numbers.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Only available through stations.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: Only available for purchase 12 weeks before travel.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: Reservations can be cancelled at any time.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: These tickets have no value and used for reservation only and only work in conjunction with a staff travel pass.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
Supplement for travelling with up to two pets in Sleeper accommodation and for the thorough cleaning of the compartment after travel so that other guests who may be allergic to pets are not affected.

Pets can only be taken in rooms which is exclusively occupied by the same group. The cleaning charge is per room, not per pet.

Validity.DayOutward: Valid on the booked Caledonian Sleeper service only. Travel must be made on the date shown on the ticket.
Validity.DayReturn:
Not applicable.

For a return journey, buy a single ticket in each direction.
Validity.TimeOutward: Valid on the booked Caledonian Sleeper service only. Travel must be made on the service shown on the ticket.
Validity.TimeReturn:
Not applicable.

For a return journey, buy a single ticket in each direction.

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: Not applicable.

TicketTypeDescription.Availability: Tickets can be booked up to 12 months in advance. Also available up until just before train departure.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing:
Tickets can be booked direct with Caledonian Sleepers at www.sleeper.scot or by calling 0330 060 0500.

Tickets can also be purchased at most major National Rail stations.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines:
Tickets must be bought in advance.

Reservations are compulsory on Sleeper services.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: There is no charge to change the date of the cleaning fee but it must show the correct date of the journey.
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds:
Refunds are available on a sliding scale depending on when the refund is requested.

  • 7 days or more before travel - a full refund is available with no admin fee
  • Less than 7 days and before midday the day before travel (2 days before for departures between 00:01 and 04:30) a 50% refund is available
  • After midday the day before departure (2 days before for departures between 00:01 and 04:30) refunds are no longer available

TicketTypeDescription.Description: GWR Surfboard
TicketTypeDescription.Conditions: Surfboards may be carried on some Great Western Railway services, but only in the luggage van on services on flows to Newquay, St. Ives, Penzance and Barnstaple. Charge is £5.00 per single journey using ticket type SFB. Available accommodation for surfboards is strictly limited.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: Available on board GWR services at time of travel.

TicketTypeDescription.Description:
This pilot product will operate in a similar way to the long-standing Weekend Upgrade. The details of the pilot are as follows:

  • The Upgrade will be available on-board only; it cannot be purchased before boarding.
  • The Weekday Upgrade is intended to fill un-used First Class seats. The decision to make Weekday Upgrades available is at the Conductor’s discretion and should consider First Class seat reservations from future calling points (visible on the seat reservation screens) and the need to retain at least some seating for unreserved full fare First Class customers.
  • The Upgrade is only valid for a one-way journey on the service on which it is purchased.
  • Conductors may advertise the availability of Upgrades via passenger announcements.
  • The pilot will operate only on Liverpool < > Newcastle services. Services operating other routes (e.g. Redcar < > Manchester Airport or Scarborough < > Manchester Victoria) are not included.
  • The same conductor retail commission terms as Weekend First will apply.
  • The Weekday Upgrade may only be sold after 09:00 Monday-Friday.
  • The customer must retain their original ticket to exit at any gatelines.
  • The Upgrade may only be sold by the Conductor, unless the Conductor has advertised availability of the Upgrade via passenger announcements and given permission for Revenue Protection colleagues to sell the Upgrade. Otherwise, Revenue Protection colleagues should sell an excess fare or refer the customer to the conductor.

Pricing of the Weekday Upgrade will depend on the length of the journey. Selected prices for popular journeys are provided below:

During the pilot we will monitor uptake of the Weekday Upgrade and adjust stocks of catering supplies as required. We will undertake a review of the initial outcomes of the pilot in Spring 2026. If successful we will consider rolling out this product to other routes.

Validity.DayOutward: The Upgrade is only valid for a one-way journey on the service on which it is purchased.
Validity.DayReturn: N/A
Validity.TimeOutward: The Weekday Upgrade may only be sold after 09:00 Monday-Friday.
Validity.TimeReturn: N/A

BreakOfJourney.OutwardNote: N/A

TicketTypeDescription.Conditions:
The Weekday Upgrade is intended to fill un-used First Class seats. The decision to make Weekday Upgrades available is at the Conductor’s discretion and should consider First Class seat reservations from future calling points (visible on the seat reservation screens) and the need to retain at least some seating for unreserved full fare First Class customers.

The customer must retain their original ticket to exit at any gatelines.

The Upgrade may only be sold by the Conductor, unless the Conductor has advertised availability of the Upgrade via passenger announcements and given permission for Revenue Protection colleagues to sell the Upgrade. Otherwise, Revenue Protection colleagues should sell an excess fare or refer the customer to the conductor.
TicketTypeDescription.Availability:
The Upgrade will be available on-board only; it cannot be purchased before boarding.

The Weekday Upgrade is intended to fill un-used First Class seats. The decision to make Weekday Upgrades available is at the Conductor’s discretion and should consider First Class seat reservations from future calling points (visible on the seat reservation screens) and the need to retain at least some seating for unreserved full fare First Class customers.
TicketTypeDescription.Retailing: The Upgrade will be available on-board only; it cannot be purchased before boarding.
TicketTypeDescription.BookingDeadlines: You can not pre-book.
TicketTypeDescription.ChangesToTravelPlans: N/A
TicketTypeDescription.Refunds: N/A

Child.Note: N/A

RailCard.Note: N/A

Group.Note: N/A

TicketTypeDescription.SpecialConditions:
Issuing method; For TTK ONLY

How to retail the First Class Weekday Upgrade

On the ticket search menu, enter origin and destination.

Enter number of adults (if any children these should also be entered as adults. E.g. 1 adult 1 child should be entered as 2 adults).

Press Offline.

Scroll to the WDU product TPE Weekday Upgrade and tap the WDU product.

Press Pay and select the method of payment.

Note: The ticket can only be issued onto PRT (paper roll ticket).
